mcqData.text: Who is the father of Human Resource Management?
mcqData.explanation: Robert Owen is widely recognized as the father of Human Resource Management for his early efforts to improve working conditions.

mcqData.text:
Middle-level managers are-

(i) Managing Director
(ii) Departmental Manager
(iii) Regional Manager
mcqData.explanation: Departmental and regional managers operate at the middle management level. Managing Directors are top-level.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Titas & Co. set a target of 10,000 units. Production department failed to notify the purchase department in time, so raw materials weren't bought. Target failed.] Which management function was not completed properly?
mcqData.explanation: Coordination is the function that aligns the activities of different departments (production and purchase) to achieve goals.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] What should be done to achieve production targets?

(i) Increase communication
(ii) Maintain unity among departments
(iii) Increase equipment capacity
mcqData.explanation: Improving communication and maintaining unity (coordination) are key to fixing the functional gap identified in the stem.

mcqData.text: What is the next step of Control in the management cycle?
mcqData.explanation: In the continuous management cycle, the control process leads to the identification of gaps, which are then addressed in the next cycle's Planning.

mcqData.text: What is the first function of management?
mcqData.explanation: Planning is the foundational and primary function upon which all other management activities are built.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Rahim is Managing Director. Mr. Jasim is an accountant handling ledger and stock register. Mr. Rahim handles policy decisions.] Mr. Rahim works at which level?
mcqData.explanation: Managing Directors are responsible for policy formulation, which is the primary duty of Top-level management.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] Regarding Mr. Jasim:

(i) Needs more technical skills
(ii) Coordinates between departments
(iii) Positioned at lower management level
mcqData.explanation: Accountants at this level perform operational tasks (lower level) and require high technical skills, not departmental coordination.

mcqData.text: Which work relates to assigning the right person to the right duty?
mcqData.explanation: Staffing is the management function specifically responsible for acquiring and placing the right talent in the right roles.

mcqData.text: Formulating policies and plans is the responsibility of which management level?
mcqData.explanation: Policy formulation and long-term strategic planning are the exclusive domains of Top-level management.

mcqData.text: Into how many parts did Henri Fayol divide management functions?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol identified five key functions of management: Planning, Organizing, Commanding, Coordinating, and Controlling.

mcqData.text: What is the first step of the management cycle?
mcqData.explanation: Planning serves as the starting point for every management process or cycle.

mcqData.text: Where did the Industrial Revolution first start?
mcqData.explanation: The Industrial Revolution originated in Britain in the late 18th century.

mcqData.text: Which level of management directly supervises the work of employees?
mcqData.explanation: Lower-level management (supervisory) is directly responsible for monitoring the day-to-day work of the operational staff.

mcqData.text: Who said "Management is universal"?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol is famous for the management principle that management principles are universal and applicable to all organizations.

mcqData.text: How many levels of management are there?
mcqData.explanation: The standard academic model identifies three levels: Top, Middle, and Lower (Supervisory) management.

mcqData.text: What is the next step after Control in the management cycle?
mcqData.explanation: Once control identifies performance gaps, the process resets by creating a new or modified plan for the next cycle.

mcqData.text: Through which function can unity be established?
mcqData.explanation: Coordination is often defined as the "essence of management" because it synchronizes diverse activities to ensure organizational unity.

mcqData.text: Who is a middle-level manager?
mcqData.explanation: Departmental Heads act as a link between top-level strategy and lower-level execution, characterizing middle management.

mcqData.text: What is the main universal purpose of management?
mcqData.explanation: The ultimate universal purpose of any management system is the effective and efficient achievement of the organization's goals.

mcqData.text: What is it called to get work done through others?
mcqData.explanation: Management is fundamentally defined as the art of getting things done through and with others.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mrs. Mahmuda Sultana started a tailoring shop with 10 tailors. Due to lack of specific work allocation, conflicts arose. She then specified duties and responsibilities for each.] Her action is related to which management function?
mcqData.explanation: Organizing is the function of arranging tasks, duties, and responsibilities among staff to achieve goals.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] The reason for the cessation of conflict is-

(i) Accountability of each will be ensured
(ii) Each will understand their work
(iii) Tendency to avoid responsibility will decrease
mcqData.explanation: Proper organization clarifies roles, leading to higher accountability, clear work understanding, and reduced responsibility avoidance.

mcqData.text: What do the 6 'M's of management refer to?
mcqData.explanation: The 6 Ms (Man, Money, Materials, Machines, Methods, Markets) are the essential resources or components of management.

mcqData.text:
Top-level management includes-

(i) Managing Director
(ii) Departmental Manager
(iii) Board of Directors
mcqData.explanation: The Board of Directors and Managing Director constitute the Top-level management responsible for strategic policy.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Arif suddenly opened a new medicine shop. He is facing various problems like lack of experienced sales staff, low number of customers, etc.] Due to the lack of which management function is he facing problems?
mcqData.explanation: Before starting a new venture, failing to perform 'Planning' (forecasting, budgeting, manpower analysis) leads to these operational problems.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] To do the work properly, Mr. Arif should have-

(i) Collected necessary information
(ii) Set a proper budget
(iii) Efficiently forecasted
mcqData.explanation: Proper planning involves gathering information, budgeting, and forecasting to anticipate and mitigate startup risks.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Shamsher Ali fixes the goal and purpose of the organization by thinking about the future. He directs the departmental managers to formulate necessary plans to achieve the goal.] Departmental managers work at which management level?
mcqData.explanation: Departmental Managers act as the link between strategy and operation, placing them firmly in the Middle-level management.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] For Mr. Shamsher Ali, which is applicable?

(i) He is at the top level of management
(ii) He needs more conceptual skills
(iii) He formulates policies and takes decisions
mcqData.explanation: As a top-level manager, he is responsible for strategic decisions and policy formulation, which requires high conceptual skills.

mcqData.text: What is the most important element of management?
mcqData.explanation: Among the 6 Ms, 'Man' (human resources) is considered the most important because humans direct all other non-human resources.

mcqData.text: Into how many parts did Henri Fayol divide management functions?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol categorized management functions into five elements: Planning, Organizing, Commanding, Coordinating, and Controlling.

mcqData.text: What is the first function of the management cycle?
mcqData.explanation: Planning is the primary function and the start of the management cycle.

mcqData.text: Where did the Industrial Revolution first start?
mcqData.explanation: The Industrial Revolution first began in Britain in the 18th century.

mcqData.text: Which management level directly supervises the employees' work?
mcqData.explanation: Lower-level management (Supervisory level) is directly responsible for overseeing the daily work of employees.

mcqData.text: Who said "Management is universal"?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol is credited with the concept that management principles are universal.

mcqData.text: How many levels of management are there?
mcqData.explanation: The classic organizational structure is divided into Top, Middle, and Lower levels.

mcqData.text: What is the next step after Control in the management cycle?
mcqData.explanation: Once the control function identifies deviations or results, the cycle begins anew with revised 'Planning'.

mcqData.text: Through which function can unity be established?
mcqData.explanation: Coordination is specifically designed to create unity, harmony, and synchronization among different departments.

mcqData.text: Who is a middle-level manager?
mcqData.explanation: Departmental Heads are the classic examples of middle-level managers.

mcqData.text: What is the main universal objective of management?
mcqData.explanation: The overarching objective of all management activities is the successful attainment of the organization's set goals.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Rinku is a sales manager. He sets goals for sales staff for a year and informs them. After six months, he reviews the progress and finds the pace unsatisfactory.] What kind of function is Rinku's first task?
mcqData.explanation: Setting goals and targets for a future period is the core of the 'Planning' function.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] What should Rinku do to solve the problem?

(i) Discuss with sales staff
(ii) Train weak staff
(iii) Provide regular direction and supervision
mcqData.explanation: To improve performance, a manager must communicate, train employees, and provide ongoing supervision.

mcqData.text: Which skill is most important at the lower level of management?
mcqData.explanation: Lower-level managers are task-oriented and directly supervise work, requiring high 'Technical' skills.

mcqData.text: The Code of Hammurabi was composed during which civilization?
mcqData.explanation: The Code of Hammurabi is a famous legal document from the ancient Babylonian civilization.

mcqData.text: Determining responsibility and accountability is related to which management function?
mcqData.explanation: Organizing involves defining roles, responsibilities, and chains of accountability within an organization.

mcqData.text:
Who contributed to the development of management in the Middle Ages?

(i) Luca Pacioli
(ii) Thomas More
(iii) James Watt
mcqData.explanation: Luca Pacioli (double-entry bookkeeping) and Thomas More (writings on administration) are key contributors from that era.

mcqData.text: Which is the first function of management?
mcqData.explanation: Planning is recognized as the foundational function that precedes all others.

mcqData.text: Who is the father of personnel (labor) management?
mcqData.explanation: Robert Owen is considered the father of personnel management due to his early focus on worker welfare.

mcqData.text: A supervisor-level manager is-
mcqData.explanation: A foreman is the traditional title for a supervisor-level (lower-level) manager in a factory setting.

mcqData.text: Which is NOT a characteristic of good direction?
mcqData.explanation: While management itself needs flexibility, direction/orders must be clear and firm; however, in many contexts, "Flexibility" is considered a trait of leadership, but "Clarity/Logicality" are more essential for 'Direction' (instruction). Note: Among options, Flexibility is often cited as a quality of planning/management rather than a rigid direction.

mcqData.text: Which is the first function of management?
mcqData.explanation: Planning is universally taught as the starting point of the management process.

mcqData.text: Authority is centralized at which level?
mcqData.explanation: Strategic authority and centralized decision-making reside at the Top management level.

mcqData.text:
Resources of an organization include-

(i) Man
(ii) Money
(iii) Method
mcqData.explanation: The 6 M's of management include Men, Money, Machines, Materials, Methods, and Markets.

mcqData.text:
Lower-level management includes-

(i) Foreman
(ii) Departmental Head
(iii) Office Supervisor
mcqData.explanation: Foreman and Office Supervisor operate at the operational/lower level. Departmental heads are Middle-level.

mcqData.text: Which component is NOT part of the management 6'M's?
mcqData.explanation: Decision making is a process/skill, whereas the 6 M's represent organizational resources.

mcqData.text: Who said "Management is universal"?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol is famous for establishing the universality of management principles.

mcqData.text: What is the final function of management?
mcqData.explanation: Control is the final step in the management cycle, evaluating performance against the plan.

mcqData.text: Through which management function are relationships between different individuals in an organization determined?
mcqData.explanation: Organizing defines the structure and relationships (authority/responsibility) between individuals.

mcqData.text: What is the basis for other management functions?
mcqData.explanation: Planning acts as the foundation upon which Organizing, Staffing, Directing, and Controlling are built.

mcqData.text: What is it called to link the work of various departments and sub-departments of an organization?
mcqData.explanation: Coordination ensures that the various units work in harmony and unity toward common goals.

mcqData.text: From which foreign language did the word 'Management' originate?
mcqData.explanation: The word 'Management' is derived from the Italian word 'Maneggiare', meaning 'to handle'.

mcqData.text: How many levels of management are there?
mcqData.explanation: The standard academic classification of management levels consists of three: Top, Middle, and Lower.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Rahim is a floor supervisor in a garment factory. His subordinates make clothes according to orders. He is trying but failing to complete production on time.] At which level is Mr. Rahim working?
mcqData.explanation: A floor supervisor is a direct overseer of operations, which is the definition of Lower-level management.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] Strategy for Mr. Rahim to increase production:

(i) Set production target per worker
(ii) Recommend promotion for best salesperson
(iii) Assist weak salespeople
mcqData.explanation: Setting targets and assisting weak workers directly impacts production. Promotion of sales staff is irrelevant to production floor management.

mcqData.text: What does the management cycle indicate the rotation of?
mcqData.explanation: The management cycle refers to the continuous rotation and repetition of the functional steps (Planning, Organizing, etc.).

mcqData.text: Which element of the organization acts as the driving force for other elements?
mcqData.explanation: Human resources (Man) are the active agents that utilize and direct all other non-human/passive resources.

mcqData.text: Top-level management includes-
mcqData.explanation: The Board of Directors constitutes the highest authority responsible for setting the strategic direction.

mcqData.text: Which management function acts as a bridge between the present and the future?
mcqData.explanation: Planning involves forecasting future needs and deciding current actions, bridging the gap between today and tomorrow.

mcqData.text: Which function helps to increase the motivation/spirit of employees?
mcqData.explanation: Motivation is the specific psychological process used to enhance the morale and drive of workers.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Mahin is involved in strategic planning, policy formulation, and decision-making for a cement company.] Mr. Mahin is an executive of which level?
mcqData.explanation: Strategic planning and policy-making are the core responsibilities of Top-level executives.

mcqData.text: Which management function is considered the foundation for other functions?
mcqData.explanation: Planning defines the path for all subsequent management activities.

mcqData.text: Who said "Management is universal"?
mcqData.explanation: Aristotle (and later Henri Fayol) is associated with the idea of universal applicability of administration/management.

mcqData.text: Which level of managers directly supervise the work of employees?
mcqData.explanation: Lower-level managers (supervisors) are in direct contact with operational staff.

mcqData.text: Who is the father of modern personnel management?
mcqData.explanation: Robert Owen is widely acknowledged as the father of modern personnel management for his reforms.

mcqData.text: Which function is necessary to resolve conflicts among organizational members?
mcqData.explanation: Leadership is the ability to influence and guide people, which is crucial for resolving interpersonal conflicts.

mcqData.text: How many levels of management are there?
mcqData.explanation: Three levels: Top, Middle, and Lower.

mcqData.text: What is the final step in the management process?
mcqData.explanation: Control is the final phase where performance is measured against standards.

mcqData.text: Who provided the concept of "Management Universality"?
mcqData.explanation: Louis A. Allen is a management theorist known for articulating and categorizing management principles.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Alam is a manager. He plans to implement company goals, policies, and strategies. He organizes resources and manages control functions. Due to his skills, the company succeeded.] Mr. Alam is at which management level?
mcqData.explanation: Middle-level managers are primarily responsible for implementing the goals, policies, and strategies set by top management.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] Mr. Alam's role in success includes-

(i) Experience
(ii) Communication skills
(iii) Power to inspire
mcqData.explanation: A successful manager requires experience, effective communication to coordinate, and the ability to motivate subordinates.

mcqData.text:
Management is a-

(i) Continuous process
(ii) Goal-oriented process
(iii) Dynamic process
mcqData.explanation: Management is an ongoing, goal-directed, and dynamic (adaptable) process.

mcqData.text: Development of management is related to which type of education?
mcqData.explanation: Modern management requires a broad set of skills (conceptual, human, and technical) rather than just single-task proficiency.

mcqData.text: Time study is mainly used to increase-
mcqData.explanation: Time study, pioneered by Taylor, is primarily used to measure and improve operational efficiency and productivity.

mcqData.text: Which function is important to ensure accountability?
mcqData.explanation: The control function measures performance and compares it against standards to establish accountability.

mcqData.text: Which is a basic element (resource) of management?
mcqData.explanation: Man' (Human Resource) is one of the 6 M's, which are the fundamental elements/resources of management.

mcqData.text: How do management functions flow?
mcqData.explanation: Management is a process where functions repeat in a continuous loop, known as the management cycle.

mcqData.text: Growth and improvement of an organization depend on what?
mcqData.explanation: Effective planning is the blueprint that dictates the growth and strategic direction of an organization.

mcqData.text: Which management level is responsible for formulating organizational policies and plans?
mcqData.explanation: Policy formulation and long-term planning are the primary functions of Top-level management.

mcqData.text: How many levels of management are there?
mcqData.explanation: There are three standard levels: Top, Middle, and Lower.

mcqData.text: To which part of the human body can management be compared?
mcqData.explanation: Management acts as the 'brain' of the organization because it coordinates, directs, and makes all strategic decisions.

mcqData.text: Determining responsibility and accountability is related to which management function?
mcqData.explanation: Organizing defines roles, responsibilities, and the chain of accountability.

mcqData.text: Who is a middle-level manager in an organization?
mcqData.explanation: Marketing Managers, Departmental Heads, etc., represent the middle-management layer.

mcqData.text: Which is NOT included in the core functions of management?
mcqData.explanation: While communication is essential, the core functions are Planning, Organizing, Staffing, Directing, Coordinating, and Controlling.

mcqData.text: Who is the father of Human Resource Management?
mcqData.explanation: Robert Owen is historically credited with the foundations of human resource management.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Samad decided to hire 50 workers. As production targets were not met, he investigated the cause and took action.] Mr. Samad is at which management level?
mcqData.explanation: A manager responsible for hiring and managing production targets for a division is typically a middle-level manager.

mcqData.text: Taking action to achieve production targets is part of which management function?
mcqData.explanation: The process of finding causes for deviations (not meeting targets) and taking corrective action is the definition of Control.

mcqData.text: Which is the next function of directing in the management cycle?
mcqData.explanation: In the standard sequence (Planning -> Organizing -> Staffing -> Directing -> Coordinating -> Controlling), Coordination follows Directing.

mcqData.text:
The 6 'M's include-

(i) Method
(ii) Material
(iii) Motivation
mcqData.explanation: The 6 M's are: Man, Money, Machines, Materials, Methods, and Markets. 'Motivation' is not a resource/element.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: A college principal noticed a teacher not using materials. He directed the teacher to use them, and the teacher complied.] Regarding Henri Fayol:

(i) Principles of management
(ii) Istanbul city
(iii) An engineer
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol formulated principles of management, was a French mining engineer, and worked in Turkey (Istanbul) for his career.

mcqData.text: Whose quote is 'Management is universal'?
mcqData.explanation: Management is universal' is a famous statement attributed to Socrates, highlighting that management principles are applicable in all fields.

mcqData.text:
Tasks included in the work of a middle-level manager are-

(i) Directing subordinate staff
(ii) Implementing plans
(iii) Formulating plans and policies
mcqData.explanation: Middle-level managers direct subordinates and implement plans set by top management; policy formulation is a top-level task.

mcqData.text: Which is a universal objective of management?
mcqData.explanation: The universal objective of management is the efficient and effective utilization of organizational resources.

mcqData.text:
Included in 6'M' are-

(i) Money
(ii) Method
(iii) Motivation
mcqData.explanation: The 6 M's are Man, Money, Machines, Materials, Methods, and Markets. Motivation is not a resource/element.

mcqData.text: How many levels of management are there?
mcqData.explanation: Management is classically divided into three levels: Top, Middle, and Lower (Supervisory).

mcqData.text:
Tasks of top-level management are-

(i) Supervising workers

mcqData.explanation: Top-level management focuses on strategic goals and policy setting, while supervision is a lower-level task.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is correct?
options: Father of Modern Management: F. W. Taylor
Father of Scientific Management: Henri Fayol
Father of MBO: P. F. Drucker
mcqData.explanation: Peter F. Drucker is known as the father of Management by Objectives (MBO). Others are incorrectly matched.

Standards of a profession are-

mcqData.explanation: A true profession requires formal recognition, specialized practical knowledge, and a set ethical code of conduct.

mcqData.explanation: Strategic planning, long-term goal setting, and policy-making are the core responsibilities of top-level management.

mcqData.text: Who is the proponent of management universality?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol is the primary proponent of the principle that management is a universal activity.

mcqData.text: What is the most acceptable sequence in the management process?
mcqData.explanation: The standard, widely accepted management function sequence begins with Planning and ends with Controlling.

mcqData.text: Who is the father of modern personnel management?
mcqData.explanation: Robert Owen is historically regarded as the father of personnel management for his focus on employee welfare.

mcqData.text: Management is always-
mcqData.explanation: Management exists solely to achieve specific organizational goals; it is inherently goal-oriented.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Anis is a manager. He plans to implement company goals, policies, and strategies. He organizes resources and manages control functions.] Mr. Anis is at which management level?
mcqData.explanation: Middle-level managers act as the bridge by implementing the policies and strategies formulated by top management.

Mr. Anis's role in success includes-

(i) Experience
(ii) Communication skills
(iii) Ability to inspire
mcqData.explanation: A successful manager requires experience to guide, communication to coordinate, and leadership to inspire.

mcqData.explanation: The lower-level (supervisory) management is in direct contact with operational workers.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Tarif is a personnel manager who does mental and physical work. In the same company, Mr. Jamal leads a team of 40 workers.] Mr. Tarif is at which level?
mcqData.explanation: A personnel (HR) manager is typically positioned at the middle-level, bridging top policy and operational needs.

mcqData.text:
Regarding Mr. Jamal in the stem:

(i) Sets policy

(iii) Does more physical labor
mcqData.explanation: As a team leader/supervisor, he implements directives and engages in closer, more practical work (closer to physical labor).

mcqData.text: Who is the father of Human Resource Management?
mcqData.explanation: Robert Owen is credited as the foundational father of Human Resource Management.

mcqData.text: [Stem: In Partex Group, A=Foreman, B=Sales Manager, C=Director.] B is at which level?
mcqData.explanation: A Sales Manager is a classic middle-level management position.

mcqData.text: Paying government taxes on time is a management's-

shortCQData.answer: The management cycle is the continuous process of performing management functions (Planning to Controlling) in a logical sequence.
Management is a "quasi-profession"; it requires specialized knowledge and code of conduct, but lacks mandatory licensing like medicine.
Management is the process of planning, organizing, leading, and controlling resources to achieve organizational goals effectively and efficiently.
It means management principles are applicable in all types of organizations (social, political, or economic) and at all levels of life.

shortCQData.text: (a) Who is the father of modern management?
shortCQData.answer: Henri Fayol is considered the father of modern management.
It is a profession because it requires a specialized body of knowledge, formal training, and intellectual application.

shortCQData.text: (a) What are the levels of management?
shortCQData.answer: Management levels refer to the division of authority and responsibility within an organization, typically categorized into top, mid, and lower levels.

shortCQData.text: (b) Why is control important in management? Explain.
shortCQData.answer: Control is vital because it measures actual performance against standards, identifies deviations, and allows for corrective actions to achieve goals.

shortCQData.text: (a) Who is the father of modern management?
shortCQData.answer: Henri Fayol is the father of modern management.
Management is a profession because it requires a specialized body of knowledge, formal training, and a code of ethics.
Management is the process of planning, organizing, staffing, directing, and controlling to achieve goals efficiently.

shortCQData.text: (b) Why is management called universal? Explain.
shortCQData.answer: It is universal because its principles are required in every collective effort, whether it is a family, a social club, or a state.
Management is the process of getting things done through and with people to achieve organizational goals effectively.

shortCQData.text: (b) What is meant by the management cycle?
shortCQData.answer: The management cycle is the continuous and sequential process of performing functions like planning, organizing, staffing, directing, and controlling.

shortCQData.text: (a) Who is the father of scientific management?
shortCQData.answer: F.W. Taylor is the father of scientific management.

shortCQData.text: (b) What is meant by consultative direction?
shortCQData.answer: Consultative direction is a leadership style where the manager discusses and seeks opinions from subordinates before making a final decision.
Motivation is the process of inspiring and encouraging employees to work with interest and dedication to achieve organizational goals.
Administration sets the core policies and goals (thinking/deciding), while management executes them (doing), making administration the guiding "brain."
Division of labor is the process of breaking down a complex task into smaller, specialized parts and assigning them to individuals based on their skills.
Management is an economic resource because it coordinates the other factors of production (land, labor, capital) to achieve maximum output with minimum waste.
The Industrial Revolution (1760–1840) was the transition to new manufacturing processes using machines, which gave birth to modern management.

shortCQData.text: (b) Explain the concept of Universality of Management.
shortCQData.answer: Universality means management principles are applicable to all collective efforts, including families, social clubs, businesses, and governments.
A profession is a specialized occupation requiring advanced education, formal training, and adherence to a specific code of ethics.

The management cycle is the continuous, circular process of Planning $\rightarrow$ Organizing $\rightarrow$ Staffing $\rightarrow$ Directing $\rightarrow$ Motivating $\rightarrow$ Coordinating $\rightarrow$ Controlling.
The management cycle is the continuous, sequential process of functions (Planning to Controlling) performed to achieve goals.
It means management principles are applicable to all collective human efforts, regardless of the nature or size of the organization.

shortCQData.text: (a) What are the levels of management?
shortCQData.answer: Management levels refer to the hierarchy of authority and responsibility (Top, Mid, Lower) within an organization.
It is a "quasi-profession" because it requires specialized knowledge and ethics, though it lacks mandatory legal licensing in some areas.
Management is the art of getting things done through people effectively to reach organizational objectives.

shortCQData.text: (b) What is meant by the management cycle? Explain.
shortCQData.answer: It is the circular flow of management functions where the end of one cycle (Control) leads to the start of the next (Planning).

They oversee the workers' daily activities, provide technical guidance, and ensure that the plans set by higher management are implemented at the operational level.

fullCQData.stem: Students of the Management Dept. at 'Khang' College planned a study tour to Sylhet. The Head of Dept. (HOD) decided the schedule and locations in advance. He then distributed tasks among teachers to ensure a successful tour.

questions.label: a
questions.text: (a) What are the levels of management?
questions.answer: Management levels refer to the division of authority and responsibility within an organization, typically categorized into top, mid, and lower levels.

questions.label: b
questions.text: (b) Why is control important in management? Explain.
questions.answer: Control is vital because it measures actual performance against standards, identifies deviations, and allows for corrective actions to achieve goals.

questions.label: c
questions.text: (c) Which management function includes the HOD's actions before going on the tour? Explain.
questions.answer: The actions belong to Planning. Planning involves deciding in advance what to do, when to do it, and who will do it. Since the HOD fixed the destination and schedule beforehand, it is planning.

questions.label: d
questions.text: (d) Analyze the management function that played a vital role later in the success of the tour based on the stem.
questions.answer:
1. The function is Organizing.

2. The HOD distributed specific duties among all teachers to execute the tour.

3. This grouping of activities and assignment of responsibility creates a clear work structure.

4. Without this distribution of tasks, the plan would not have been executed successfully.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Shimul sets policies and coordinates with external organizations. Mr. Pradeep is an accounting manager who achieves goals by getting accounting tasks done through his subordinates.

questions.label: a
questions.text: (a) Who is the father of modern management?
questions.answer: Henri Fayol is the father of modern management.

questions.label: b
questions.text: (b) 'Management is a profession'—Explain.
questions.answer: Management is a profession because it requires a specialized body of knowledge, formal training, and a code of ethics.

questions.label: c
questions.text: (c) Which level officer is Mr. Shimul? Explain.
questions.answer: Mr. Shimul is a Top-level Manager. His roles involve policy-making, defining long-term goals, and representing the firm to outside entities.

questions.label: d
questions.text: (d) Analyze the nature of Mr. Pradeep's skills.
questions.answer:
1. Mr. Pradeep primarily demonstrates Human (Interpersonal) Skills.

2. He also requires Technical Skills as he manages the accounting department specifically.

3. His ability to get work done "through subordinates" highlights his leadership and communication skills.

4. These skills are essential at the middle management level to bridge the gap between policy and action.

questions.label: a
questions.text: (a) What is management?
questions.answer: Management is the process of planning, organizing, staffing, directing, and controlling to achieve goals efficiently.

questions.label: b
questions.text: (b) Why is management called universal? Explain.
questions.answer: It is universal because its principles are required in every collective effort, whether it is a family, a social club, or a state.

questions.label: c
questions.text: (c) Which function is missing at the '?' mark? Explain.
questions.answer:
1. The '?' mark (step 3) represents Staffing (কর্মীসংস্থান).

2. It follows organizing and precedes directing.

3. It involves recruiting, selecting, and training people.

4. Without the right people, the structure remains empty.

questions.label: d
questions.text: (d) "Without 'P', management work is bound to be meaningless and chaotic"—Do you agree? Give logic.
questions.answer:
1. 'P' represents Controlling (নিয়ন্ত্রণ), the final stage.

2. Control ensures that actual work aligns with the initial plan.

3. Without it, errors go undetected, leading to chaos and goal failure.

4. It provides the basis for the next planning cycle.

fullCQData.stem: Md. Russel is a sales officer who works hard to achieve goals. He coordinates with various departments and implements the plans made by the Board of Directors through his subordinates. He believes implementation is more important than just making decisions.

questions.label: a
questions.text: (a) What is management?
questions.answer: Management is the process of getting things done through and with people to achieve organizational goals effectively.

questions.label: b
questions.text: (b) What is meant by the management cycle?
questions.answer: The management cycle is the continuous and sequential process of performing functions like planning, organizing, staffing, directing, and controlling.

questions.label: c
questions.text: (c) Which level of management does Mr. Russel belong to? Explain.
questions.answer:
1. Mr. Russel is a Mid-level Manager.

2. He acts as a bridge between the Board (Top level) and subordinates.

3. He focuses on implementing departmental plans.

4. He coordinates with other departments to ensure goal attainment.

questions.label: d
questions.text: (d) "Not just making decisions, but implementing them is the real task"—Evaluate in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
1. Execution Focus: Russel turns the top management's vision into actionable reality.

2. Departmental Success: Without implementation, the best strategic decisions remain theoretical.

3. Supervision: He ensures subordinates work according to the set standards.

4. Goal Attainment: Real results are only achieved when plans are executed effectively on the field.

questions.label: a
questions.text: (a) Who is the father of scientific management?
questions.answer: F.W. Taylor is the father of scientific management.

questions.label: b
questions.text: (b) What is meant by consultative direction?
questions.answer: Consultative direction is a leadership style where the manager discusses and seeks opinions from subordinates before making a final decision.

questions.label: c
questions.text: (c) What is the function at stage 'F' in the diagram? Explain.
questions.answer:
1. Stage 'F' represents Coordinating (সমন্বয়সাধন).

2. It integrates the activities of different departments.

3. It ensures all members work toward a common goal.

4. It prevents conflict and overlap in tasks.

questions.label: d
questions.text: (d) The work at stage 'A' is very important. Discuss.
questions.answer:
1. 'A' is Planning (পরিকল্পনা), the first step.

2. It sets the foundation for all other functions.

3. It reduces uncertainty and risk.

4. It defines the "What, How, and When" of an organization.

questions.label: a
questions.text: (a) What is motivation?
questions.answer: Motivation is the process of inspiring and encouraging employees to work with interest and dedication to achieve organizational goals.

questions.label: b
questions.text: (b) "Administration is the brain of management"—Explain.
questions.answer: Administration sets the core policies and goals (thinking/deciding), while management executes them (doing), making administration the guiding "brain."

questions.label: c
questions.text: (c) Which management function is performed at stage 'ক'? Explain.
questions.answer:
1. Stage 'ক' represents Organizing (সংগঠন).

2. It involves grouping tasks and assigning authority.

3. It establishes relationships between workers and resources.

4. It creates the organizational structure.

questions.label: d
questions.text: (d) "Absence of the function at 'খ' will create chaos in the organization"—Do you agree? Analyze.
questions.answer:
1. 'খ' represents Coordinating (সমন্বয়সাধন).

2. Yes, I agree; it integrates departmental efforts.

3. Without it, individuals work in isolation, causing conflict.

4. It ensures all "moving parts" work toward one goal.

fullCQData.stem: Surma Cement' is a large factory in Munshiganj. Mr. Rafiq, a director, sets goals and policies. Mr. Kabir arranges the field-level implementation of those plans.

questions.label: a
questions.text: (a) What is division of labor?
questions.answer: Division of labor is the process of breaking down a complex task into smaller, specialized parts and assigning them to individuals based on their skills.

questions.label: b
questions.text: (b) 'Management is an economic resource'—Explain.
questions.answer: Management is an economic resource because it coordinates the other factors of production (land, labor, capital) to achieve maximum output with minimum waste.

questions.label: c
questions.text: (c) Which level of manager is Mr. Rafiq? Explain.
questions.answer:
1. Mr. Rafiq is a Top-level Manager.

2. He is a "Director" involved in defining objectives and policy formulation.

3. These functions determine the long-term vision of the company.

4. He provides the overall direction for 'Surma Cement'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: (d) "Mr. Kabir's role is vital for achieving goals"—Evaluate in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
1. Execution: Kabir bridges the gap between Rafiq’s policy and actual results.

2. Field Management: He ensures that strategy becomes action.

3. Supervision: He guides lower-level staff to follow the standards.

4. Success: Goals remain theoretical without Kabir's practical implementation.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. A is the owner of a factory. Mr. B is the Production Manager, and Mr. C is the Marketing Manager. Two supervisors handle constant field-level responsibilities.

questions.label: a
questions.text: (a) What is the Industrial Revolution?
questions.answer: The Industrial Revolution (1760–1840) was the transition to new manufacturing processes using machines, which gave birth to modern management.

questions.label: b
questions.text: (b) Explain the concept of Universality of Management.
questions.answer: Universality means management principles are applicable to all collective efforts, including families, social clubs, businesses, and governments.

questions.label: c
questions.text: (c) At which level of management are the supervisors working? Explain.
questions.answer:
1. Supervisors work at the Lower-level (Operational) Management.

2. They directly oversee the workers' activities.

3. Their focus is on technical execution and daily targets.

4. They act as a link between workers and middle management.

questions.label: d
questions.text: (d) 'A and B achieve institutional goals, but their nature of work is different'—Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
1. Level Difference: A is Top-level (Owner), while B is Mid-level (Manager).

2. Focus: A decides "what" to do; B decides "how" to do it.

3. Scope: A looks at the whole business; B looks at production specifically.

4. Skill: A needs conceptual skills; B needs human and technical skills.

fullCQData.stem: Shahed Chowdhury, a Sales Manager, plans everything but fails to meet targets due to lack of supervision. To fix this, he adopts new managerial decisions for better monitoring.

questions.label: a
questions.text: (a) What is a profession?
questions.answer: A profession is a specialized occupation requiring advanced education, formal training, and adherence to a specific code of ethics.

questions.label: b
questions.text: (b) Explain the management cycle.
questions.answer: The management cycle is the continuous, circular process of Planning $\rightarrow$ Organizing $\rightarrow$ Staffing $\rightarrow$ Directing $\rightarrow$ Motivating $\rightarrow$ Coordinating $\rightarrow$ Controlling.

questions.label: c
questions.text: (c) With which management function did Shahed start the sales activity? Explain.
questions.answer:
1. He started with Planning.

2. The stem mentions he "fixed everything beforehand."

3. Planning is the primary function that sets targets.

4. It provides the roadmap for sales operations.

questions.label: d
questions.text: (d) Evaluate the appropriateness of Shahed’s decision for achieving future targets.
questions.answer:
1. Decision: He chose to implement Controlling.

2. Corrective Action: Control identifies why targets weren't met (lack of supervision).

3. Discipline: It brings order and dynamic movement to the department.

4. Verification: It ensures future performance matches the planned standards.

fullCQData.stem: Rashed Akbar wants maximum output. He shares 5% of profits with workers and takes steps to improve their standard of living. He is highly respected by his staff.

questions.label: a
questions.text: (a) What is the management cycle?
questions.answer: The management cycle is the continuous, sequential process of functions (Planning to Controlling) performed to achieve goals.

questions.label: b
questions.text: (b) 'Management is universal'—Explain.
questions.answer: It means management principles are applicable to all collective human efforts, regardless of the nature or size of the organization.

questions.label: c
questions.text: (c) Which management function has Mr. Rashed performed? Explain.
questions.answer:
1. He performed Motivating (প্রেষণা).

2. He provided financial incentives (5% profit).

3. He focused on their welfare.

4. Motivation inspires workers to give their best effort.

questions.label: d
questions.text: (d) Evaluate the specific skill of Mr. Rashed that played a role in his acceptance among workers.
questions.answer:
1. He demonstrates Human (Interpersonal) Skill.

2. This skill allows him to understand and work with people.

3. His focus on welfare built trust and respect.

4. It bridges the gap between management and labor.

fullCQData.stem: Chairman Omar Faruk formulated housing policies for officials. He also introduced pension, medical, and family security benefits. Workers now have high morale.

questions.label: a
questions.text: (a) What are the levels of management?
questions.answer: Management levels refer to the hierarchy of authority and responsibility (Top, Mid, Lower) within an organization.

questions.label: b
questions.text: (b) Is management a profession? Explain.
questions.answer: It is a "quasi-profession" because it requires specialized knowledge and ethics, though it lacks mandatory legal licensing in some areas.

questions.label: c
questions.text: (c) Which level of manager is Mr. Omar Faruk? Explain.
questions.answer:
1. He is a Top-level Manager.

2. As "Chairman," he is the highest authority.

3. He formulates broad institutional policies.

4. He decides long-term benefits and vision.

questions.label: d
questions.text: (d) Analyze Mr. Omar Faruk’s interpersonal skills.
questions.answer:
1. Human Skill: He understands employee needs for security.

2. Morale Building: His policies directly increased worker dedication.

3. Leadership: He acts as a provider and guide.

4. Retention: These skills help in maintaining a stable and loyal workforce.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Amir runs a medicine factory where he sets all policies and supervises personally. He is active in social service and says management is applicable to all fields of life.

questions.label: a
questions.text: (a) What is management?
questions.answer: Management is the art of getting things done through people effectively to reach organizational objectives.

questions.label: b
questions.text: (b) What is meant by the management cycle? Explain.
questions.answer: It is the circular flow of management functions where the end of one cycle (Control) leads to the start of the next (Planning).

questions.label: c
questions.text: (c) At which level of management is Mr. Amir working? Explain.
questions.answer:
1. Mr. Amir is in Top-level Management.

2. He "sets all policies and strategies."

3. He holds the ultimate decision-making power.

4. He oversees the entire factory's direction.

questions.label: d
questions.text: (d) Analyze the validity of Mr. Amir's opinion in light of Socrates' famous quote on management.
questions.answer:
1. Socrates' View: He stated "Management is Universal."

2. Alignment: Amir's claim that it applies "from individuals to all fields" reflects this.

3. Evidence: Management is needed in families, NGOs, and factories.

4. Conclusion: His opinion is highly valid as management is a fundamental life skill.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Shihan is involved in strategic planning and policy formulation for a production-oriented firm. Ms. Maliha provides instructions and advice to floor-in-charge staff to implement those plans. The collective effort and skill of the employees are increasing the firm's profit and image.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Question A is missing from the original row; placeholder inserted.
questions.answer: Management is the process of planning, organizing, staffing, directing, motivating, coordinating, and controlling resources to achieve organizational goals effectively and efficiently.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Question B is missing from the original row; placeholder inserted.
questions.answer: A supervisor is considered a lower-level manager because they are directly involved in the field-level execution of tasks. They oversee the workers' daily activities, provide technical guidance, and ensure that the plans set by higher management are implemented at the operational level.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Question C is missing from the original row; placeholder inserted.
questions.answer:
Ms. Maliha belongs to Mid-level Management.

Explanation: Mid-level managers act as a bridge between top management and the operational level. In the stem, Ms. Maliha takes the plans formulated by Mr. Shihan (Top level) and provides specific instructions and advice to the floor-in-charges to ensure execution. This role of translating strategy into actionable instructions for supervisors is the core characteristic of middle management.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Question D is missing from the original row; placeholder inserted.
questions.answer:
I disagree with the statement. There is a significant difference in the types of skills required for their respective roles:

1. Shihan (Top Level): Primarily requires Conceptual Skills to formulate long-term strategies and navigate complex situations.

2. Maliha (Mid Level): Primarily requires Human (Interpersonal) Skills to lead, motivate, and coordinate with the floor-in-charges.

3. Nature of Work: Shihan focuses on mental labor and decision-making, whereas Maliha focuses on implementation and supervision.

4. Scope: Shihan's skills impact the entire organization's image and profit, while Maliha’s skills focus on departmental efficiency.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Question A is missing from the original row; placeholder inserted.
questions.answer: The Greek philosopher Socrates said "Management is Universal."

questions.label: b
questions.text: Question B is missing from the original row; placeholder inserted.
questions.answer: "Unity is Strength" (Esprit de Corps) is important because it fosters team spirit and coordination. When all members of an organization work together harmoniously toward a common objective, resources are used efficiently and the overall success of the institution is ensured.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Question C is missing from the original row; placeholder inserted.
questions.answer:
A' represents Top-level Management.
* Role: Managers at this level are the highest authority in an organization (e.g., Chairman, Directors).
* Functions: Their primary tasks involve defining long-term goals, formulating broad policies, and determining strategic plans.
* Focus: They focus on the organization as a whole rather than specific departments.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Question D is missing from the original row; placeholder inserted.
questions.answer:
I disagree with this statement. While all are managers, the mix of skills required at each level is fundamentally different:
* Level 'A' (Top): Requires high Conceptual Skills for strategy and policy-making.
* Level 'B' (Mid): Requires high Human (Interpersonal) Skills to bridge the gap between policy and execution.
* Level 'C' (Lower): Requires high Technical Skills to supervise workers directly on the field.
* Labor Type: 'A' involves mostly mental labor, while 'C' involves more physical/technical labor.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Shahed (MD) instructs Mr. Rafiq (Dept. Head) to improve worker quality. Rafiq takes action. Mr. Rashed supervises the production workers directly.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Who is the father of scientific management?
questions.answer: F.W. Taylor is the father of scientific management.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by management?
questions.answer: Management is the process of getting things done through others effectively to reach specific goals.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which management function is Mr. Rafiq involved in? Explain.

fullCQData.stem:
A diagram of the Management Cycle with two missing functions: 'ক' and 'খ'. (Summary of the Management Cycle shown in the Diagram:
Planning (পরিকল্পনা): Setting goals and deciding how to achieve them.
Organizing ('খ' - সংগঠন): Structuring resources and responsibilities.
Staffing (কর্মীসংস্থান): Recruiting and training the right people.
Directing (নির্দেশনা): Guiding and instructing the workforce.
Motivating (প্রেষণা): Inspiring employees to work with high morale.
Coordinating (সমন্বয়): Harmonizing activities across different departments.
Controlling ('ক' - নিয়ন্ত্রণ): Monitoring performance and making corrections.)

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is the management cycle?
questions.answer: The management cycle is the continuous and sequential process of interrelated functions (Planning, Organizing, Staffing, Directing, Motivating, Coordinating, and Controlling) performed to achieve organizational goals.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is an interview conducted?
questions.answer: An interview is conducted during the staffing process to evaluate a candidate's suitability for a specific role. It helps the management assess the candidate's communication skills, personality, and professional knowledge to ensure the right person is selected for the job.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Explain the management function that belongs in position 'ক'.
questions.answer:
Position 'ক' represents Controlling (নিয়ন্ত্রণ).
* Definition: It is the final stage of the management cycle where actual performance is compared against planned standards.
* Process: It involves identifying deviations, analyzing the reasons for failure, and taking corrective actions.
* Link: In the diagram, 'ক' follows 'Coordination' and leads back to 'Planning', which is the core characteristic of the controlling function.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "The function in position 'খ' is like an engine"—Do you agree? Analyze your opinion.
questions.answer:
I agree. Position 'খ' represents Organizing (সংগঠন).
* Engine Analogy: Just as an engine integrates various parts to make a machine run, organizing integrates the "6 Ms" (Man, Machine, Material, Money, Method, Market) to make the plan operational.
* Structuring: It involves defining duties, allocating resources, and establishing authority-responsibility relationships.
* Efficiency: Without organizing, even the best plan remains static; this function provides the "structure" or "engine" that drives the organization toward its goals.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is management?
questions.answer: Management is the art of getting things done through others effectively to achieve specific organizational goals.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by the management cycle?
questions.answer: The management cycle refers to the continuous and sequential process of interrelated functions—planning, organizing, staffing, directing, motivating, coordinating, and controlling—that repeat in a circular manner to sustain an organization.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which management function is performed in position 'ক'? Explain.
questions.answer:
Position 'ক' represents Planning (পরিকল্পনা).
* Definition: It is the primary function of management that involves deciding in advance what is to be done, when, where, how, and by whom.
* Foundation: It acts as the blueprint for all other management activities.
* Placement: In the management cycle, it is the starting point that follows 'Controlling' from the previous cycle, as seen in the circular arrows of the diagram.

questions.label: d
questions.text: What is the relationship between the functions marked 'ক' and 'খ'? Analyze.
questions.answer:
There is a deep and inseparable relationship between 'ক' (Planning) and 'খ' (Controlling).
* Two Sides of a Coin: Planning sets the standards, and Controlling verifies if those standards are met.
* Interdependence: Planning is meaningless without Controlling (as there is no oversight), and Controlling is impossible without Planning (as there is no standard to compare against).
* Corrective Loop: As shown in the diagram, 'খ' (Control) identifies deviations which then serve as the basis for the next 'ক' (Plan), ensuring the cycle remains continuous and effective.

Direct Supervision: As a foreman, his primary duty is to oversee the workers directly and ensure that production meets the required standards.

Strategic Decision: Deciding to expand a factory is a major strategic move that involves high-level planning and resource allocation.
Top-level Authority: Only individuals at the top-level management (such as Directors or MDs) have the authority to make such expansionary decisions and appoint supervisors like Mr. Shafiq.
Objective Setting: By initiating the expansion steps, he is determining the future path and growth targets of the institution.

questions.answer: The management cycle is the continuous, circular process of functions (Planning to Controlling) performed in sequence to achieve goals.

questions.answer: It is a social process because it involves managing and coordinating people (the social element) within a community to achieve common welfare.

questions.answer:

2. Investigation Result: Since no negligence was found at lower levels, the "Standard" set by the top was flawed.

3. Resource Gap: Top management may have failed to provide necessary raw materials or tech.

4. Policy Failure: The plan lacked the flexibility to adapt to field-level realities.

4. It ensures unity of action.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the decision-making arrangement of the two organizations in light of Henri Fayol's principles.
questions.answer:
1. It reflects the principle of Centralization and Decentralization.

2. JAXA kept "core policies" (Centralization).

3. Takara Tomy handled "less critical decisions" (Decentralization).

4. This balance ensures efficiency and speed.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Jamil decided to buy a new machine. He instructed Mr. Akash, a department official, to recruit a mechanical engineer. Mr. Akash began the recruitment process.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is administration?
questions.answer: Administration is the higher-level function of an organization concerned with setting goals, policies, and broad strategies.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What do you mean by the universality of management?
questions.answer: It refers to the global applicability of management principles across all types of organizations and levels of human activity.

questions.label: c
questions.text: At which level of management is Mr. Akash? Explain.
questions.answer:
1. Mr. Akash is a Mid-level Manager.

2. He is a departmental official.

3. He implements orders from top management.

4. He coordinates between levels.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the management function being performed by Mr. Akash.
questions.answer:
1. He is performing Staffing (কর্মীসংস্থান).

2. This involves recruiting and selecting the right person.

3. It ensures technical expertise (Engineer) is available.

4. It is vital for resource management.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Raihan (Director) sets objectives and policies at Square Pharmaceuticals. Mr. Rajin ensures these plans are implemented at the field level.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Who is the father of scientific management?
questions.answer: F.W. Taylor is the father of scientific management.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by motivation?
questions.answer: Motivation is the process of inspiring and encouraging employees to work enthusiastically toward organizational goals.

questions.label: c
questions.text: At which level of management is Mr. Raihan? Explain.
questions.answer:
1. Mr. Raihan is a Top-level Manager.

2. He is a "Director".

3. He handles "policy formulation".

4. He defines the firm's purpose.

questions.label: d
questions.text: How should the mix of conceptual and technical skills differ between Raihan and Rajin?
questions.answer:
1. Raihan (Top): High Conceptual Skill, low technical skill.

2. Rajin (Mid/Lower): High Technical/Human Skill, moderate conceptual skill.

3. This balance ensures strategy meets execution.

fullCQData.stem: Ms. Priti sets policies for TS Co. Mr. Rifat (Dept. Head) implements plans. Mr. Raju (Foreman) supervises and encourages workers directly.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is directing?
questions.answer: Directing is the process of instructing and guiding employees to ensure tasks are performed correctly.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by formal organizational structure?
questions.answer: It is a structure where authority, responsibility, and relationships are clearly defined through official rules and hierarchy.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Analyze Ms. Priti's management level in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
1. Ms. Priti is a Top-level Manager.

2. She is responsible for "policy and planning".

3. She holds ultimate authority.

4. She provides the strategic vision.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Whose contribution is greater for increasing production—Rifat or Raju? Explain.
questions.answer:
1. Mr. Raju's contribution is more direct.

2. As a "foreman," he is at the field level.

3. He directly "supervises and encourages" workers.

4. Production happens under his watch.

mcqData.text: Henri Fayol was a citizen of which country?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol was a French mining engineer and is the father of modern management principles.

mcqData.text:
Included in the principles of management provided by the father of modern management are-

(i) Unity of command
(ii) Division of work
(iii) Principle of flexibility
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol identified 14 principles, including Unity of Command and Division of Work; flexibility is not among his original 14.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Manager Mr. Alfaz of 'XYZ' institution strictly monitors that work is not unequally distributed among employees.] Which principle does he follow?
mcqData.explanation: Ensuring equal distribution of work to avoid overload or underload on individuals follows the principle of Balance.

mcqData.text: What was F.W. Taylor by profession?
mcqData.explanation: F.W. Taylor, the father of Scientific Management, worked as a Mechanical Engineer, but specifically served as a Chief Engineer in a steel company (Mine Engineer/Steel/Metal works background).

mcqData.text:
Scientific management principles are-

(i) Science instead of rule of thumb
(ii) Establishing labor-management relations
(iii) Unity of command
mcqData.explanation: Science instead of rule of thumb and harmonious labor-management relations are key to Taylor's theory; Unity of Command is a Fayol principle.

mcqData.text: Which skill is most required for top-level managers?
mcqData.explanation: Conceptual skill is essential for top-level managers to visualize the organization as a whole and plan strategically.

mcqData.text: Which skill is most required for a Chief Executive Officer (CEO)?
mcqData.explanation: CEOs, being at the highest level of management, require high conceptual skills to handle complex strategic decision-making.

mcqData.text: Which management skill is most required for a 'Supervisor'?
mcqData.explanation: Supervisors work directly with operations and machines; therefore, Technical skill is their most essential requirement.

mcqData.text: Which is a function of administration?
mcqData.explanation: Administration involves the formulation of policies, objectives, and broad planning, while management implements them.

mcqData.text: The 'Scalar Chain' principle relates to-
mcqData.explanation: The Scalar Chain ( জোড়া-মই-শিকল) refers to the hierarchy or formal line of authority from top to bottom.

mcqData.text: Who is the father of scientific management?
mcqData.explanation: F. W. Taylor is globally recognized as the father of scientific management.

mcqData.text: [Stem: An accounts officer asks the peon to serve tea at 11 AM. Another official sends him to photocopy at the same time. The accounts officer gets angry.] Which principle is violated?
mcqData.explanation: Unity of Command states an employee should receive orders from only one superior to avoid confusion and conflict.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] The impact on the organization is-

(i) Frustration among workers
(ii) Increased loyalty
(iii) Chaotic environment created
mcqData.explanation: Violating the unity of command creates confusion, leading to worker frustration and a lack of order (chaos).

mcqData.text: The invention of scientific management was what kind of revolution?
mcqData.explanation: Taylor described the transition to scientific management as a 'Mental Revolution' for both workers and management.

mcqData.text: To perform interpersonal roles effectively, which skill must a manager have?
mcqData.explanation: Human skills involve the ability to work well with others, which is vital for playing interpersonal roles.

mcqData.text: Who is the proponent of motion study?
mcqData.explanation: F. W. Taylor and the Gilbreths pioneered Motion Study to increase efficiency by eliminating unnecessary movements.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Roni, a CEO, gives important work to his preferred employees regardless of skill. Preferred employees get benefits. Targets were not met. Productivity is declining.] Which principle was violated?
mcqData.explanation: Favoritism in benefits and work allocation without regard to merit violates the principle of Equity (Fair Remuneration/Treatment).

mcqData.text:
To increase productivity, Roni's tasks should be-

(i) Evaluate merit
(ii) Change attitude
(iii) Establish equity
mcqData.explanation: Productivity requires merit-based evaluation (Equity), a mindset shift (Mental Revolution), and fair treatment for all staff.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Aiman divides all work into 6 categories based on type. Appointed expert department heads.] Which principle is observed?
mcqData.explanation: Breaking down work into specialized tasks and assigning them to experts is the definition of Division of Work.

mcqData.text:
As a result of Aiman's actions-

(i) Dual subordination avoided
(ii) Non-cooperation will increase
(iii) Workers will become expert in specific tasks
mcqData.explanation: Specialization through division of work makes employees experts, and clear reporting avoids dual subordination. Cooperation is usually improved.

mcqData.text: What is meant by 'Unity of Direction'?
options: Receiving orders from a single superior
mcqData.explanation: In management principles, unity of direction (often confused with unity of command) refers to having one plan and one head for a set of activities with the same objective.

mcqData.text: Preparing an income-expenditure statement by an accountant is which type of skill?
mcqData.explanation: Technical skills refer to the proficiency in using methods, processes, or techniques, like accounting statements.

mcqData.text:
Essential skills for good management are-

(i) Interpersonal skill
(ii) Skill of suppression
(iii) Problem perception skill
mcqData.explanation: Good management requires human (interpersonal) skills and the ability to perceive and solve problems.

mcqData.text: How many principles of management are there?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol formulated 14 fundamental principles of management.

mcqData.text: Who is the author of the book 'The Principles of Scientific Management'?
mcqData.explanation: F. W. Taylor is the author of the landmark book 'The Principles of Scientific Management' (1911).

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mrs. Soma divides organizational tasks under different departments and appoints experienced individuals as heads.] Which management principle is followed?
mcqData.explanation: Assigning specific tasks to specific departments based on specialization is the Principle of Division of Work.

mcqData.text: What is a core principle of scientific management?
mcqData.explanation: One of the primary scientific management principles is establishing harmonious relationships between labor and management.

mcqData.text: [Stem: To investigate a fire in 'Bonlota' garment and recommend prevention, 5 officers were tasked. Due to differences in opinion, reporting is delayed.] What type of structure is this?
mcqData.explanation: A group of individuals tasked with solving a specific problem or making a recommendation is a Committee structure.

mcqData.text:
Which of Henri Fayol's principles could eliminate the delay in the committee?

(i) Esprit de corps
(ii) Discipline
(iii) Authority and Responsibility
mcqData.explanation: Delay due to disagreement can be resolved by 'Esprit de corps' (team unity), 'Discipline' (orderly work), and clear 'Authority & Responsibility'.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mina has been attaching buttons on shirts for five years. As a result, she can do it with 100% accuracy.] Which principle of Fayol does this align with?
mcqData.explanation: Specialization through repeated performance of a specific task is the result of the Principle of Division of Work.

mcqData.text:
Which is true about Henri Fayol?

(i) Father of modern management
(ii) Inventor of 14 principles
(iii) Born in 1841
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol is the father of modern management, defined 14 principles, and was born in 1841 in Istanbul.

mcqData.text: What does 'Scalar chain' mean?
mcqData.explanation: Scalar chain refers to the hierarchy or formal line of authority connecting all superiors from the top to the lowest rank.

mcqData.text: What is the meaning of the French word 'Esprit De Corps'?
mcqData.explanation: Esprit de corps' is a French term meaning team spirit or unity within an organization.

mcqData.text: [Stem: A company made work processes simple and independent to increase discipline and creativity. Workers can now make decisions within rules, improving collective results.] Which principle is applied?
mcqData.explanation: Providing independence and decision-making power to lower levels of the organization is Decentralization.

mcqData.text: What can be the result of increased discipline and creativity among workers?
mcqData.explanation: Improved employee decision-making and processes directly lead to increased productivity and effectiveness.

mcqData.text:
Standards of a profession include-

(i) Certification
(ii) Professional association
(iii) Code of conduct
mcqData.explanation: A profession is defined by formal training (certification), an association, and a binding code of conduct.

mcqData.text: Which is the most important function of administration?
mcqData.explanation: Administration is primarily concerned with policy formulation and "thinking" (conceptualizing), while management is execution.

mcqData.text: How many primary roles does an ideal manager have to play in daily tasks?
mcqData.explanation: According to Mintzberg, managers play three main types of roles: Interpersonal, Informational, and Decisional.

mcqData.text: What revolution is implied in scientific management?
mcqData.explanation: F. W. Taylor referred to the paradigm shift in the relationship between labor and management as a "Mental Revolution."

mcqData.text: Who wrote the famous book 'Shop Management'?
mcqData.explanation: Shop Management' (1903) is one of the earliest influential works written by F. W. Taylor.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Ms. Maliha is an accountant at South Sandwip College. She accurately records and maintains the college's financial transactions.] Which management skill is reflected in her actions?
mcqData.explanation: Technical skills involve the ability to perform specialized tasks such as accounting, recording, and financial maintenance.

mcqData.text: Management can be compared to which organ of the human body?
mcqData.explanation: The brain controls and coordinates all body functions, just as management controls and coordinates organizational activities.

mcqData.text: The 'Scalar Chain' principle relates to which of the following?
mcqData.explanation: The Scalar Chain refers to the formal line of authority from the highest to the lowest rank.

mcqData.text:
Required skills for an ideal manager are-

(i) Skill of suppression
(ii) Interpersonal skill
(iii) Problem perception skill
mcqData.explanation: An ideal manager requires human/interpersonal skills and conceptual/problem perception skills. "Skill of suppression" is not a standard management skill.

mcqData.text: To keep pace with changing situations for achieving institutional objectives, which principle should be followed?
mcqData.explanation: The principle of flexibility allows management to adapt its plans and strategies to changing environmental circumstances.

mcqData.text: How many principles of management were given by Henri Fayol?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol famously introduced 14 universal principles of management.

mcqData.text: Which of Henri Fayol's principles ensures equal and impartial treatment of employees?
mcqData.explanation: The principle of Equity implies that managers should be kind and fair to their subordinates to ensure loyalty and devotion.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Aminul Islam keeps all information of employees. He has delegated some decision-making authority to subordinates so that employees of all departments can be utilized.] Which of Fayol's principles did he follow?
mcqData.explanation: Delegating decision-making power to lower levels of management is the essence of the principle of Decentralization.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] The qualities of an ideal manager in Mr. Aminul Islam are-

(i) Experience
(ii) Risk-taking
(iii) Communication skills
mcqData.explanation: An effective manager needs experience for decision-making, the willingness to take calculated risks, and communication skills for coordination.

mcqData.text: Which scholar suggested using scientific methods for every task instead of the 'rule of thumb' method?
mcqData.explanation: F. W. Taylor, in his Scientific Management theory, emphasized replacing 'rule of thumb' with scientific study.

mcqData.text:
Among the 14 management principles provided by Henri Fayol are-

(i) Unity of Command
(ii) Unity of Direction
(iii) Principle of Flexibility
mcqData.explanation: Fayol's 14 principles include Unity of Command and Unity of Direction; flexibility is generally not listed as one of his 14 core principles.

mcqData.text: The principle of adapting to changing situations is-
mcqData.explanation: Flexibility (Flexibility Principle) is essential for adapting organizational operations to external changes.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Sharmin has been doing only sewing work in a garment factory for 5 years. Now she can do the work with efficiency.] This falls under which principle?
mcqData.explanation: Assigning a specific task to one person repeatedly leads to specialization, which is the Principle of Division of Work.

mcqData.text: Which principle helps avoid dual subordination?
mcqData.explanation: Unity of Command ensures that an employee receives orders from only one superior, preventing dual reporting.

mcqData.text: Who is included in administration?
mcqData.explanation: Administration involves the top-level governing body, such as the Board of Directors, responsible for policy and objective setting.

mcqData.text: Which principle is needed to make the control system effective?
mcqData.explanation: The Principle of Exception allows management to focus on significant deviations rather than routine issues, making control effective.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Ms. Rafika is a design manager. She always creates new designs to achieve organizational goals so that changing situations can be easily faced.] Which principle does she follow?
mcqData.explanation: Constantly creating new designs to adapt to changing environments is an application of the Principle of Flexibility.

mcqData.text: What indicates the extent of decentralization?
mcqData.explanation: Decentralization is measured by the extent to which authority is delegated from top management to lower levels.

mcqData.text: Whose work is it to set organizational objectives?
mcqData.explanation: Setting organizational objectives and broad policy is the primary function of Administration.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Matin is the Managing Director of 'Rajshahi Textile Mills'. Rahim works as a crane operator in the same institution.] At which level does Mr. Matin work?
mcqData.explanation: The Managing Director (MD) holds the highest position in an organization, functioning at the Top-level of management.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Matin is the MD of 'Rajshahi Textile Mills'. Rahim works as a crane operator in the same institution.] Which type of skill is most needed for Rahim's performance?
mcqData.explanation: Crane operation is a specialized operational task, which requires high Technical skills.

mcqData.text: To whom are the administrators of a company accountable?
mcqData.explanation: Administrators/Top management act on behalf of the shareholders who own the company.

mcqData.text: The 'Scalar Chain' principle relates to which of the following?
mcqData.explanation: The Scalar Chain refers to the hierarchical line of authority flow from top to bottom.

mcqData.text:
The roles of an ideal manager include-

(i) Interpersonal roles
(ii) Informational roles
(iii) Decisional roles
mcqData.explanation: Henry Mintzberg identified these three major categories (Interpersonal, Informational, and Decisional) as the core roles of a manager.

mcqData.text: How many principles of management were given by Henri Fayol?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol formulated the 14 universal principles of management.

mcqData.text: Where was Henri Fayol born?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol was born in Istanbul, Turkey, in 1841.

mcqData.text: What is the lifespan of F. W. Taylor?
mcqData.explanation: F. W. Taylor (the father of scientific management) lived from 1856 to 1915.

mcqData.text: From which language does the word 'Esprit de Corps' originate?
mcqData.explanation: Esprit de Corps' is a French term meaning "team spirit" or unity.

mcqData.text:
F. W. Taylor's notable contributions to management include-

(i) Introduction of differential piece-rate
(ii) Centralization and decentralization principles
(iii) Time study
mcqData.explanation: Taylor's major contributions are Differential Piece-Rate and Time/Motion study; Centralization/Decentralization is primarily a Fayol principle.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Shamim takes some decisions himself and delegates others to subordinates. He always prioritizes institutional profit over personal.] Which principle regarding decision-making is followed?
mcqData.explanation: Delegating authority while retaining some decision-making power is the Principle of Centralization and Decentralization.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] As a result of the steps taken by Mr. Shamim-

(i) Costs will increase
(ii) Workers become skilled
(iii) Achievement of institutional goals becomes easier
mcqData.explanation: Delegation of authority empowers employees (making them skilled) and streamlines operations, facilitating the achievement of goals.

mcqData.text: Of which country was Henri Fayol a resident?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol was a French citizen and mining engineer.
