mcqData.text: In which year was F. W. Taylor born?
mcqData.explanation: F. W. Taylor was born in 1856 in Philadelphia, USA.

mcqData.text: [Stem: An executive believes that success is impossible if the right person is not in the right place and the right object is not in the right place.] To which management principle does this refer?
mcqData.explanation: The Principle of Order (Fayol) emphasizes "A place for everything and everything in its place" (including people).

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] The advantage of adopting this principle is-

(i) Merit is valued
(ii) Unity of command is established
(iii) Proper transfer/placement of resources
mcqData.explanation: This principle ensures that the right person is at the right job and resources are managed efficiently, improving placement and value.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Nirob divides work and specifies responsibility and authority. If there is an obstacle, the responsible person can be identified.] Which principle is observed?
mcqData.explanation: Assigning specific tasks and responsibilities to individuals is the Principle of Division of Work.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] Due to the principles adopted by Mr. Nirob, what will happen?

(i) Complexity decreases
(ii) Employee efficiency increases
(iii) Supervision and accountability become easy
mcqData.explanation: Specialization reduces complexity, improves efficiency, and makes accountability clear through defined roles.

mcqData.text: Which is compared to the brain of the human body in management?
mcqData.explanation: Management is often compared to the brain because it is responsible for planning, controlling, and coordinating the "body" of the organization.

mcqData.text: "An individual should have only one order-giver"—this statement supports which principle?
mcqData.explanation: Unity of Command specifically states that each employee should receive orders from only one superior.

mcqData.text:
Conceptual skills include-

(i) Correct forecasting
(ii) Encouraging people
(iii) Decision-making in emerging situations
mcqData.explanation: Conceptual skills involve looking at the big picture, forecasting, and handling emerging strategic situations. Encouraging people is a Human skill.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Zahir divides organizational tasks into Production, Purchase, Sales, and Accounts departments and appoints experts as heads.] Which management principle is followed?
mcqData.explanation: Assigning specific work to specific departments based on specialization is the Principle of Division of Work.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Same as above] As a result of Mr. Zahir's steps-
options: Employees will become expert in specific tasks
mcqData.explanation: Division of work allows employees to focus on specific tasks, which leads to specialization and expertise.

mcqData.text: Which management principle does not approve dual subordination?
mcqData.explanation: The Principle of Unity of Command mandates that an employee should receive orders from only one superior.

mcqData.text: In which management principle is it mentioned to treat employees fairly?
mcqData.explanation: The Principle of Equity mandates that managers should be kind, fair, and impartial to all employees.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Ashfaq builds a sense of unity, compromise, and mutual cooperation among his employees.] Which principle of Henry Fayol does he follow?
options: Subordination of individual interest to general interest
mcqData.explanation: Esprit de corps' (Unity is Strength) emphasizes the promotion of team spirit and harmony among staff.

mcqData.text:
In decision-making-

(i) Institutional capacity must be considered
(ii) Existing employees must be laid off
(iii) Efficient use of resources must be considered
mcqData.explanation: Rational decision-making requires analyzing institutional capacity and resource efficiency; laying off employees is not a standard requirement.

mcqData.text: Which principle mentions adapting to changing situations?
mcqData.explanation: The Principle of Flexibility allows an organization to change plans according to the evolving needs of the environment.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. 'Y' is a foreman. He supervises 6 workers. He loves praise. He gives more benefits to those who praise him, even if they work less.] Which management principle is violated?
mcqData.explanation: Favoritism and biased allocation of benefits based on personal praise violates the Principle of Equity (fairness).

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] The impact on the workers will be-

(i) Job satisfaction will decrease
(ii) Interest in work will decrease
(iii) Productivity will decrease
mcqData.explanation: Unfair treatment leads to lower job satisfaction, loss of motivation (interest), and reduced organizational productivity.

mcqData.text: Henri Fayol was a citizen of which country?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol was a French mining engineer and management theorist.

mcqData.text: Unity of command means-
mcqData.explanation: Unity of Command means an employee receives instructions and orders from only one superior.

mcqData.text: [Stem: A manager noticed that his computer operator is better at accounting, while his accountant is better at computer work.] Which management principle is violated?
mcqData.explanation: Assigning the wrong person to the wrong task (mismatching skills) violates the Principle of Order and indirectly creates inequity in productivity.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Dr. Joya works in the surgery department of a medical college. She is famous for surgery. She performs 8 surgeries daily.] Which skill is most needed?
mcqData.explanation: Surgical work is a highly specialized task, which requires advanced Technical skills.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] The qualities required for Dr. Joya's work are-

(i) Education
(ii) Experience
(iii) Patience
mcqData.explanation: Specialized surgical work demands formal education, long-term experience, and immense patience.

mcqData.text: Who is the author of the famous book "General and Industrial Management"?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol wrote this seminal book in 1916.

mcqData.text: The 'Unity of Command' principle is the opposite of which of Taylor's principles?
mcqData.explanation: Functional Foremanship involves having multiple foremen supervise one worker, which directly contradicts Fayol's Unity of Command (one boss).

mcqData.text: Which management principle ensures that orders and organizational instructions flow from top to bottom?
mcqData.explanation: Chain of Command (Scalar Chain) ensures the formal vertical flow of authority and instructions.

mcqData.text: Evaluating all employees impartially is synonymous with which principle?
mcqData.explanation: The Principle of Equity ensures that employees are treated fairly, kindly, and impartially.

mcqData.text: Where was Henri Fayol born?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol was born in Istanbul, Turkey.

mcqData.text: How many 'ideals' or principles of scientific management are there?
mcqData.explanation: F. W. Taylor proposed 4 main principles of scientific management.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Ms. Mim is the Managing Director of ABC Bank Ltd. She wants to formulate a sound plan for her institution.] In this case, which task must she do first?
mcqData.explanation: Planning always begins with the identification and setting of organizational goals and objectives.

mcqData.text: Which work is associated with the 'Scalar Chain' principle?
mcqData.explanation: The Scalar Chain refers to the hierarchical line of authority flow from top to bottom.

mcqData.text: Who wrote the book "The Principles of Scientific Management"?
mcqData.explanation: F. W. Taylor is the author of this landmark book published in 1911.

mcqData.text:
Differences between administration and management include-

(i) Difference in level
(ii) Difference in labor
(iii) Difference in power
mcqData.explanation: Administration and management differ in their organizational hierarchy (level), nature of work (labor), and authority (power).

mcqData.text: Which management principle mentions adapting to changing situations?
mcqData.explanation: The Principle of Flexibility allows organizations to adapt strategies to changing environmental factors.

mcqData.text: Who wrote the book "Administration Industrielle et Générale"?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol wrote this seminal book, which is translated as "General and Industrial Management".

mcqData.text: What was Henri Fayol's profession in his professional life?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol was a renowned French mining engineer and an executive in the mining industry.

mcqData.text: How many principles did Henri Fayol mention?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol formulated the 14 universal principles of management.

mcqData.text: In which principle is the avoidance of dual subordination mentioned?
mcqData.explanation: Unity of Command states that an employee should receive orders from only one superior to avoid confusion.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Rakib is the MD of Vertical Ltd. He has to sign important documents and represents the company in branch openings.] Which role of an ideal manager is he performing?
mcqData.explanation: A Figurehead role involves performing symbolic or representative duties like signing documents and attending ceremonies.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Ranjan is a partner in a CA firm. He is an audit expert. Once he starts a task, he puts in his utmost effort to finish it.] Which management skill is more important for him?
mcqData.explanation: Since he is an audit expert performing specialized tasks (auditing), his Technical skills are the most critical.

mcqData.text: What kind of problem arises if authority is higher but responsibility is lower?
mcqData.explanation: Excessive authority without commensurate responsibility often leads to the misuse of power and autocratic behavior.

mcqData.text:
To overcome management inefficiency in Bangladesh, what is needed?

(i) Recognition of management as a discipline
(ii) Creation of training opportunities
(iii) Provision for management education
mcqData.explanation: Professionalizing management through recognition, training, and academic education is essential for improving efficiency.

mcqData.text: What is the first book of F.W. Taylor?
mcqData.explanation: F. W. Taylor's early work was "Shop Management" (1903), followed by his later major theory book.

mcqData.text: Which skill is equally necessary for all types of directors/managers?
mcqData.explanation: Interpersonal (human) skills are essential at all levels of management for working effectively with others.

mcqData.text: According to which management principle is the decision-making power of employees reduced?
mcqData.explanation: Centralization is the concentration of decision-making authority at the top level, thereby reducing power for subordinates.

mcqData.text: Who said that productivity can be increased by preventing waste through the development of management?
mcqData.explanation: Robert Owen, a pioneer of management, emphasized improving human efficiency and welfare to increase productivity.

mcqData.text: For the development of which theory did Henri Fayol start the "Centre of Administrative Studies"?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol founded this centre to promote his Administrative (Management) theory.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Ahmed is an officer in a motorcycle factory. Five workers work under his direct supervision.] What kind of skill does Mr. Ahmed need most?
mcqData.explanation: Supervisors working directly with workers and mechanical tasks require high Technical skills.

mcqData.text: What is the number of Henri Fayol's management principles?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol formulated 14 management principles.

mcqData.text: The 'Scalar Chain' principle relates to which of the following?
mcqData.explanation: Scalar Chain relates to the line of authority flowing from the highest to lowest ranks.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Shahid is going to take long-term plans for his institution.] As a manager, which skill does he need most now?
mcqData.explanation: Long-term strategic planning is a conceptual activity requiring high Conceptual skills.

mcqData.text:
Notable topics in F.W. Taylor's research work are-

(i) Time study
(ii) Motion study
(iii) Fatigue study
mcqData.explanation: Taylor's scientific management research famously includes Time, Motion, and Fatigue studies.

mcqData.text: Which principle mentions exhibiting equal treatment towards employees?
mcqData.explanation: The Principle of Equity ensures that managers treat all subordinates with fairness and impartiality.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Everyone in the 'GQ' institution prioritizes institutional profit/growth over personal profit. As a result, the institution is at the top.] Which principle should be followed?
options: Subordination of individual interest to general interest
mcqData.explanation: Prioritizing the institution's goals over individual interests is the Principle of Subordination of Individual Interest to General Interest.

mcqData.text: Who wrote the book "Industrial and General Administration"?
mcqData.explanation: Henri Fayol wrote this book, widely recognized in English as "General and Industrial Management".

mcqData.text:
Among Henri Fayol's 14 principles are-

(i) Unity of command
(ii) Unity of direction
(iii) Unity of flexibility
mcqData.explanation: Fayol's principles include Unity of Command and Unity of Direction; flexibility is not one of the 14 core principles.

mcqData.text: According to which management principle is the employee's role in decision-making reduced?
mcqData.explanation: Centralization concentrates power at the top, reducing subordinate involvement in decision-making.

mcqData.text: Who is included in administration?
mcqData.explanation: Administration involves top-level governing bodies (like the Chief Executive), responsible for policy and broad objectives.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Ruhul Amin keeps records of all employee data. He delegates some less important decision-making tasks to subordinates.] Which principle of Henry Fayol has he followed?
mcqData.explanation: Delegating decision-making authority to lower levels is the principle of Decentralization.

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Ruhul Amin maintains all employee data and delegates minor decisions to them to utilize all departments.] The qualities of a manager Ruhul Amin possesses-

(i) Organizational skill
(ii) Risk-taking
(iii) Communication skill
mcqData.explanation: Ruhul Amin demonstrates organizational skill (managing data/tasks) and communication skill (delegating/coordinating), but risk-taking is not explicitly indicated.

mcqData.text: Which management principle does not approve of dual subordination?
mcqData.explanation: The Principle of Unity of Command explicitly states that an employee should receive orders from only one superior.

mcqData.text: Who is the father of scientific management?
mcqData.explanation: F. W. Taylor is globally recognized as the father of scientific management.

mcqData.text: Impartial behavior relates to which management principle?
mcqData.explanation: The Principle of Equity mandates that managers treat all employees with fairness and without partiality.

mcqData.text: [Stem: Mr. Abir is CEO of Green Hospital. Doctors, nurses, and staff work in an integrated/coordinated manner, leading to customer satisfaction and reputation.] Which principle did the hospital follow?
mcqData.explanation: The coordinated effort of all employees toward a common goal is the essence of 'Esprit de corps' (Unity is Strength).

mcqData.text:
[Stem: Same as above] Implementation of this principle results in-

(i) Quality of customer service increases
(ii) Employee morale increases
(iii) Waste decreases
mcqData.explanation: Team unity (Esprit de corps) naturally improves coordination, service quality, employee morale, and operational efficiency (reduced waste).

mcqData.text: What does the French term 'Esprit de corps' mean?
mcqData.explanation: Esprit de corps' is a French term that translates to team spirit or "Unity is Strength".

mcqData.text: The formula for managerial skill or success is-
mcqData.explanation: The formula S = M + m + T (where M=Management, m=Manpower/money/materials, T=Technology) represents managerial success factors.

mcqData.text: Conceptual skill of a manager includes-
mcqData.explanation: Conceptual skill is the ability to visualize the organization as a whole, including the ability to foresee future situations.

mcqData.text: A manager who possesses knowledge of industrial processes, equipment, and potential problems has acquired what kind of skill?
mcqData.explanation: Knowledge of tools, processes, and specific operational problems is the definition of Technical skill.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Maruf is a supervisor of a production project. For his work, he has to be accountable to multiple managers. Due to being accountable to multiple managers at the same time, the dynamics of his work are hampered. He is thinking of informing the higher authority about the matter.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Where was Henri Fayol born?
questions.answer: Henri Fayol was born in Istanbul, Turkey.

questions.label: b
questions.text: "Management is a separate branch of knowledge"—Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Management is recognized as a separate branch of knowledge because it has its own distinct theories, principles, concepts, and specialized methods that can be taught, learned, and applied systematically.

Explanation: Traditionally, management was often thought of as an inherent art or talent. However, over time, extensive research and contributions from scholars like Henri Fayol and Frederick Winslow Taylor have developed it into a structured discipline. Today, management is taught globally in universities and business schools as a professional degree, possessing its own literature, code of conduct, and specialized techniques, which qualifies it as a distinct branch of knowledge.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which level of management's responsibility is Mr. Maruf performing? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: Mr. Maruf is performing the responsibilities of the lower-level management (or supervisory/operational management).

Explanation: The lower level of management is the bottom-most tier of an organizational hierarchy, situated directly above the non-managerial workers. Supervisors, foremen, and line managers belong to this level. Their primary role is to execute the plans and decisions made by higher management, oversee day-to-day operations, and directly manage workers on the field.

Application to Stem: In the stem, Mr. Maruf is explicitly mentioned as a supervisor of a production project. He works on the operational ground floor, translating managerial directives into actual production activities and reporting to higher-level managers. Therefore, his position and duties clearly align with lower-level management.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Which principle of management is being violated, causing problems in Mr. Maruf's work? Evaluate it in the light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The principle of management being violated in Mr. Maruf's work is the "Unity of Command" (আদেশের ঐক্য নীতি).

Explanation: The principle of unity of command states that an employee should receive orders and instructions from only one direct superior at any given time, and should be accountable to that superior alone. If an employee receives orders from multiple bosses simultaneously, it leads to confusion, conflict, overlapping authority, and evasion of responsibility, ultimately reducing operational efficiency.

Application to Stem: According to the stem, Mr. Maruf has to remain accountable to multiple managers at the same time for his supervisor duties. This dual subordination has severely disrupted the dynamics and speed of his work, causing unnecessary friction and decision paralysis. He is unable to prioritize instructions, which is a textbook consequence of violating the unity of command.

Evaluation: Based on the analysis, the violation of this principle is the direct root cause of Mr. Maruf's workplace issues. To restore work momentum, eliminate confusion, and ensure clear accountability, management must strictly adhere to the unity of command. Mr. Maruf's decision to inform higher authorities is entirely justified, as a structured organization cannot function efficiently without a single, clear line of command for its field supervisors.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Sohan is the Managing Director of 'X' Ltd. pharmaceutical company. He runs the organization skillfully and in the right method by using machinery. Due to this, the organization becomes profitable. Recently, he has started retrenching workers even for minor mistakes. As a result, the employees remain constantly panicked and leave the company whenever they find a better job elsewhere. Consequently, the productivity of the organization is decreasing.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a principle?
questions.answer: A principle is a fundamental truth or a set of guidelines that serves as a basis for systemized belief, behavior, or action.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is F.W. Taylor called the father of scientific management?
questions.answer:
Knowledge: F.W. Taylor (Frederick Winslow Taylor) is called the father of scientific management because he replaced traditional "rule-of-thumb" methods with systematic, scientific analysis to maximize industrial efficiency.

Explanation: Taylor conducted groundbreaking time, motion, and fatigue studies to standardize work procedures and determine the most efficient way to perform tasks. By publishing his core philosophies in his famous book "The Principles of Scientific Management" (1911), he laid down the foundation for scientific selection, training of workers, and harmonious cooperation between management and labor, revolutionizing the field of industrial production.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Due to which skill of an ideal manager did Mr. Sohan's organization become profitable? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: Mr. Sohan's organization became profitable due to his exceptional "Technical Skill" (কারিগরি দক্ষতা).

Explanation: Technical skill refers to the specialized knowledge, proficiency, and ability to use specific tools, machinery, procedures, techniques, and methodologies required to perform specific operational tasks. It is highly critical for managers to understand these mechanics to ensure that resources and equipment are being utilized at their optimum capacity.

Application to Stem: In the stem, it is stated that Mr. Sohan managed the company skillfully and in the correct method by utilizing the machinery. His ability to properly operate and guide the technical framework shifted the enterprise into a highly profitable state. This practical proficiency in handling operational resources directly highlights his strong technical skill.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the principle whose absence is causing the decrease in productivity in Mr. Sohan's organization.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The principle whose absence is causing the decrease in productivity in Mr. Sohan's organization is the "Stability of Tenure of Personnel" (কর্মীদের চাকরির স্থায়িত্বের নীতি).

Explanation: This principle, formulated by Henri Fayol, dictates that employees should be given job security and sufficient time to settle into their roles to perform efficiently. High employee turnover is both a cause and effect of bad management. If employees fear losing their jobs constantly, their morale drops, they cannot focus, and the organizational costs of recruitment and retraining rise significantly.

Application to Stem: According to the stem, Mr. Sohan began laying off employees over trivial mistakes. This created an atmosphere of constant fear and panic among the workforce. Instead of developing loyalty, the workers started fleeing the organization as soon as they found alternative employment, resulting in a severe drop in overall organizational productivity.

Evaluation: Based on the scenario, the severe lack of job security is the primary driving factor behind the company's declining efficiency. While technical prowess initially brought profits, ignoring the stability of personnel destroyed employee morale and human resource continuity. Therefore, to reverse the trend of falling productivity, Mr. Sohan must urgently reinstate job safety, replace fear with constructive feedback, and respect this vital management principle.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Sifat is an efficient manager. He looks at all his subordinates with the same eye. The subordinates also respect him. As a result, the mutual relationship among them remains very strong. Besides, he assigns and places the human resources and other materials of the organization in the right place, so that efficient performance is ensured and no one faces any problem during work expansion.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is administration?
questions.answer: Administration refers to the highest-level authoritative body or function in an organization responsible for determining overall goals, policies, and major plans.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is interpersonal skill important for a sales officer?
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Interpersonal skill is vital for a sales officer because it enables them to effectively communicate, build positive relationships, and easily persuade diverse types of prospective customers.

Explanation: A sales officer's primary responsibility is to interact directly with clients and convert potential leads into successful sales. Since human behavior varies from person to person, a sales officer equipped with high interpersonal skills can empathize with customers, patiently address grievances, and establish a foundation of trust, which directly boosts consumer loyalty and the company's long-term sales figures.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which principle of management did Mr. Sifat follow in the first phase mentioned in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: In the first phase mentioned in the stem, Mr. Sifat followed the "Principle of Equity" (সাম্যের নীতি) of management.

Explanation: The principle of equity emphasizes that a manager must treat all subordinates with justice, fairness, and equal affection, completely free from any personal bias, nepotism, or discrimination. When employees observe that justice prevails and everyone is evaluated solely on merit, their loyalty, dedication, and dedication towards the organization increase significantly.

Application to Stem: According to the stem, Mr. Sifat treats all his subordinates with the same eye without any discrimination. This balanced, fair behavior has earned him immense respect from his subordinates and fostered a highly robust mutual relationship between them. This scenario perfectly mirrors the core application of the principle of equity.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the importance of the principle mentioned in the last part of the stem in making an organization dynamic.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The principle mentioned in the last part of the stem is the "Principle of Order" (শৃঙ্খলার নীতি), which plays an indispensable role in making an organization dynamic.

Explanation: The principle of order does not mean maintaining discipline; rather, it refers to "a right place for everything/everyone and everything/everyone in its/their right place." It ensures that material resources are structured properly and human resources are placed into positions that best match their individual qualifications, eliminating idle time, wastage, and operational chaos.

Application to Stem: The stem states that Mr. Sifat systematically places the organization's manpower and materials in their appropriate locations. This deliberate approach guarantees efficient work execution and smooth operational expansion without creating unnecessary workplace bottlenecks or confusion for anyone involved.

Evaluation: Based on the analysis, the principle of order acts as a cornerstone for maintaining operational momentum. When the right person is assigned the right task and materials are readily accessible, confusion is minimized, processing speed accelerates, and overall productivity skyrockets. Therefore, it can be concluded that adopting this principle is vital to transforming an ordinary business into a highly dynamic, smooth-running, and competitive enterprise.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Dulal is a public relations officer of a company. He works to maintain communication with various internal and external parties related to the company and to create a good image of the organization. The Managing Director of the organization is a very busy person. He has to think about which direction the organization will go in the future. He can anticipate the future using his insight. The Board of Directors highly respects this skill of his.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a management cycle?
questions.answer: Management cycle refers to the continuous, sequential process of management functions starting from planning and moving through organizing, staffing, directing, motivating, controlling, and back to planning.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by the principle of scalar chain?
questions.answer:
Knowledge: The principle of scalar chain (জোড়া-মই-শিকলের নীতি) means that the line of authority, communication, and accountability should run smoothly in an unbroken chain from the highest rank to the lowest rank within an organization.

Explanation: According to Henri Fayol, this principle ensures that every position in the hierarchy is linked, preventing any gaps in communication or command. It establishes who reports to whom, thereby minimizing organizational confusion; however, in emergencies, a temporary cross-link called a "gang-plank" can be used to speed up communication between peers without breaking the core hierarchy.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which type of managerial role is evident in Mr. Dulal's work in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: In the stem, the "Interpersonal Role" (specifically the Liaison role) of a manager is evident in Mr. Dulal's work.

Explanation: According to Henry Mintzberg, managers perform interpersonal roles to maintain smooth organizational relationships and build social networks. The liaison role within this category involves establishing and maintaining communication channels with both internal stakeholders and external entities (like suppliers, media, public, and government) to create a cooperative web of information.

Application to Stem: In the stem, Mr. Dulal serves as a public relations officer. His core duties involve keeping in touch with various parties inside and outside the company and building a favorable public image for the enterprise. Because his tasks center heavily on network building and serving as a bridge between the firm and external environments, his activities directly represent the interpersonal liaison role.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Under which managerial skill does the quality of the Managing Director, which everyone respects, fall? Evaluate its role in the progress of the organization in the light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The quality of the Managing Director highly respected by everyone falls under "Conceptual Skill" (কাল্পনিক বা ধারণা সংক্রান্ত দক্ষতা).

Explanation: Conceptual skill represents a manager's mental capacity to visualize the entire organization as a unified whole, analyze complex situations, anticipate future trends, and formulate long-term strategic plans. It is the most critical attribute for top-level managers, as their primary responsibility is to chart the future pathway of the enterprise based on market changes and internal potential.

Application to Stem: According to the stem, the Managing Director is a top-tier executive who constantly deliberates over the future strategic direction of the company. Equipped with sharp personal insight, he can successfully predict future industry trends, challenges, and opportunities, a specialized capability that commands immense respect from the Board of Directors.

Evaluation: Based on the scenario, conceptual skill plays an indispensable role in securing the long-term progress of the organization. While operational or technical skills keep daily functions running, it is the conceptual skill of top leadership that steers the business away from structural threats and guides it toward sustainable growth. Without this visionary capacity to anticipate the future, an enterprise would struggle to adapt to volatile market forces, making this skill the ultimate anchor for institutional advancement.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Nabin Hosain is a production manager of a manufacturing organization. He has taken various steps to implement the production work of his organization. But the way he has structured different departments shows that some departments are overly burdened with work, while some other departments are spending idle time. Besides, he does not look at all the employees of the organization with an equal eye. This creates various types of conflicts among the workers.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is called a principle of management?
questions.answer: Principles of management refer to the fundamental, time-tested truths or general guidelines that managers follow to execute organizational activities and make decisions.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is the principle of "Unity is strength" important in achieving goals?
questions.answer:
Knowledge: The principle of "Unity is strength" (Esprit de Corps) is important because it fosters team spirit, mutual trust, and collective cooperation among employees, making it easier to achieve challenging organizational goals.

Explanation: In any organization, individual efforts cannot bring success unless they are unified toward a common objective. When management promotes a sense of unity and belongings, employees work together as a cohesive unit, which minimizes internal friction, increases operational speed, and helps overcome organizational obstacles efficiently.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which principle of management was not followed first in the organization of the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The principle of management that was first not followed in the organization is the "Principle of Balanced Workload or Division of Work" (specifically focusing on the lack of "Balance/Harmony" in organizational design). Note: In standard Henri Fayol principles, this structural imbalance directly violates the core spirit of proper Division of Work and Responsibility (কার্যবিভাজন নীতি).

Explanation: The principle of division of work implies dividing the total organizational task into small, manageable parts and assigning them to individuals or departments based on capability. A crucial aspect of this setup is balancing the workload across sections. If departments are structured haphazardly, it creates an uneven distribution where one unit gets exhausted while another sits idle, breaking organizational harmony and reducing efficiency.

Application to Stem: According to the stem, Mr. Nabin structured his various departments in an unscientific and unequal manner. As a direct result, some departments became excessively overburdened with pressure, while other departments were left spending idle time with no significant tasks. This structural flaw clearly shows that the principle of balanced division of work and organizational harmony was completely ignored in the initial departmental design phase.

questions.label: d
questions.text: What kind of steps do you think Mr. Nabin Hosain should take to resolve the conflicts among the employees?
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: To resolve the conflicts among the employees, Mr. Nabin Hosain must strictly implement the "Principle of Equity" (সাম্যের নীতি) and restructure workloads according to scientific management principles.

Explanation: The principle of equity states that managers must treat all subordinates with equal affection, fairness, and justice, entirely free from personal bias or favoritism. When an executive practices favoritism or distributes work unjustly, it breeds resentment, destroys morale, and turns the workplace into a breeding ground for destructive internal politics and interpersonal conflicts.

Application to Stem: The stem reveals that Mr. Nabin does not view all his employees with an equal eye, which has directly triggered intense jealousy and psychological conflicts among the workforce. Combined with the frustratingly unequal distribution of workloads between departments, the human resource environment of his manufacturing firm has completely destabilized.

Evaluation: Based on the situation, I believe Mr. Nabin must take two urgent corrective steps to clear the conflict. First, he must change his behavioral approach and treat every single employee with impartial dignity and fairness under the principle of equity. Second, he needs to conduct a thorough job analysis to redistribute responsibilities evenly across departments so that no team feels exploited or left out. Embracing these steps will replace internal friction with unity, ultimately restoring a healthy and productive work environment.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Abesh is an officer-in-charge of the production department. Being directed by the Managing Director, he executes all the activities of the production department and remains accountable for it. With the aim of increasing production, he wants to appoint a supervisor. His expectation is that the appointed supervisor will be skilled in using machinery and capable of explaining work practically (hands-on) to the workers.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is decentralization?
questions.answer: Decentralization refers to the systematic and permanent delegation of decision-making authority down to the lower levels of an organizational hierarchy.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Explain the principle of unity of command.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: The principle of unity of command states that an employee should receive orders from only one direct superior and should be held accountable to that single superior alone.

Explanation: According to Henri Fayol, dual subordination must be avoided because if an employee receives conflicting commands from multiple bosses, it creates confusion, indiscipline, and division of responsibility. Adhering to this principle ensures operational clarity, preserves workplace discipline, and makes it easier to measure individual performance accurately.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which level of manager is Mr. Abesh in the stem? Describe.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: In the stem, Mr. Abesh is a "Mid-level Manager" (মধ্যবর্তী স্তরের ব্যবস্থাপক).

Explanation: Mid-level management acts as a bridge between top leadership (which formulates policies and strategies) and lower-level operational management (which executes field tasks). Roles like department heads, divisional managers, and officers-in-charge fall into this category. Their primary duties involve interpreting top-level plans, transmitting directives downward, coordinating operational activities, and maintaining accountability to upper executives.

Application to Stem: According to the stem, Mr. Abesh is the officer-in-charge of the production department. He receives structural directions directly from the Managing Director (top management), executes those departmental plans, and remains formally accountable to him. Furthermore, he possesses the authority to plan the recruitment of field supervisors (lower management) to optimize manufacturing, which confirms his position as a mid-level manager.

questions.label: d
questions.text: What type of skill do you think the supervisor should possess to fulfill Mr. Abesh's expectation in the stem? Analyze with arguments.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: To fulfill Mr. Abesh's expectations, the supervisor must possess strong "Technical Skill" (কারিগরি দক্ষতা).

Explanation: Technical skill refers to the specialized knowledge, hands-on proficiency, and practical expertise required to utilize specific tools, machinery, operating procedures, and technical methodologies effectively. This attribute is paramount for lower-level operational managers or supervisors, as they directly guide field labor and oversee day-to-day physical production tasks.

Application to Stem: In the stem, Mr. Abesh intends to appoint a supervisor with specific technical capabilities. His precise expectations are that the new hire must be highly proficient in operating manufacturing machinery and must possess the clarity to explain tasks to ground workers through practical, hands-on demonstrations. These demands directly match the definition of technical skill.

Evaluation: Based on the analysis, technical skill is the ultimate prerequisite for the supervisor's role. While mid and top-tier managers rely heavily on conceptual or human skills, a field-level supervisor cannot ensure quality control or prevent machine accidents without direct mechanical expertise. Therefore, possessing strong technical skills will enable the supervisor to successfully satisfy Mr. Abesh's operational goals and drive up factory productivity.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Suman is the owner of a large company. He determines the goals and objectives of his organization. He divides his organization into separate departments according to the nature and type of work. He appoints an expert in each department.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is unity of command?
questions.answer: Unity of command means that an employee should receive orders and instructions from only one direct superior at a time.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by technical skill?
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Technical skill refers to the specialized knowledge, hands-on proficiency, and expertise required to use specific tools, machinery, techniques, and operational procedures to perform tasks effectively.

Explanation: This skill is highly essential for lower-level management, supervisors, and operational field workers who directly oversee production. Having technical skill ensures that machinery is handled correctly, resource wastage is minimized, and the physical output meets the precise quality standards of the organization.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which principle of management has Mr. Suman followed? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: Mr. Suman has followed the fundamental "Principle of Division of Work" (কার্যবিভাজন বা শ্রমবিভাগ নীতি) of management.

Explanation: The principle of division of work states that the total work of an organization should be divided into small, specific parts or departments based on its nature, and each part should be assigned to an individual or group according to their specialized qualifications and capacity. This system ensures that tasks are handled systematically, leading to specialization, increased accuracy, and heightened operational speed.

Application to Stem: In the stem, Mr. Suman systematically breaks down the vast operations of his large corporate enterprise into separate, specialized departments based on the exact nature and characteristics of the tasks. Furthermore, he ensures efficiency by placing a domain-specific expert in charge of each distinct department. This strategic organizational layout directly matches the application of the division of work principle.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Do you think the principle followed by Mr. Suman will be profitable for the organization? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: Yes, I strongly believe that the principle of division of work followed by Mr. Suman will prove highly profitable and beneficial for his organization.

Explanation: Implementing the division of work directly yields the benefits of specialization. When a particular task is handled repeatedly by a dedicated department run by field experts, human errors drop drastically, production cycles accelerate, and organizational resources are utilized at their maximum capacity. This directly cuts structural overhead expenses and boosts the firm's competitive edge.

Application to Stem: According to the stem, Mr. Suman's company is large-scale, where operational chaos can easily manifest without structural boundaries. By departmentalizing operations and putting dedicated specialists at the helm, he establishes an environment where tasks are performed error-free and with high professional precision.

Evaluation: Based on this structural assessment, dividing work leads to a profound increase in both labor and mechanical productivity. Since higher productivity and minimized resource wastage are the direct precursors to high financial returns, Mr. Suman's strategy is perfectly aligned with maximizing corporate profits. Therefore, it can be concluded that this principle will act as a major catalyst in driving his large company toward immense financial success.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Robiul is a manager of Priya Tiles Company. He is quite caring towards his subordinates and looks at everyone with the same eye. The subordinates also respect him. As a result, the mutual relationship among them is very strong. He stays aware of the needs and grievances of the employees and tries to resolve them considering the human side. One day, when a trouble arose among the subordinates regarding work, he resolved it quickly through his experience and sharp intelligence.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a profession?
questions.answer: A profession refers to a specialized occupation that requires advanced academic training, specific intellectual skills, and adherence to a strict professional code of conduct.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by unity of command?
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Unity of command means that an employee or subordinate should receive orders and instructions from only one direct superior or boss at a time.

Explanation: According to Henri Fayol, if a worker is subjected to orders from more than one superior, it creates confusion, overlapping authorities, and conflicts in execution. Adhering strictly to this principle prevents the evasion of responsibility, ensures seamless discipline, and maintains stable organizational relationships.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which principle of management has Mr. Robiul followed? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: Mr. Robiul has followed the fundamental "Principle of Equity" (সাম্যের নীতি) of management.

Explanation: The principle of equity states that a manager must treat all subordinates with justice, fairness, and equal affection, completely avoiding any personal bias, favoritism, or discrimination. When employees feel they are treated equally and justly, their loyalty, trust, and dedication to the organization increase dramatically.

Application to Stem: In the stem, it is clearly highlighted that Mr. Robiul is highly empathetic toward his team and looks at all his subordinates with the exact same eye. This completely unbiased treatment has built deep respect and a rock-solid mutual relationship within the workplace, which matches the core application of the principle of equity.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Is Mr. Robiul an ideal manager in the light of the stem? Give arguments in support of your answer.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: Yes, in the light of the stem, Mr. Robiul can undoubtedly be considered an "Ideal Manager" (আদর্শ ব্যবস্থাপক).

Explanation: An ideal manager possesses a blend of strong human, conceptual, and interpersonal skills. Such a leader doesn't just look at numbers but understands human behavior, maintains absolute equity, proactively addresses employee grievances, and possesses the decisive problem-solving intelligence required to neutralize unexpected workplace conflicts smoothly.

Application to Stem: According to the stem, Mr. Robiul perfectly exhibits these qualities. He runs his team based on fairness, keeps a continuous check on employee needs from a human perspective, and maintains high morale. Furthermore, when an operational crisis or conflict broke out among the staff regarding work, he instantly resolved it by leveraging his vast field experience and sharp analytical intellect.

Evaluation: Based on this comprehensive behavior, Mr. Robiul fulfills all standard benchmarks of an effective leader. He successfully balances employee satisfaction with swift, intelligent conflict resolution, ensuring that organizational operations remain dynamic and completely undisturbed. Therefore, it is completely justified to declare him an ideal manager.

fullCQData.stem: Navana Spinning Limited is a large business organization located in Comilla. There are 450 workers working in the organization. Dividing all the activities of the organization into various departments and sub-departments according to their characteristics, the responsibility and authority have been given to one skilled person for each department and sub-department.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Who is the father of modern management?
questions.answer: Henri Fayol is the father of modern management.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Explain the principle of scalar chain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: The principle of scalar chain implies that the line of authority and communication should extend in a continuous, unbroken chain from the highest rank to the lowest rank within an organization.

Explanation: According to Henri Fayol, this principle ensures that every executive and subordinate knows exactly who to report to and who to receive orders from, eliminating organizational communication gaps. While it maintains strict order, in absolute emergencies where hierarchical delays could cause loss, peers can communicate directly across lines using a temporary shortcut called a "gang-plank" with prior notification to superiors.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which principle of management has been followed in Navana Spinning Limited? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The fundamental "Principle of Division of Work" (কার্যবিভাজন বা শ্রমবিভাগ নীতি) has been followed in Navana Spinning Limited.

Explanation: The principle of division of work dictates that the total workload of an corporate enterprise should be divided into smaller, specific operational segments or departments based on specialization. Instead of forcing one person to execute everything, assigning distinct functions to individual employees based on their specialized skills allows them to build speed, eliminate operational errors, and attain maximum efficiency over time.

Application to Stem: In the stem, Navana Spinning Limited is described as a large-scale enterprise with a workforce of 450 employees. To run operations systematically, management logically divided all corporate tasks into specialized departments and sub-departments based on their core traits. Furthermore, they placed a highly skilled individual in charge of each specific division, which perfectly demonstrates the execution of the division of work principle.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Do you agree that the organization in the stem will get more benefits in various fields as a result of following that principle of management? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: Yes, I completely agree with the statement that Navana Spinning Limited will attain greater operational advantages across multiple sectors by implementing the principle of division of work.

Explanation: For any large-scale industrial firm, structural specialization is the cornerstone of commercial growth. Breaking down complex industrial functions into department-specific tasks leads to a massive reduction in operational time, prevents the duplication of effort, and minimizes resource wastage. This structural discipline ensures that production quality remains uniformly high while decreasing overhead expenses.

Application to Stem: According to the stem, Navana Spinning Limited operates on a massive scale with 450 workers. By organizing the workforce into dedicated, expertly managed units, the enterprise creates an optimized environment where assignments are executed smoothly without chaos, ensuring high-quality yarn output and stable processing cycles.

Evaluation: Based on this strategic arrangement, the division of work transforms ordinary industrial labor into specialized assets, resulting in a dramatic increase in collective productivity. Since high structural productivity and optimized operational speed are the absolute precursors to maximizing corporate profitability, the company will gain immense competitive advantages in the textiles market. Therefore, it is entirely rational to conclude that following this principle will bring comprehensive long-term success to Navana Spinning Limited.

fullCQData.stem: In a factory, under two supervisors 'A' and 'B', 10 workers each are engaged in the same type of work in two separate rooms. The instructions given by both supervisors regarding overtime matters of the workers were not the same. Although the rate of remuneration was identical, the working hours of the workers in the two rooms were different. As a result, dissatisfaction arises among the workers.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Who is the father of scientific management?
questions.answer: Frederick Winslow (F.W.) Taylor is the father of scientific management.

questions.label: b
questions.text: "Decentralization increases the importance of subordinates"—Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Decentralization increases the importance of subordinates because it transfers decision-making authority and operational autonomy from the top management down to lower-level executives.

Explanation: When authority is decentralized, lower-level employees no longer have to wait constantly for higher approvals to execute day-to-day operations. This trust boosts their self-confidence, enhances operational skills, and fosters a sense of organizational belonging. As their decision-making power expands, their accountability and overall role within the organizational structure become highly vital.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which principle of management have the supervisors in the stem violated? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The supervisors in the stem have violated the "Principle of Equity" (সাম্যের নীতি) and the "Principle of Unity of Direction" (নির্দেশনার ঐক্য নীতি). Note: The root cause of the operational breakdown is a clear violation of Equity due to unequal treatment for identical work profiles.

Explanation: The principle of equity dictates that all subordinates performing similar job profiles under similar conditions must be treated with equal justice, fairness, and uniform standards. If workers performing the same category of labor are subjected to varying hours or uncoordinated rules, it breeds extreme resentment and fractures factory harmony.

Application to Stem: In the stem, two identical groups of 10 workers are executing the exact same type of manufacturing tasks in two different rooms under supervisors 'A' and 'B'. However, due to poor coordination, the supervisors issued conflicting rules regarding overtime, forcing one room to clock different hours than the other for the same rate of pay. This blatant disparity in managing working hours for an identical job represents a text-book violation of administrative equity.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Recommend necessary measures to remove the dissatisfaction among the workers in the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: To eliminate the widespread dissatisfaction among the factory workers, the company management must immediately establish a Uniform Service Rule/Policy and enforce the Principle of Equity across all departments.

Explanation: Dissatisfaction in an industrial setup can only be cleared when workers perceive absolute transparency, institutional fairness, and a lack of arbitrary supervision. Management must step in to override conflicting supervisory orders by standardizing work schedules, overtime calculations, and reward systems across all operational floors.

Application to Stem: According to the stem, the workers became highly dissatisfied because supervisors 'A' and 'B' acted autonomously without a unified policy, causing room-wise discrimination in working hours. To fix this, management must design an identical, clear blueprint for overtime and operational hours that applies uniformly to both supervisor-led teams.

Evaluation: Based on these observations, creating a centralized, balanced work protocol is the only logical remedy to end workplace friction. By formalizing identical working hours for identical job descriptions, the factory will restore horizontal equity and eliminate the root cause of employee anger. Enforcing these systematic steps will successfully defuse the current crisis, boost labor morale, and re-establish standard industrial productivity.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Mokbul Hosain Bhuiyan is the Chief Executive Officer of Al Hera Group. He personally makes critical decisions regarding employee selection, promotion, and salary. However, minor decisions related to production are taken through supervisors and workers. He is always vigilant to tackle competitor organizations and keep future profits continuous.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a management principle?
questions.answer: A management principle refers to a fundamental truth or general guideline that serves as a basis for managerial decision-making and action.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Explain the relationship between management and administration.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Management and administration are deeply interconnected, where administration is the highest policy-making level and management is the operational level that executes those policies.

Explanation: Administration handles the conceptual thinking aspect of an enterprise, determining the broad organizational objectives, long-term plans, and corporate regulations. On the other hand, management represents the active, hands-on mechanism that uses resources to implement the goals laid down by the administration, meaning administration decides what is to be done and management figures out how to do it.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which principle of management has been highlighted in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The principle of management highlighted in the stem is the "Principle of Centralization and Decentralization" (কেন্দ্রীকরণ ও বিকেন্দ্রীকরণ নীতি).

Explanation: Centralization refers to retention or concentration of decision-making authority at the top management levels, whereas decentralization means distributing authority down to lower tiers. An ideal management system avoids extreme absolute setups and instead strikes an optimal balance between the two, keeping core strategic choices centralized while delegating routine operational matters.

Application to Stem: In the stem, Mr. Mokbul Hosain retains absolute authority over core structural matters like hiring workers, approving promotions, and establishing salary scales. Simultaneously, he delegates secondary, floor-level production matters directly to supervisors and ground workers. This deliberate operational division directly mirrors the principle of balancing centralization with decentralization.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Mr. Mokbul Hosain Bhuiyan possesses the quality of foresight—Evaluate the statement in the light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The statement that Mr. Mokbul Hosain Bhuiyan possesses the vital managerial quality of "Foresight" (দূরদর্শিতা) is entirely accurate and logical.

Explanation: Foresight is a manager's cognitive ability to look ahead, accurately read shifting market dynamics, anticipate future challenges, and formulate proactive strategies. For a top-tier executive like a CEO, foresight is critical to shield an enterprise from unexpected external economic shocks and ensure long-term, sustainable commercial viability.

Application to Stem: The stem explicitly notes that Mr. Mokbul is consistently vigilant and active regarding market competition. Instead of reacting defensively after a loss, his sharp strategic focus ensures the firm handles rival forces smoothly and maintains uninterrupted profitability in the upcoming years, showing he plans ahead using solid trends.

Evaluation: Based on the scenario, it can be concluded that his forward-thinking mindset is what keeps Al Hera Group structurally stable. By keeping his focus free from micro-managing floor operations and directing his attention toward market trends and macro threats, he safeguards the corporate future of the conglomerate. Therefore, the statement perfectly encapsulates his leadership profile as a visionary, high-performing executive.

fullCQData.stem: The eleventh-grade students of the Business Studies department of 'Kha-Gha' College applied to the Principal to go on an educational tour. The Principal decided when, where, how, and at what cost they would go, and determined the subscription amount. Later, he divided the tasks into several parts and assigned the responsibilities to different teachers. Then, a senior teacher was given overall responsibility and authority. Since everyone performed their duties correctly, the educational tour was successful. The students are happy about it.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a principle?
questions.answer: A principle is a fundamental truth or an established rule of action that serves as a guide to thought or conduct.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is management called a social process?
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Management is called a social process because it is entirely centered around directing, organizing, and developing human relationships within a society to achieve specific goals.

Explanation: Society consists of people, and management cannot exist without people. It involves mobilizing human resources, fostering collective teamwork, and satisfying various social stakeholders. Since managers must continuously interact with individuals, guide their behaviors, and fulfill social responsibilities, the entire mechanism functions as an integral part of an ongoing social system.

questions.label: c
questions.text: With which function of management is the work done first by the Principal consistent? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The work done first by the Principal is completely consistent with "Planning" (পরিকল্পনা), which is the primary function of management.

Explanation: Planning is the process of deciding in advance what is to be done, when it is to be done, how, and by whom. It acts as a blueprint for future courses of action by setting objectives and choosing the best alternatives. In any organized activity, planning serves as the foundational step upon which all other subsequent management functions are built.

Application to Stem: In the stem, when the students applied for the educational tour, the Principal proactively determined the essential details of the trip. He explicitly fixed the venue (where), the time (when), the methodology of execution (how), and calculated the necessary financial subscription. Because he mapped out the entire blueprint of the tour beforehand, his initial steps directly represent the planning function.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the rationale behind the subsequent activities of the Principal that played a role in making the study tour successful, in light of the functions of management.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The subsequent activities of the Principal represent the functions of "Organizing" (সংগঠন) and "Delegation of Authority" (দায়িত্ব ও ক্ষমতা অর্পণ), which are highly rational and critical for institutional success.

Explanation: Organizing is the managerial function that involves identifying and grouping the work to be performed, defining and delegating responsibility and authority, and establishing relationships to enable people to work together effectively. Without organizing, a plan cannot be executed because tasks remain unassigned and authority lines remain blurred, leading to operational chaos and failure.

Application to Stem: After completing the planning phase, the Principal divided the tour tasks into several manageable parts and systematically distributed those responsibilities among various teachers. Furthermore, to ensure seamless execution, he appointed a senior teacher, vesting him with the overall authority and power to supervise the entire arrangement. This structured division and clear line of command directly align with organizing and staffing frameworks.

Evaluation: Based on the analysis, the subsequent steps taken by the Principal were the absolute turning point that converted a mental plan into a practical success. By distributing specific tasks to different teachers, he eliminated individual overburdens and ensured specialization. Granting formal authority to a senior leader created a focused command center, allowing everyone to execute their roles flawlessly and ensuring a highly successful, conflict-free tour.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Maruf is a supervisor of a manufacturing project. For his work, he has to be accountable to more than one manager. Due to being accountable to multiple managers at the same time, the dynamism of his work is hampered. He is thinking of informing the higher authority about the matter.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Where was Henri Fayol born?
questions.answer: Henri Fayol was born in Istanbul, Turkey.

questions.label: b
questions.text: "Management is a separate branch of knowledge"—Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Management is considered a separate branch of knowledge because it possesses its own distinct set of systematic principles, specialized theories, concepts, and structural methodologies that can be studied and applied professionally.

Explanation: Over the last century, management has evolved away from a simple instinctual activity into an independent academic and professional discipline. Today, universities worldwide offer specialized degrees (such as BBA and MBA) and dedicated research streams focusing entirely on planning, organizational behavior, and strategic management, establishing its status as a self-contained, structured body of science.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which level of management responsibility does Mr. Maruf perform? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: Mr. Maruf performs the duties and responsibilities of "Lower-level Management" (নিম্ন স্তরের বা মাঠ পর্যায়ের ব্যবস্থাপনা).

Explanation: Lower-level management, also known as operational or supervisory management, forms the bottom tier of the managerial hierarchy. It includes field positions such as supervisors, foremen, and line inspectors who directly oversee the physical work execution of non-managerial laborers and transmit ground-floor updates to mid-level departmental heads.

Application to Stem: The stem explicitly states that Mr. Maruf works directly as a field supervisor within a manufacturing project. Because his structural role involves working on the production floor directly overseeing daily mechanical output and answering directly to higher-level functional managers, his profile perfectly aligns with operational lower-level management.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Which principle of management is being violated, causing problems in Mr. Maruf's work? Evaluate it in the light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The core principle of management being violated in Mr. Maruf's work is the "Principle of Unity of Command" (আদেশের ঐক্য নীতি).

Explanation: The principle of unity of command, formulated by Henri Fayol, dictates that an employee or subordinate must receive operational orders and tasks from only one direct supervisor at any given time and should be accountable to that single superior alone. If this layout is broken, it causes a state of dual subordination where conflicting instructions paralyze worker actions.

Application to Stem: According to the stem, Mr. Maruf is forced to remain accountable to more than one manager simultaneously for his daily assignments. This multi-headed authority model naturally leads to operational overlap, task confusion, and a severe breakdown in execution, which has directly hampered the speed and overall dynamism of his manufacturing project.

Evaluation: Based on this scenario, the total absence of unity of command is the primary source of Mr. Maruf's systemic workplace frustration. When a subordinate faces multiple bosses, it becomes impossible to prioritize assignments fairly, leading to severe delays and a total loss of industrial momentum. Therefore, the higher authority must urgently intervene to fix this structural defect, establish a single clear line of command, and restore standard operational workflow.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Salam is a Managing Director of an organization. Considering the type and characteristics of the organization, he divides the organizational tasks into various segments and distributes the responsibilities and duties among the workers according to their qualifications and skills. In addition, he pays special attention to establishing an appropriate salary and wage structure along with other facilities for the workers of his organization, which will be capable of fulfilling the needs of the employees. As a result, the workers' attention to the organizational tasks increases and the organization achieves extensive success.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Write the full form of CEO.
questions.answer: The full form of CEO is Chief Executive Officer.

questions.label: b
questions.text: "Employee selection is a negative process"—Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Employee selection is called a negative process because it involves screening, sorting, and rejecting unqualified candidates from the total pool of applicants to find the most suitable ones.

Explanation: Unlike recruitment, which is a positive process designed to attract as many applicants as possible to apply for a job, selection aims to eliminate those who do not meet the precise institutional benchmarks. Since the number of rejected candidates is almost always higher than the number of selected individuals, the overall nature of the selection phase is considered structurally negative.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which principle of management was followed first in the organization of the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The principle of management followed first by Mr. Salam in his organization is the "Principle of Division of Work" (কার্যবিভাজন বা শ্রমবিভাগ নীতি).

Explanation: The principle of division of work dictates that the entire task of an enterprise should not be forced onto a single individual; instead, it should be divided into small, distinct, specialized parts and assigned to different employees based on their academic capacity, practical experience, and natural skills. This method fosters job specialization, cuts down operational execution times, and elevates overall labor productivity.

Application to Stem: In the stem, Mr. Salam meticulously assesses the functional nature and structural goals of his enterprise. He systematically breaks down the collective workload into specialized divisions and assigns those targeted responsibilities to specific field workers based on their personal efficiency and eligibility, which perfectly showcases the execution of the division of work principle.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "The principle of management implemented by Mr. Salam through his subsequent work played a primary role in increasing both organizational success and employee attention."—Evaluate the validity of this statement in the context of the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The principle of management implemented by Mr. Salam through his subsequent work is the "Principle of Fair Remuneration" (উপার্জন বা ন্যায্য পারিশ্রমিক নীতি), and the statement evaluating its impact is completely valid.

Explanation: The principle of fair remuneration states that employees must be compensated with reasonable, competitive, and adequate monetary rewards (salaries, wages, and financial perks) that match their efforts, satisfy their living needs, and stay within the financial capacity of the employer. When labor is compensated justly, it acts as a massive psychological catalyst that eliminates frustration and boosts organizational loyalty.

Application to Stem: According to the stem, Mr. Salam does not restrict his role merely to distributing duties; he takes strategic steps to design an optimized wage and salary structure along with welfare incentives targeted at completely satisfying his workers' standard needs. This direct financial care captures the attention of his entire workforce, turning their professional dedication into a powerful asset for the enterprise.

Evaluation: Based on this case, the subsequent implementation of fair compensation served as the main engine behind the firm's widespread market success. While dividing work structures a business, it is the fair reward system that keeps workers deeply motivated to give their maximum output without slacking. By aligning employee satisfaction with corporate goals, Mr. Salam effectively built a high-performance work culture, making the statement an absolute truth.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Nazmul Hosen is a successful production manager of a manufacturing enterprise. He treats all the employees of the organization equally. He takes necessary steps to make the workers efficient by training them on the specific work methods, techniques, and machinery required to increase production. As a result, everyone is satisfied and pays full attention to their work.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Who is the father of scientific management?
questions.answer: Frederick Winslow (F.W.) Taylor is the father of scientific management.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why should dual subordination be avoided in an organization?
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Dual subordination should be avoided because receiving orders from multiple superiors simultaneously destroys organizational discipline, creates operational confusion, and dilutes accountability.

Explanation: According to Henri Fayol's principle of unity of command, an employee must be accountable to only one direct superior. When an employee is subjected to dual subordination, it becomes impossible to prioritize tasks, leading to personal conflicts between managers and allowing the subordinate to easily evade responsibility for poor work performance.

questions.label: c
questions.text: The behavior shown by Mr. Nazmul Hosen toward the workers in the first part of the stem falls under which principle of Henri Fayol? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The behavior shown by Mr. Nazmul Hosen toward the factory workers in the first part of the stem falls under the fundamental "Principle of Equity" (সাম্যের নীতি) of management.

Explanation: The principle of equity dictates that a manager must look at all subordinates with the same eye, ensuring absolute justice, fairness, and equal affection while completely avoiding personal bias or favoritism. Implementing equity in the workplace establishes trust, minimizes inner friction, and motivates employees to dedicate themselves fully to corporate targets.

Application to Stem: In the stem, Mr. Nazmul Hosen maintains a highly balanced and just workplace culture by treating every single worker in his production department equally. Because he refuses to practice discrimination or show favoritism, his team remains completely satisfied, highly loyal, and deeply focused on their manufacturing duties, which directly reflects the principle of equity.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the role of the managerial skills developed by Mr. Nazmul Hosen's initiatives in achieving the organizational goals of the manufacturing enterprise mentioned in the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The initiatives taken by Mr. Nazmul Hosen aim to develop the "Technical Skills" (কারিগরি দক্ষতা) of the workers, which plays a primary role in achieving corporate goals.

Explanation: Technical skill refers to the specialized knowledge, practical proficiency, and hands-on capability required to operate specific machinery, execute precise methodologies, and handle tools safely on the production floor. For a manufacturing enterprise, enhancing the technical expertise of the workforce is vital to boost operational speed, eliminate product defects, and maximize output.

Application to Stem: According to the stem, Mr. Nazmul Hosen actively identifies and introduces optimized work methods, processing techniques, and machine handling procedures to train his workers. By systematically upgrading their practical execution abilities, he removes operational inefficiencies and fosters an environment where workers confidently focus on minimizing manufacturing errors.

Evaluation: Based on this structural analysis, developing solid technical capability acts as the primary driver for industrial growth. While strategic planning sets the direction, a factory cannot hit its output targets if the ground-floor laborers lack mechanical expertise. By aligning employee skill development with modern production requirements, Mr. Nazmul Hosen ensures high-quality manufacturing, making his initiatives completely indispensable for achieving the enterprise's long-term goals.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Azwad is a marketing manager of Korotoa Limited. He behaves well with his subordinate employees and, after properly evaluating their completed work, pays them fair wages and salaries in due time. Recently, when extreme chaos arose among the employees over an unexpected matter, Mr. Azwad resolved it by utilizing his experience, honesty, and wisdom.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Who are managers?
questions.answer: Managers are individuals in an organization who are responsible for directing, coordinating, and overseeing the work of others to achieve institutional goals.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Can management be called a continuous process? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Yes, management can undoubtedly be called a continuous process because its core activities run systematically and perpetually as long as the organization exists.

Explanation: The managerial process operates as an unbroken cycle, shifting sequentially from planning to organizing, staffing, directing, motivating, coordinating, and controlling. Once a specific institutional goal is attained, controlling feeds new data back into a fresh planning phase, ensuring that the operational wheel never comes to a standstill.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which principle of Henri Fayol is reflected in the activities of the marketing manager in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Point: The activities of the marketing manager, Mr. Azwad, reflect Henri Fayol's fundamental "Principle of Fair Remuneration" (ন্যায্য পারিশ্রমিক নীতি) and the "Principle of Equity" (সাম্যের নীতি). Note: The timely evaluation and payment structure primarily highlights Fair Remuneration.

Explanation: The principle of fair remuneration states that employees must receive adequate, just, and satisfactory compensation for their services to keep them motivated.
