Therefore, it can be concluded that the success of Nura Soft Drinks Ltd. stems from their flawless identification of planning premises. By aligning their corporate blueprint with the cultural and religious realities of the target market, they ensured the perfect feasibility of their plan, validating that proper assessment of premises is the cornerstone of organizational success.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Abbas exports tea to different countries. He adopted a plan to increase his production volume by 25%. For this purpose, he undertook a program to establish several new tea gardens in Sylhet and Thakurgaon districts. If this program is implemented, he will be able to tackle competition and export his produced tea to various countries even after the next 10 years.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a budget?
questions.answer: A budget is the numerical or quantitative expression of a plan compiled for a specific future period.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is flexibility necessary in planning? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Flexibility is necessary in planning so that the plan can be adapted or modified to align with unexpected changes in the future business environment.

Explanation: The future is inherently uncertain and dynamic. If a plan is made too rigidly, it may completely fail when confronted with sudden economic shifts, changes in consumer tastes, or new government regulations. Incorporating flexibility ensures that the organization can change its operational strategy or paths smoothly without abandoning its ultimate goal, thereby reducing risks and preventing financial loss.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on nature, what type of plan is the one adopted by Mr. Abbas in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

Based on nature, the plan adopted by Mr. Abbas is a 'Strategic Plan' (or Long-term Plan).

Explanation Paragraph:

A strategic plan is a comprehensive, long-term blueprint designed by top-level management to achieve competitive advantages and overall institutional goals in a highly competitive market. These plans usually cover a period exceeding five years, involve massive capital investment, and focus directly on handling environmental threats and industrial competition.

Application Paragraph:

In the stem, Mr. Abbas establishes a major expansion roadmap to boost tea production by 25% by setting up entirely new tea plantations in Sylhet and Thakurgaon. His vision is explicitly long-term, aiming to tackle international market competition and maintain export dominance even after the next 10 years. Because this plan involves structural expansion, high-level investment, and a decade-long strategic outlook to fight competition, it perfectly reflects a strategic plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "The program undertaken by Mr. Abbas will help him survive in the international market"—Analyze the validity of the statement in the light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

The statement "The program undertaken by Mr. Abbas will help him survive in the international market" is entirely logical and valid.

Explanation Paragraph:

In management, a 'Program' is a type of single-use strategic plan that includes a concrete combination of goals, policies, procedures, rules, and steps required to execute a massive, non-recurring project. For a business targeting global trade, a well-structured production program ensures economies of scale, enhances quality control, lowers production costs, and secures supply stability—all of which are vital to surviving against international competitors.

Application Paragraph:

According to the stem, Mr. Abbas targets a substantial 25% production increase. To turn this vision into reality, he designs a specialized program to establish new plantations across two prominent districts (Sylhet and Thakurgaon). This systematic expansion ensures he will have a massive raw material base. By executing this comprehensive program, his supply capacity becomes robust enough to reliably satisfy international buyers and counter market rivalries over the next 10 years.

Conclusion Paragraph:

Therefore, it can be concluded that the program serves as the tactical execution vehicle for his long-term strategic vision. By expanding physical infrastructure and scaling up production capacity, it gives him the structural strength needed to hold a strong position in the global supply chain, fully validating the statement.

fullCQData.stem: ABC School and College made a plan to take its students on an educational tour. Because the institution formulated the plan without gathering and analyzing any kind of information, they faced chaos and disorder when they went on the tour following that predetermined plan.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Which is the first step in formulating a plan?
questions.answer: The first step in formulating a plan is the analysis of future opportunities or identifying objectives.

questions.label: b
questions.text: "Planning is the foundation of other functions of management"—Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Planning is called the foundation of other management functions because all subsequent functions—organizing, staffing, directing, motivating, coordinating, and controlling—are designed and executed based on the guidelines set by the plan.

Explanation: Planning is the advanced blueprint of future actions. Without a clearly defined plan, an organization has no direction, meaning structures cannot be organized, personnel cannot be properly deployed, and directives cannot be issued. Just as a building cannot stand without a solid foundation, the success and structural efficiency of every other managerial activity depend entirely on the clarity and accuracy of the core plan.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on nature, what type of plan is the one adopted in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

Based on nature, the plan adopted by the institution in the stem is a 'Single-use Plan' (specifically, a Program or a Project).

Explanation Paragraph:

A single-use plan is a non-recurring blueprint designed to achieve a specific, unique objective or handle a one-time event that is unlikely to be repeated in the exact same format. Once the specific event or task is concluded, the relevance of that plan ends, and a completely fresh plan must be drafted for any future event.

Application Paragraph:

In the stem, ABC School and College formulated a tailored plan exclusively for conducting an educational tour for its current batch of students. An educational tour is a unique, seasonal, or occasional event with non-repetitive logistical demands (such as specific dates, destinations, and participant numbers). Since this layout was engineered solely to guide a single, temporary event, it fits perfectly under the characteristics of a single-use plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Which matter in the stem do you think was not followed, resulting in the chaotic situation? Analyze.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

I believe the chaotic situation arose because the institution failed to follow the essential step of 'Information Gathering and Analysis' (or evaluating environmental facts) during the planning process.

Explanation Paragraph:

A successful plan relies heavily on standard steps: identifying opportunities, gathering/analyzing relevant data, establishing planning premises, and choosing the best alternative. Gathering accurate data regarding costs, routes, safety, and accommodation forms the structural backbone of real-world planning. Skipping data collection turns a plan into mere guesswork, causing structural failure during execution.

Application Paragraph:

In the stem, the authorities of ABC School and College drafted their educational tour blueprint blindly, without collecting or analyzing any preliminary information. They failed to investigate field realities, local conditions, transport availability, or structural constraints. Consequently, when they attempted to execute this blind, predetermined plan, their assumptions collapsed under real-world variables, directly resulting in severe operational chaos and disorder during the trip.

Conclusion Paragraph:

Therefore, it can be concluded that a plan cannot succeed in isolation from facts. By entirely neglecting the phase of data analysis and environmental study, the institution developed an unrealistic framework. This vital omission stripped the organization of its foresight, proving that skipping foundational planning steps inevitably invites managerial failure.

fullCQData.stem: Jahid is an officer in the sales department of an organization. He adopted some plans for expanding the product market and uses that plan repeatedly. Due to this, he gets many benefits.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is planning?
questions.answer: Planning is the detailed blueprint or advance determination of what is to be done, how, when, and by whom to achieve specific organizational objectives.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by flexibility?
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Flexibility in planning refers to the capability or scope to adjust, alter, or modify a plan to match unexpected changes in the business environment without shifting from the main goal.

Explanation: Because the future is uncertain, a rigid framework can cause an organization to fail when facing external shifts like changes in government policy, economic conditions, or customer tastes. A flexible plan acts as a dynamic roadmap, allowing managers to switch operational strategies smoothly when unforeseen problems arise, minimizing operational risks and financial losses.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of plan has been adopted in the organization of the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

The type of plan adopted in the organization of the stem is a 'Standing Plan'.

Explanation Paragraph:

A standing plan is a permanent or long-term plan designed by management to guide recurring, identical organizational situations or activities. Once formulated, it remains in use indefinitely, saving time and effort, until a major environmental or structural shift requires it to be altered or replaced. Classic examples include policies, procedures, methods, and rules.

Application Paragraph:

In the stem, Jahid is a sales officer who designed strategic plans focused on market expansion. Instead of discarding this blueprint after a single run, he uses this specific plan repeatedly to streamline ongoing promotional campaigns and sales cycles. Since the plan serves as a continuous, reusable framework for handling recurring market expansion tasks, it fits the description of a standing plan perfectly.

questions.label: d
questions.text: What benefits do you think Jahid can get in the stem? Give an answer with arguments.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

Jahid can achieve significant managerial and operational benefits, primarily including time savings, cost reductions, quick decision-making, and increased consistency across his sales activities.

Explanation Paragraph:

Using a standing plan provides distinct operational advantages to a department. Because the core framework is already tested and established, managers do not have to spend extra energy drafting a fresh strategy every time a routine situation arises. This consistency reduces administrative confusion, simplifies employee training, improves coordination, and allows the workforce to execute tasks with high efficiency and fewer errors.

Application Paragraph:

In the stem, Jahid benefits directly by applying his market expansion layout repeatedly. Because the plan acts as a standard guide, he avoids the stress of researching and designing a new sales strategy for every individual campaign. This allows him to respond quickly to market changes, keep his sales team aligned, and maximize resource utilization, which explains why the stem notes that "he gets many benefits."

Conclusion Paragraph:

Therefore, it can be concluded that adopting a standing plan provides structural stability and operational momentum to Jahid's department. By eliminating repetitive planning tasks, it enables him to focus his attention on field execution and performance monitoring, proving that reusable plans are an effective tool for boosting organizational efficiency.

fullCQData.stem: The Principal of X College made a plan to take the students on an educational tour to Paharpur. In this regard, a committee was formed under the leadership of a senior teacher. After returning from the educational tour, many complained that due to a shortage of time, they could not see the place properly.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a goal?
questions.answer: A goal is the concrete, desired end result or target that an organization directs its efforts and activities to achieve.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by a standing plan?
questions.answer:
Knowledge: A standing plan is a permanent or ongoing plan designed by management to be used repeatedly to guide routine, recurring situations or activities within an organization.

Explanation: Once a standing plan is formulated, it remains effective indefinitely over a long period. It eliminates the need to create a fresh blueprint every time a predictable situation occurs, thereby streamlining decision-making and saving time. Examples of standing plans include company policies, standard operating procedures, methods, and formal rules.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Due to a deficiency in which step of the planning process in the stem were the students unable to see the place properly? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

The students were unable to see the place properly due to a deficiency in the 'Time Allocation' or 'Scheduling' step within the tactical planning process (specifically, failing to design an accurate schedule/itinerary).

Explanation Paragraph:

A schedule or itinerary is a time-bound operational planning step that details exactly when each activity will begin and end. In any project or event plan, allocating realistic time frames for travel, rest, and exploration is critical. If management fails to accurately estimate transit times or neglects to factor in local constraints during the scheduling phase, the plan collapses during execution, resulting in rushed operations and unfulfilled targets.

Application Paragraph:

In the stem, X College's administration planned a long-distance educational tour to Paharpur and formed an execution committee. However, upon returning, a major complaint arose: the students could not tour the site properly because of time constraints. This proves that while the overarching goal was clear, the committee failed to formulate a realistic, detailed time budget or daily itinerary. They underallocated time for actual sightseeing and over-allocated or lost track of travel/transit time.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Express your opinion on how to smoothly implement the said plan.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

To smoothly implement a localized single-use plan like an educational tour, the committee must prioritize thorough 'Information Gathering', accurate 'Planning Premises' assessment, and a comprehensive 'Itinerary/Time Schedule'.

Explanation Paragraph:

The implementation phase of any single-use plan requires deep operational foresight. For an outdoor event involving a large group, organizers cannot rely on assumptions. They must actively collect preliminary data regarding road conditions, opening hours, local security, and exact geographical distances. Translating these data points into a well-coordinated schedule ensures a balanced and flexible operation on the ground.

Application Paragraph:

In this case, the committee of X College should have gathered detailed info about Paharpur beforehand. To ensure smooth execution, they should have constructed a written, hour-by-hour itinerary that leaves buffer room for unexpected delays (like traffic jams). Furthermore, breaking down tasks among committee members—such as assigning specific teachers to monitor food, transportation, and student guiding—would prevent chaotic time management during the visit.

Conclusion Paragraph:

Therefore, it is my opinion that successful plan execution depends entirely on structural detailing. If the committee had combined rigorous information collection with clear division of labor and a realistic time allocation model, the implementation would have been flawless, allowing the students to enjoy a meaningful educational experience without any stressful time shortages.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Jamal is the Human Resource Manager of an organization. He formulates separate and distinct plans for the tasks of his department. At the end of the work, he designs a new plan again. On the other hand, Mr. Belal is the Production Manager of the said organization. He formulates a plan for everyone's use with the objective of solving the recurring problems of his department. This reduces his workload.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a vision?
questions.answer: A vision is a clear, inspiring, and long-term ultimate dream or future destination that an organization strives to achieve over time.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is flexibility necessary in planning? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Flexibility is necessary in planning so that the operational course of action can be easily adjusted or modified to match sudden changes in the business environment.

Explanation: The future business environment is highly volatile and uncertain due to unpredictable economic, political, or market demand shifts. If a plan is constructed with a rigid framework, it completely breaks down when real-world conditions change. Introducing flexibility serves as an operational buffer, allowing managers to dynamically tweak procedural steps without sacrificing the ultimate goal, thereby preventing unnecessary wastage of resources and administrative failure.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of plan is the one adopted by Mr. Jamal? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

The plan adopted by Mr. Jamal is a 'Single-use Plan'.

Explanation Paragraph:

A single-use plan is a non-recurring corporate blueprint designed to guide a unique, specific, or one-time activity that is not expected to happen repeatedly in the exact same manner. Once the designated task or project concludes, the plan loses its functional relevance, and management must formulate a brand-new plan for subsequent distinct activities. Programs, budgets, and specific projects are key examples.

Application Paragraph:

In the stem, Mr. Jamal, the Human Resource Manager, designs separate and distinct plans tailored strictly for individual seasonal tasks within his department. The stem clearly states that as soon as a project or task ends, he starts completely fresh by designing a new plan again. Because his operational frameworks are systematically consumed and retired after a single implementation phase, his strategy aligns perfectly with the features of a single-use plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: In terms of the principle of time and cost reduction, whose plan in the stem is better? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

In terms of the principle of time and cost reduction, Mr. Belal's plan is significantly better and more efficient than Mr. Jamal's plan.

Explanation Paragraph:

The principles of time and cost reduction dictate that managerial frameworks should minimize administrative repetition and operational overhead. Standing plans (such as policies or standardized methods) are pre-formulated to handle recurring institutional issues. Because they serve as permanent, reusable guidelines, they completely eliminate the need to expend fresh energy, time, and intellectual capital on routine problem-solving, making them highly economical.

Application Paragraph:

According to the stem, Mr. Belal formulated a recurring plan designed for collective use to solve ongoing, repetitive technical issues in the production department. This structural setup directly minimized his administrative workload. Conversely, Mr. Jamal keeps drafting entirely new, distinct single-use plans for every task, which naturally consumes excessive administrative hours, increases planning costs, and demands continuous cognitive effort for routine management functions.

Conclusion Paragraph:

Therefore, it can be concluded that Mr. Belal’s approach aligns flawlessly with the principles of cost and time optimization. By designing a reusable standing plan for routine issues, he saved substantial organizational hours and administrative expenses. Thus, his planning approach is undeniably superior for driving operational economy within the enterprise.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Adil is the MD of a large-scale company. He directed to formulate separate plans for production, purchase, sales, and all other departments of the organization. However, due to a lack of proper coordination and linkage among these plans, the desired targets were not achieved. Therefore, to attain the determined goals of the organization, he emphasized coordinating the plans adopted at different levels with the main objective.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a budget?
questions.answer: A budget is the numerical or quantitative expression of a plan compiled for a specific future period.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by the premises of planning? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Planning premises refer to the expected environment, conditions, boundaries, and assumptions under which a plan is expected to operate and be executed.

Explanation: Before formulating any plan, management must analyze and make assumptions about future internal and external conditions (such as government policy, economic trends, customer demand, and availability of raw materials). These assumptions serve as the background or foundation for the blueprint. If the planning premises are incorrect or poorly evaluated, the final plan becomes unrealistic and fails during practical implementation.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on the organizational structure, what type of plan is made in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

Based on the organizational structure, the plans made in the stem are 'Departmental Plans' (which fall under Operational/Tactical Planning).

Explanation Paragraph:

When plans are classified based on organizational structure or division, they are divided into master plans, departmental plans, and sectional plans. A departmental plan is a localized, operational blueprint designed independently for a specific functional unit (such as sales, production, or finance) to streamline the internal activities of that particular department.

Application Paragraph:

In the stem, Mr. Adil, the Managing Director, orders the creation of separate, distinct action plans for the production, purchase, sales, and other individual units of his large-scale corporation. Because these blueprints are created separately for each specific functional department rather than the entire enterprise as a single unit, they are classified precisely as departmental plans.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Is it justified to link the plans adopted at different levels in the light of the main objective in the stem? Give arguments based on the characteristics of planning.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

Yes, it is entirely logical and justified to link the plans adopted at different levels in the light of the main objective, reflecting the 'Unity of Planning' characteristic.

Explanation Paragraph:

Unity is a fundamental characteristic of planning, which states that all minor, departmental, and sectional plans within an organization must support and merge into one primary overarching master plan. An enterprise cannot succeed if its sub-units work in isolation. Every small target achieved by a single department must act as a stepping stone toward fulfilling the central goal of the entire organization.

Application Paragraph:

In the stem, the company initially faced failure because the departmental plans for production, purchase, and sales were running independently without any coordination or linkage. Because they lacked unity, their separate efforts did not help the business reach its collective goal. Recognizing this flaw, Mr. Adil rightly shifted focus to aligning and linking all multi-level sub-plans with the main core objective of the firm.

Conclusion Paragraph:

Therefore, it can be concluded that coordinating separate plans under a singular organizational objective is a mandatory requirement for success. Without integrating these different layers of planning, structural chaos occurs and resources are wasted. Mr. Adil's corrective action perfectly demonstrates the principle of unity, making his decision completely justified.

fullCQData.stem: The government has decided to establish the Mirsharai Industrial Zone within the next 5 years with the aim of national economic development and employment generation. The factors currently considered to design the industrial zone plan may not always remain the same. Therefore, the authorities predetermine alternative steps, including modifications to the plan, if future conditions change. As a result, it is expected that the establishment of the industrial zone will be completed within the specified timeframe.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a program?
questions.answer: A program is a major, single-use operational plan that outlines a comprehensive mix of targets, policies, procedures, rules, and steps necessary to execute a large-scale project or mission.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is strategic planning important in an industrial enterprise? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Strategic planning is important in an industrial enterprise because it enables the organization to survive and maintain a long-term competitive advantage in a dynamic, highly volatile market environment.

Explanation: Industrial enterprises involve massive financial investment, extensive infrastructure, and exposure to external environmental shifts like technological advancements, regulatory changes, or market globalizations. Strategic planning is designed by top-level management to systematically analyze organizational strengths and market threats. By formulating a broad, macro-level long-term vision, it ensures that resources are allocated sustainably to face industrial competition effectively.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on time, what type of plan is the one formulated for establishing the Mirsharai Industrial Zone? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

Based on time/duration, the plan formulated for establishing the Mirsharai Industrial Zone is a 'Mid-term Plan'.

Explanation Paragraph:

In management, planning is classified by duration into short-term (one year or less), mid-term (more than one year up to a maximum of five years), and long-term (exceeding five years). Mid-term plans are designed to turn long-term visions into realistic operational achievements by offering a balanced timeline for physical infrastructure development and funding setup.

Application Paragraph:

In the stem, the government outlines a concrete timeline to establish the Mirsharai Industrial Zone "within the next 5 years" to boost economic growth and create jobs. Since the development cycle and targeted operational deadline are bound explicitly to a five-year horizon, this infrastructural blueprint falls directly into the category of a mid-term plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the characteristic of planning that accommodates changes in future conditions in the light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

The characteristic of planning reflected in accommodating changing future conditions in the stem is 'Flexibility'.

Explanation Paragraph:

Flexibility is the unique attribute of a well-designed plan that allows it to be adjusted, modified, or re-engineered smoothly when confronted with unexpected changes in internal or external operational environments. Because the future is inherently uncertain, a rigid corporate blueprint can collapse when economic variables shift. Designing dynamic alternative steps ensures that the organization remains resilient and path-corrected toward achieving its foundational goal.

Application Paragraph:

According to the stem, the authorities acknowledge that the socio-economic and industrial factors evaluated during the current phase may fluctuate over the five-year period. Rather than relying on a fixed, unalterable strategy, they actively predetermine contingency actions and alternative operational paths to handle future variables. This built-in adaptability guarantees that structural work keeps progressing smoothly even if unexpected challenges hit the project.

Conclusion Paragraph:

Therefore, it can be evaluated that incorporating flexibility is a critical prerequisite for the successful execution of complex industrial projects. By proactively embedding alternative courses of action into the core layout, the authorities have insulated the project from external disruptions, proving that flexibility is the defining feature that ensures tasks are completed perfectly on time.

fullCQData.stem: Ashok Karmakar, the Human Resource Manager of Superstar Plastic Company, has formulated a new policy for employee promotion. According to the policy, promotion will be given solely based on merit. No feedback or opinion from the employees was taken while formulating this policy. Because only merit was considered, the employees expressed their dissatisfaction regarding the policy. The senior employees have decided to inform their higher authorities about their grievance.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a goal?
questions.answer: A goal is the specific, expected ultimate result or target that an organization directs its efforts and activities to achieve.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is planning called the foundation of other management functions? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Planning is called the foundation of other management functions because all subsequent functions—organizing, staffing, directing, motivating, coordinating, and controlling—are structurally dependent on the guidelines and objectives set by the plan.

Explanation: Planning provides the baseline roadmap for an organization. Without a clear plan, there is no structural direction to build a department, assign responsibilities, hire workforce, or issue managerial directives. Because no executive action can be initiated or measured without a predetermined blueprint, every management function rests entirely on planning as its structural base.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on nature, what type of plan is the one formulated by Ashok Karmakar? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

Based on its nature, the plan formulated by Ashok Karmakar is a 'Policy' (which is a type of Standing Plan).

Explanation Paragraph:

A policy is a broad, permanent guidelines designed by management to channel executive decision-making and handle repetitive or recurring situations uniformly across the enterprise. Since it serves as a continuous, long-term framework for organizational behavior, it falls precisely under the classification of standing plans.

Application Paragraph:

In the stem, the Human Resource Manager establishes a standard, long-term regulation dictating that all future employee promotions will be decided strictly based on individual merit. This administrative framework is not a single-use project; rather, it is a continuous blueprint meant to guide all upcoming promotion cycles uniformly. Therefore, this long-term operational guideline represents a formal organizational policy.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "The absence of a characteristic of an ideal plan is the cause of employee dissatisfaction"—Explain the justification of this statement in the light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

The statement is completely justified; the specific characteristic missing from the plan is 'Acceptability' (or failure to consider the human element and ensure employee participation).

Explanation Paragraph:

An ideal plan must possess the characteristic of acceptability or democratic participation, meaning it should consider the feelings, interests, and practical insights of the subordinates who will execute or be affected by it. When management formulates a highly sensitive internal plan unilaterally without taking feedback from its workforce, it lacks internal legitimacy. This omission breaches trust, alienates experienced personnel, and generates strong workplace resistance.

Application Paragraph:

According to the stem, Ashok Karmakar drafted a major structural change regarding employee advancement but completely ignored the opinions and feedback of the staff. By basing the entire policy solely on raw merit, he completely marginalized the value of long-term loyalty and seniority. This arbitrary execution style naturally made the senior workforce feel insecure and unvalued, directly sparking widespread grievance and pushing them to report their dissatisfaction to top executives.

Conclusion Paragraph:

Therefore, it can be concluded that a plan cannot be considered ideal if it ignores stakeholder psychology. While a merit-based system is theoretically rational, executing it without democratic consultation stripped the policy of its institutional acceptability. This foundational oversight is precisely what transformed a logical management tool into a source of industrial friction, fully validating the statement.

fullCQData.stem: Jalalabad Steel manufactures iron sheets using imported raw materials. Due to the establishment of more steel mills in the country, they are facing stiff competition. The production manager believes that they can survive in the market by reducing production costs and lowering the selling price. Therefore, it is necessary to bring some changes to the decision. Finally, a plan is adopted to manufacture iron sheets using local raw materials instead.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a budget?
questions.answer: A budget is the numerical or quantitative expression of an operational plan compiled for a specific future period.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is planning called a thoughtful/intellectual process? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Planning is called a thoughtful or intellectual process because it requires creative thinking, deep mental reflection, foresight, and sound judgment rather than physical labor.

Explanation: Planning involves deciding in advance what is to be done, how, when, and by whom to achieve specific organizational objectives. It is entirely a cognitive exercise where a manager must analyze past data, evaluate current resources, and predict future opportunities or risks to choose the best alternative course of action. Because it demands high intellectual capacity and imaginative vision to map out a future blueprint, it is considered a mental or thoughtful process.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on the organizational structure, what type of plan is the one in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

Based on the organizational structure, the plan in the stem is a 'Departmental Plan' (which falls under operational or tactical planning).

Explanation Paragraph:

When plans are classified based on organizational structure, structure-wise subdivisions, or divisions, they are divided into master plans, departmental plans, and sectional plans. A departmental plan is a localized blueprint designed specifically for a single functional unit or department (such as production, marketing, or finance) to handle its internal activities and support the broad targets of the firm.

Application Paragraph:

In the stem, the initiative to change the manufacturing strategy is driven by the insights of the production manager. The focus of the plan is strictly on altering operational parameters—specifically changing the source of raw materials from foreign imports to domestic supplies to reduce manufacturing costs. Because this plan centers on the operational activities of the production department rather than rewriting the entire corporate charter, it represents a departmental plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Do you think the characteristic of flexibility is present in the plan of the stem? Analyze in the light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

Yes, the characteristic of flexibility is distinctly present in the plan adopted by Jalalabad Steel in the stem.

Explanation Paragraph:

Flexibility is the unique attribute of an ideal plan that allows it to be adjusted, modified, or altered dynamically when confronted with unexpected changes in the internal or external business environments without abandoning the core objective. Since the future is uncertain and competitive market landscapes shift rapidly, incorporating alternative paths or adjustments prevents a plan from breaking down, reducing risks and institutional losses.

Application Paragraph:

In the provided stem, Jalalabad Steel initially operated on a fixed plan of manufacturing iron sheets using imported raw materials. However, the external market environment changed due to an increase in local competitors, creating intense rivalry. Instead of rigidly sticking to their original strategy which was causing sustainability issues, the management evaluated the production manager's feedback and altered their core operational decision, smoothly shifting to domestic raw materials to lower production costs and match competitive market pricing.

Conclusion Paragraph:

Therefore, it can be concluded that the dynamic adjustment made by the management highlights the principle of flexibility. By altering their raw material sourcing strategy to adapt to a surge in market competition, they successfully protected their core objective of corporate survival, proving that the plan contains strong features of flexibility.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Ariful Islam's enterprise sells wheat in different divisional cities of the country. Currently, in consultation with his appointed sales manager, he formulates a plan to increase sales by 20%. In addition, he takes comprehensive preparations for long-term success in a highly competitive market.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a planning horizon?
questions.answer: A planning horizon refers to the specific future period of time over which a plan is intended to be implemented, monitored, and evaluated.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is planning called a thoughtful/intellectual process?
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Planning is called a thoughtful or intellectual process because it requires creative thinking, deep mental reflection, foresight, and analytical judgment rather than physical labor.

Explanation: Planning involves deciding in advance what is to be done, how it is to be done, when it is to be done, and who is to do it. It is entirely a cognitive exercise where a manager analyzes past data, evaluates current organizational capabilities, and predicts future market conditions to select the best alternative course of action. Because it demands high intellectual capacity and imaginative vision to map out a future blueprint, it is considered a mental or thoughtful process.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on nature, what type of plan did Mr. Ariful Islam adopt initially? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

Based on nature, the plan initially adopted by Mr. Ariful Islam is an 'Operational Plan' (specifically, a short-term target or tactical program).

Explanation Paragraph:

In management, plans classified by nature include strategic, tactical, and operational plans. Operational plans focus on the execution of specific, routine targets within functional departments (such as sales or production) to achieve short-term milestones. These plans are concrete, numerical, and usually bound to a short time frame.

Application Paragraph:

In the stem, Mr. Ariful Islam collaborates directly with his functional department head—the sales manager. Together, they design a sharp, localized target to boost wheat sales volume by exactly 20%. Because this initial framework addresses immediate, tactical sales performance rather than changing the entire corporate charter, it is classified as an operational plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Will the subsequent preparation taken by Mr. Ariful Islam help him realize his objectives? Analyze its effectiveness by mentioning the type of planning in the light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge Paragraph:

Yes, the subsequent preparation taken by Mr. Ariful Islam will definitely help him realize his objectives, and this type of broad, long-term preparation is known as 'Strategic Planning'.

Explanation Paragraph:

Strategic planning is a comprehensive, long-term blueprint designed by top-level management to tackle intense industrial competition, mitigate external market risks, and ensure corporate sustainability. It involves assessing macro-environmental threats and opportunities to establish a lasting competitive advantage. Without a robust strategy, short-term operational targets fail to sustain an enterprise against market rivalries.

Application Paragraph:

According to the stem, Mr. Ariful Islam does not stop at a short-term 20% sales increase target. Recognizing the volatility of his market, he takes comprehensive, long-term preparations to ensure the institutional success of his wheat business amid fierce competition. This dynamic preparation aims to strengthen market position, manage long-term supply chains, and build brand equity, which directly reflects a strategic plan.

Conclusion Paragraph:

Therefore, it can be concluded that his strategic preparation serves as the foundational shield for his enterprise. While his initial operational plan drives short-term sales growth, it is the strategic plan that ensures the organization's survival and growth over the years, making his decision highly effective and indispensable for ultimate success.

fullCQData.stem: The villagers of Rasulpur made a plan to construct a new mosque building. After starting the construction work, the price of construction materials in the market suddenly increased. As a result, it was not possible to construct the building according to the previous plan, so the villagers made necessary changes and corrections to the adopted plan. Currently, they are very optimistic.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Where is the birthplace of Henri Fayol?
questions.answer: Henri Fayol was born in Istanbul, Turkey.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by the principle of unity of command?
questions.answer:
Unity of command is a fundamental principle of management which dictates that an employee should receive orders and be accountable to only one direct superior.

The main purpose of this principle is to avoid confusion, overlapping of authority, and dual subordination in the workplace. If a subordinate receives orders from more than one supervisor simultaneously, it creates conflicts, reduces work efficiency, and makes it difficult to fix responsibility. Therefore, to ensure disciplined operations, a single clear line of command must flow from a single boss to a subordinate.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of planning, in terms of nature, is the plan adopted by the villagers of Rasulpur in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
The planning adopted by the villagers of Rasulpur in the stem is a flexible planning (নমনীয় পরিকল্পনা) in terms of nature.

In management, flexibility is a core characteristic of a sound plan. Flexible planning refers to a plan that contains the scope to be altered, modified, or adjusted smoothly in response to unexpected changes in the internal or external environment without causing major disruption or loss.

In the stem, the villagers initially designed a fixed plan to construct a mosque. However, an unexpected rise in the market prices of construction materials halted their progress. Instead of abandoning the project, the villagers adapted to the new situation by modifying and correcting their original plan. Since their plan allowed for adjustment in response to changing economic realities, it perfectly exemplifies the nature of a flexible plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the justification in light of the stem regarding the characteristic of a good plan that has made the villagers of Rasulpur optimistic.
questions.answer:
The characteristic of a good plan that has made the villagers of Rasulpur optimistic is "Flexibility" (নমনীয়তা), and its justification is highly valid in this context.

Flexibility in planning means the capability to adapt to changing circumstances. A rigid plan often fails when faced with unforeseen environmental or economic challenges, whereas a flexible plan allows decision-makers to alter strategies and resource allocations to ensure the ultimate goal is achieved despite obstacles.

According to the stem, the sudden price hike of construction materials threatened to derail the mosque construction under the original layout. Had the plan been rigid, the project would have collapsed. However, because the villagers incorporated flexibility, they were able to make necessary revisions and corrections to match their budget and resources, keeping the project alive.

Therefore, it can be concluded that the flexibility of the plan is what restored the villagers' confidence and made them optimistic about the successful completion of the mosque. Without this adaptive characteristic, the project would have met a dead end, proving that flexibility is indeed the savior of their collective dream.

fullCQData.stem: Chittagong College Alumni Association plans a pleasure trip to Sandwip via speedboat. When the picnic bus reaches the Sitakunda boat terminal on the scheduled date, speedboat communication between Sandwip and Chittagong becomes disconnected due to bad weather. In such a situation, the picnic is instantly and successfully completed at the Eco-Park in Sitakunda.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is planning?
questions.answer: Planning is the advance determination of what is to be done, how it is to be done, when it is to be done, and who is to do it to achieve a specific goal.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Establish the relationship between purpose and objective.
questions.answer:
Purpose and objective are closely related as hierarchical elements of planning, where purpose represents the fundamental, broad mission of an organization, while objectives are the specific, measurable targets set to fulfill that purpose.

While purpose provides the ultimate direction and long-term reason for an organization's existence, objectives break down this broad purpose into concrete, time-bound milestones. For example, a company's purpose might be to provide affordable healthcare, and its objective for the current year could be to build three new clinics. Therefore, objectives serve as tactical steps directly designed to realize the overarching purpose.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of planning by nature is the pleasure trip plan described in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
The pleasure trip plan described in the stem is a Single-use plan (একাত্মক পরিকল্পনা) in terms of its nature.

A single-use plan is a specific course of action designed to accomplish a unique, non-recurring goal or event. Once the specific event or project is completed or the situation passes, the relevance of that particular plan ends, and it is not repeated in the exact same format in the future.

In the stem, the Chittagong College Alumni Association formulated a plan specifically for a one-time pleasure trip to Sandwip. This excursion is a unique event rather than a continuous, day-to-day operational activity. Since the plan was mapped out solely for this specific occasion, it falls perfectly under the category of a single-use plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the successful completion of the picnic at the Eco-park described in the stem in light of the special characteristics of a good plan.
questions.answer:
The successful completion of the picnic at the Sitakunda Eco-park highlights the special characteristic of "Flexibility" (নমনীয়তা) in a good plan, making the execution highly justified and commendable.

Flexibility in planning refers to the built-in capability to adapt, alter, or choose an alternative course of action smoothly when unexpected changes or environmental constraints disrupt the original course of action.

According to the stem, the original single-use plan to visit Sandwip via speedboat was completely disrupted by sudden hostile weather conditions. Instead of rigidly sticking to an impossible plan or canceling the entire event in frustration, the organizers instantly shifted their location to the Sitakunda Eco-park. This immediate adjustment saved the event from failing entirely and ensured a satisfying outcome for the participants.

Therefore, it can be evaluated that the quick shift to the Eco-park demonstrates the dynamic nature of a good plan. The success of the picnic proves that a realistic plan must not be rigid; rather, it should possess the elasticity to cope with unforeseen crises, ensuring that the ultimate broader goal of recreation is fulfilled.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Shahadat, the executive officer of Shampa Limited, planned to export 400 metric tons of tea in 2024. Due to not receiving minimum wages and benefits, some workers left for Dubai in the middle of the year. After a few days, some more senior and skilled workers retired. As a result, the desired production was disrupted, and due to the inability to export tea according to the orders, the organization had to face huge losses, and the image of the organization was also damaged.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is forecasting?
questions.answer: Forecasting is the process of making predictions or estimating future trends and conditions based on past and present data.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is flexibility necessary in planning?
questions.answer:
Flexibility is necessary in planning to ensure that an organization can successfully adapt its strategies and operations to unexpected changes in the business environment.

The business environment is highly dynamic and unpredictable, involving sudden shifts in market demand, economic conditions, or resource availability. If a plan is too rigid, it fails when conditions change, leading to operational losses. Incorporating flexibility allows managers to adjust the plan smoothly without abandoning the ultimate organizational objectives, thereby minimizing risks and ensuring survival.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which step of planning formulation was not properly followed in the stem, causing the organization to face huge losses? Explain.
questions.answer:
The step of planning formulation that was not properly followed in the stem is "Analyzing premises/Determining planning premises" (পরিকল্পনার উপাদান বা পটভূমি মূল্যায়ন/পূর্বাবস্থা নির্ধারণ).

Planning premises refer to the anticipated future environment, conditions, and assumptions under which a plan is expected to operate. Analyzing both internal factors (such as labor availability, satisfaction, and retirement schedules) and external conditions is a crucial primary step in formulation to identify potential obstacles before execution.

In the stem, Mr. Shahadat set an ambitious export target of 400 metric tons of tea without evaluating his internal labor conditions. He failed to account for existing worker dissatisfaction regarding minimum wages, nor did he assess the upcoming retirement schedule of his key skilled personnel. Because this environmental and internal scanning step was completely neglected, the unexpected labor shortage derailed production, proving that a failure to properly establish planning premises led directly to the company's financial catastrophe.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze what Mr. Shahadat should do to restore the image of Shampa Limited in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
To restore the tarnished image of Shampa Limited, Mr. Shahadat needs to focus immediately on the management function of "Corrective action through controlling and human resource planning" (নিয়ন্ত্রণ ও মানবসম্পদ পরিকল্পনার মাধ্যমে সংশোধনমূলক ব্যবস্থা গ্রহণ).

A damaged corporate image due to supply failure can only be recovered by stabilizing internal operations and building a dependable workforce. Since the crisis emerged from labor shortages and dissatisfaction, immediate revisions must be integrated into the company's strategic planning framework to prevent future operational gaps.

In light of the stem, Mr. Shahadat must implement the following systematic points:

১. Re-evaluating the Compensation Structure: He should immediately adjust and guarantee standard minimum wages and facilities to retain existing workers and stop the migration trend.

২. Strategic Human Resource Forecasting: He needs to design a continuous recruitment and training program to systematically replace retired skilled workers ahead of time.

৩. Introducing Flexible Backup Plans: A contingent or flexible production plan should be drafted to keep operations running even during minor internal crises.

Therefore, it can be analyzed that by addressing the root cause—worker dissatisfaction and lack of foresight—through effective workforce planning and corrective management, Mr. Shahadat can rebuild production capacity. Consistently fulfilling future export orders on time is the only definitive way to win back client trust and successfully restore the company's market reputation.

fullCQData.stem: Students at the Higher Secondary level of Karnaphuli College are not achieving good results in the board examinations. An investigation report formed on this matter reveals that the 1st paper is taught in the first year and the 2nd paper is taught in the second year at the college. Mainly due to this reason, students are not getting good results in the first paper. The Principal calls an academic meeting regarding this issue. In consultation with everyone, it is decided that from now on, both papers will be taught in each year.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is division of work?
questions.answer: Division of work is the management principle of distributing the total work of an organization among individuals or departments based on their qualifications, skills, and specialization.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is the objective crucial in formulating a plan?
questions.answer:
The objective is crucial in formulating a plan because it acts as the primary target, ultimate goal, and directional guide for all future activities of an organization.

Without a clear objective, planning becomes directionless and loses its purpose, making it impossible to coordinate efforts or measure progress. It serves as the foundation upon which strategies, policies, and operational schedules are built, ensuring that all organizational resources are efficiently utilized toward a specific destination.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of planning is the new decision taken in the academic committee meeting based on the organizational structure? Explain.
questions.answer:
The new decision adopted in the academic committee meeting is an Operational plan (কার্যকরী বা পরিচালনামূলক পরিকল্পনা) based on the organizational structure.

Based on organizational structure and levels of management, operational planning is designed by lower-level or mid-level tactical bodies to execute the daily or academic operations of a specific segment or department of an institution, aligning with its broader institutional goals.

In the stem, the academic meeting called by the Principal analyzed a specific, operational bottleneck: the distribution of paper teaching across academic terms. The decision to teach both papers simultaneously across both academic years is aimed directly at improving immediate student performance. Since this plan dictates the structural and routine execution of specific class curricula, it falls squarely under operational planning.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "A special managerial skill helps the Principal arrive at the right decision"—Evaluate the justification of this statement.
questions.answer:
The special managerial skill that helped the Principal arrive at the right decision is "Conceptual Skill" (ধারণাগত বা দূরদর্শী যোগ্যতা), and the statement is entirely justified.

Conceptual skill refers to the mental ability of a top-level manager to visualize the organization as a whole, understand how its various parts depend on one another, and anticipate how a change in one section will impact the entire system.

In light of the stem, the Principal demonstrated high conceptual skill by calling an academic meeting and probing deep into the root causes of poor board exam results. Instead of placing superficial blame, he correctly diagnosed that a systemic mismatch in curriculum distribution (teaching papers in isolation across different years) was harming the students' long-term retention and academic success.

Therefore, it can be evaluated that by comprehensively analyzing the problem and consulting stakeholders to balance the curriculum, the Principal's conceptual skill guided him to an optimal structural decision. This administrative foresight will directly resolve the academic deficiency, proving that such higher-level managerial insight is indispensable for effective organizational problem-solving.

fullCQData.stem: Bengal Traders is a large industrial enterprise. They have production factories in Sirajganj, Bogra, and Natore. A total of 650 employees work in the factories. The goods produced by the enterprise are sold at wholesale prices through its own showroom in Sirajganj. There are 20 employees to manage the responsibilities of this showroom. To expand the company's activities, the Board of Directors decided to set up one showroom each in Kushtia, Naogaon, and Pabna. In addition, the enterprise has also decided to increase production in their factories.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a short-term plan?
questions.answer: A short-term plan is an operational plan formulated for a duration of one year or less to achieve immediate operational goals.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is the cost lower in standing plans?
questions.answer:
The cost is lower in standing plans because they are formulated once and used repeatedly over a long period to handle recurring situations, eliminating the need for continuous research and design expenses.

Since standing plans—such as organizational policies, procedures, and rules—serve as permanent guidelines, managers do not need to spend time, effort, or financial resources to create new plans every time a routine situation arises. This repetitive application establishes operational consistency and significantly minimizes administrative and decision-making costs over time.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which factors do you think should be considered in the decision-making process of Bengal Traders? Explain.
questions.answer:
In the decision-making process regarding the expansion and production increase of Bengal Traders, "Planning Premises" (পরিকল্পনার উপাদান বা পটভূমি) and relevant environmental factors must be comprehensively considered.

Planning premises involve assessing the future internal and external environmental conditions under which the decisions will be executed. For a large-scale expansion, making choices without analyzing resource limitations, market demands, and infrastructural feasibility can lead to strategic failure.

To ensure successful execution, Bengal Traders must specifically evaluate the following key points:

১. Market Demand Assessment: They must analyze whether there is sufficient customer demand for their wholesale products in Kushtia, Naogaon, and Pabna.

২. Financial and Supply Chain Capacity: The enterprise needs to evaluate if they have the capital required for new showroom setups and if their factories can consistently supply goods.

৩. Human Resource Availability: They must estimate the additional workforce needed to manage the expanded factory production and run the three new showrooms smoothly.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the justification of the steps taken by Bengal Traders.
questions.answer:
The structural steps taken by Bengal Traders to expand their business and increase production capacity are highly justified and strategically sound for long-term growth.

In management, business expansion and increasing production are vital proactive strategies to capitalize on economies of scale, maximize market share, and increase profitability. However, for these steps to be successful, they must align logically with the organization's existing capabilities and structural capacity.

According to the stem, Bengal Traders is already operating successfully across multiple districts (Sirajganj, Bogra, Natore) with a large workforce of 650 factory workers. Their decision to open new outlets in Kushtia, Naogaon, and Pabna is a logical step forward because expanding their distribution network creates a direct channel to tap into new regional consumer bases.

Furthermore, setting up new showrooms naturally increases sales volume, which perfectly justifies their parallel decision to boost production in their existing factories to meet the impending growth in demand. By balancing market presence with supply capability, Bengal Traders demonstrates sound operational synchronization, making their overall expansion project highly realistic, timely, and justified.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Rakesh is the owner of a soap manufacturing industrial enterprise. He plans to increase soap production by 50% over the next five years. Considering various alternatives for the increased production, he adopts short-term plans.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a single-use plan?
questions.answer: A single-use plan is a specific plan designed to achieve a unique, non-recurring goal or event, which loses its utility once that objective is met.

questions.label: b
questions.text: "Planning is a mental process"—Why?
questions.answer:
Planning is considered a mental process because it involves intellectual thinking, foresight, imagination, and sound judgment rather than physical labor.

Before executing any action, a manager must mentally analyze future opportunities and risks, evaluate alternative courses of action, and make conscious decisions. It requires high cognitive ability to predict market trends and calculate consequences in advance, which is why it is purely a thinking and reflective activity.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on the duration, what type of plan has Mr. Rakesh made in the stem to increase production? Explain.
questions.answer:
Based on the duration mentioned for the main objective, Mr. Rakesh has formulated a Long-term plan (দীর্ঘমেয়াদি পরিকল্পনা) to increase production.

In management, plans are classified based on time into short, mid, and long-term. A plan made for a period exceeding five years (or five years and above) to achieve core strategic goals is termed a long-term plan.

In the stem, Mr. Rakesh establishes a primary corporate objective: increasing his factory's total soap production by 50% over the next "five years." Since this target spans a five-year horizon, the overarching framework is fundamentally a long-term plan. (Note: The short-term plans he later takes are tactical sub-components designed to fulfill this major long-term timeline).

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the justification of the step that Mr. Rakesh gave priority to while formulating the plan to increase production in the stem.
questions.answer:
The step that Mr. Rakesh gave priority to while formulating his plan is "Identification and evaluation of alternative courses of action" (বিকল্প কার্যপন্থা উদ্ভাবন ও মূল্যায়ন), and his approach is highly justified.

A successful planning process requires generating multiple strategic paths to reach a goal and evaluating them against cost, feasibility, and risk. Choosing a direction without examining options increases the probability of operational failure, whereas exploring alternatives ensures optimized resource allocation.

According to the stem, Mr. Rakesh wants to boost soap output by 50%. To make this massive growth realistic, he didn't blindly follow a single method; instead, he carefully considered various alternative production methods and market scenarios, which ultimately guided him to break down the large target into manageable short-term operational plans.

Therefore, it can be concluded that Mr. Rakesh's emphasis on exploring and analyzing alternative pathways is completely logical and standard. This analytical prioritization bridges the gap between his high five-year ambition and practical, day-to-day manufacturing execution, ensuring the expansion project remains sustainable and risk-mitigated.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Reza set up an electronic goods retail business in his hometown. Without considering the income and demand of the local people, he planned to sell high-quality, high-priced laptops, computers, and refrigerators, and purchased products accordingly. But as sales were not up to expectations, out of frustration, he engaged in specific thinking and again planned to sell low-priced televisions, refrigerators, irons, etc., and decorated his business business by purchasing them. As a result, the buyers of the town were able to purchase products according to their capacity and demand, and Mr. Reza also became profitable in the business.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is planning?
questions.answer: Planning is the pre-determined outline of future courses of action to be taken in order to achieve specific organizational objectives.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by a standing plan?
questions.answer:
A standing plan refers to a permanent, continuous guideline formulated by management to address recurring, routine situations within an organization over a long period.

Once designed, these plans remain effective and are applied repeatedly whenever similar operational conditions arise, until they become obsolete or require policy updates. Examples of standing plans include organizational policies, standard operating procedures (SOPs), and strict disciplinary rules, all of which help maintain structural consistency and reduce routine decision-making costs.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Due to which characteristic of planning could Mr. Reza not be profitable in business initially in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
In the stem, Mr. Reza initially failed to be profitable because his first plan lacked the vital characteristic of "Realism or Feasibility" (বাস্তবমুখীতা).

A primary characteristic of a sound plan is that it must be realistic and practical, meaning it should be formulated based on solid facts, market data, and environmental feasibility rather than guesswork. An idealistic or disconnected plan that ignores external environmental variables—such as consumer purchasing power, local demographics, and actual market demand—is bound to collapse during execution.

According to the stem, Mr. Reza launched his venture in a small hometown but completely ignored the local economic landscape. He stocked high-priced, premium electronics without assessing whether the local residents possessed the necessary income levels or purchasing interest to buy them. Because his initial plan lacked a realistic foundation and failed to match the practical socioeconomic environment of his target location, his sales crashed, leading directly to initial business losses.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the logical justification behind Mr. Reza's failure in the first phase and business success in the second phase of profitability.
questions.answer:
The clear logical justification behind Mr. Reza's initial failure and subsequent profitability lies in the transition from an unrealistic framework to a "Flexible and Market-oriented Plan" (নমনীয় ও বাজারভিত্তিক পরিকল্পনা).

In management, business success depends heavily on alignment with market conditions and the agility to implement corrective actions. A rigid stance on an impractical strategy guarantees bankruptcy, whereas identifying structural flaws and pivoting toward consumer-centric parameters ensures organizational sustainability.

To thoroughly analyze his progression across both phases, the following points explain the outcomes:

১. First Phase Failure (Lack of Environmental Scanning): Mr. Reza's initial failure was a direct result of ignoring his target market's purchasing power. By pricing his stock beyond the financial reach of local consumers, he created an immediate mismatch between product supply and consumer affordability.

২. Second Phase Turnaround (Adopting Flexibility): Instead of shutting down operations after his initial losses, Mr. Reza demonstrated dynamic management by evaluating his mistakes. He restructured his inventory based on actual consumer capacity, replacing premium goods with affordable, essential home appliances.

৩. Achieving Strategic Realism: By adjusting his product mix to match the local purchasing capacity and daily utility needs, his second planning phase became highly realistic. This alignment unlocked customer purchasing power, leading to rapid sales volumes and financial recovery.

Therefore, it can be concluded that the contrast between his two phases is a textbook example of planning theory. His first phase failed due to speculative rigidity, while his second phase succeeded because he successfully matched consumer capability with organizational supply. This logical corrective shift saved his enterprise and converted an imminent loss into a sustainable, highly profitable business model.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Naif works as the marketing manager of Nova Sweater Ltd. He is highly visionary. Keeping in mind what measures can bring good results in any emerging situation, he formulates plans. This kind of skill smoothens the path to achieving his desired goals. Since all decisions of the enterprise have to be given by him, it causes time delays in decision implementation; therefore, he will speak with management to resolve this issue.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a mid-term plan?
questions.answer: A mid-term plan is a tactical plan formulated for a duration ranging from more than one year to a maximum of five years.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is flexibility necessary in planning?
questions.answer:
Flexibility is necessary in planning to ensure that an organization can dynamically adjust its operational strategies when faced with unexpected environmental or market disruptions.

The business environment is continuously changing due to volatile economic trends, sudden price hikes, or changing competitor actions. If a plan is formulated with rigid guidelines, it breaks down completely when unforeseen events occur; incorporating flexibility keeps alternative choices open, minimizing losses and protecting the core organizational goals.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of skill is present in Mr. Naif in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Mr. Naif possesses "Conceptual Skill" or "Foresight Skill" (ধারণাগত বা দূরদর্শী যোগ্যতা/দক্ষতা) as described in the stem.

Conceptual skill is the cognitive ability of a manager to look at the entire organization holistically, analyze complex emerging situations, anticipate future environmental changes, and formulate realistic strategic plans accordingly.

In the stem, Mr. Naif is described as a highly visionary marketing manager who visualizes future unexpected scenarios in advance. He outlines his plans by pre-determining exactly what operational measures will yield successful outcomes when a new situation emerges. Because he acts with long-range vision and strategically aligns future challenges with current plans, his capability perfectly matches the definition of conceptual skill.

questions.label: d
questions.text: What type of measure do you think can be taken to solve the problem in the stem? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
To solve the decision-making delay described in the stem, the organization should implement "Decentralization of Authority" (কর্তৃত্ব বিকেন্দ্রীকরণ).

Decentralization involves the systematic delegation of decision-making authority down to the lower and middle levels of management. When an enterprise concentrates all power at a single point, it creates operational bottlenecks and severe procedural delays, whereas spreading authority creates agility and boosts employee morale.

In light of the stem, Nova Sweater Ltd. is facing a crisis of administrative delays because Mr. Naif is overloaded with giving approval for every single decision. To resolve this problem efficiently, the following organizational adjustments should be made:

১. Delegation of Routine Power: Management must distribute basic, routine operational decision-making powers to subordinate assistant managers.

২. Defining Accountability: While delegating authority, a clear framework of accountability must be established for lower-level supervisors.

৩. Transition to Decentralized Structure: Shifting from a highly centralized command system to a decentralized structure will allow departments to function independently and quickly.

Therefore, it can be concluded that adopting a decentralized management approach is the most logical solution for Nova Sweater Ltd. By empowering lower-level managers to handle routine matters, Mr. Naif will be relieved of unnecessary burdens, allowing him to focus entirely on macro-level strategic planning while eliminating implementation delays across the company.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Rohan is the sales manager of Kirtankhola Foods Ltd. Although the market for one of their products, 'Shorna Juice', is good across the country, its sales are not satisfactory in the northern region due to the popularity of juices from several local competitor companies. Therefore, he formulates a plan to increase sales in that region by offering free candy with the juice. Since success was not achieved, what kind of plan should Mr. Rohan adopt to increase the sales of juice in this highly competitive market?

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a special program?
questions.answer: A special program is a single-use plan that outlines a time-bound combination of activities, resources, and steps designed to achieve a specific project goal.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why does the possibility of mistakes decrease in standing plans?
questions.answer:
The possibility of mistakes decreases in standing plans because they are thoroughly reviewed, tested, and established based on historical data to handle recurring institutional situations.

Since standing plans—such as long-term corporate policies and standard operational rules—are formulated by senior executives after meticulous assessment, they eliminate the need for hasty, ad-hoc decisions. Employees follow these pre-determined paths step-by-step during routine tasks, which minimizes behavioral deviations, ensures consistency, and sharply drops operational errors.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of plan, on the basis of function, is the plan adopted by Mr. Rohan? Explain.
questions.answer:
The plan adopted by Mr. Rohan in the stem is a Sales plan (বিক্রয় পরিকল্পনা) or Marketing plan (বাজারজাতকরণ পরিকল্পনা) on the basis of organizational functions.

Functional plans are operational frameworks categorized according to specific departments within an enterprise. A sales plan specifically details the tactical approaches, promotional offers, and targets managed by the marketing department to boost product distribution within a given territory.

In the stem, Mr. Rohan serves as the sales manager of Kirtankhola Foods Ltd. Facing poor performance specifically for 'Shorna Juice' in the northern zone, he designed a regional promotional scheme—offering a "free candy" incentive alongside the main product. Since this specific roadmap was designed by a departmental head to drive immediate consumer purchasing behavior, it exemplifies a functional sales plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: What type of plan should Mr. Rohan adopt in the stem? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
To survive and increase sales in this highly competitive regional market, Mr. Rohan should adopt a "Strategic Plan" (কৌশলগত পরিকল্পনা).

A strategic plan is a long-term, competitive course of action designed by management to counter aggressive rival moves, exploit market opportunities, and ensure corporate sustainability within an unstable, competitive environment. Simple tactical or functional plans fail when competitors possess stronger local loyalty, requiring a macro-level competitive response.

According to the stem, Mr. Rohan's basic operational tactic of offering a free candy incentive failed to crack the northern market because local competitor brands hold a massive, deeply rooted popularity edge. To counter this intense competition effectively, the following strategic dimensions must be integrated into his new plan:

১. Competitive Pricing or Differentiation: He needs to re-evaluate the product's value proposition, potentially altering the price or taste profile to distinctly outperform local alternatives.

২. Aggressive Brand Positioning: Instead of temporary sales incentives, long-term brand awareness campaigns must be launched to capture consumer trust over local brands.

৩. Territorial Distribution Reform: Streamlining the supply chain to make Shorna Juice more accessible than local competitors can secure a permanent market foothold.

Therefore, it can be concluded that relying on superficial, functional promotions will not resolve a deep competitive disadvantage. Mr. Rohan must look at the bigger picture and execute a robust strategic plan. By analyzing rival strengths and systematically repositioning his product, he can successfully break through the competitive barrier, regain market share, and turn his regional losses into sustainable success.

fullCQData.stem: Considering the development of the country and the interest of the people, the government has decided to set up the Karnaphuli Tunnel in Chittagong within three years. The assumptions considering which a plan is made may not always match reality. Therefore, if future situations change, the authorities pre-determine the tasks to be done along with plan revisions. As a result, the construction work of the Karnaphuli Tunnel is completed on time.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a target?
questions.answer: A target is the specific, quantitative, or measurable milestone established within a plan to achieve an overall objective.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is vision important? Explain.
questions.answer:
Vision is crucial because it serves as a long-term, inspirational blueprint that outlines where an organization wants to see itself in the distant future.

Without a clear vision, management lacks a primary source of inspiration and strategic direction, making its operational decisions disjointed.
