It acts as a guiding philosophy that unites all stakeholders, guides the formulation of core corporate objectives, and provides a clear pathway for sustainable structural growth over decades.

questions.label: c
questions.text: On the basis of time, what type of plan is the setup of the Karnaphuli Tunnel? Explain.
questions.answer:
Based on time, the plan to set up the Karnaphuli Tunnel within three years is a Mid-term plan (মধ্যমেয়াদি পরিকল্পনা).

In management, plans are structurally categorized by duration into short-term (one year or less), mid-term (ranging from more than one year up to five years), and long-term (exceeding five years). Mid-term plans break down grand long-term national objectives into actionable projects.

According to the stem, the government planned to construct and complete the major engineering infrastructure of the Karnaphuli Tunnel within a specific duration of "three years." Since a three-year implementation frame falls perfectly within the boundary of more than one year and up to five years, it is classified precisely as a mid-term plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate how the characteristic of planning that accommodates future situational changes is highlighted in the stem.
questions.answer:
The characteristic of planning that accommodates future situational changes and facilitates adaptation is "Flexibility" (নমনীয়তা), and its implementation in the stem is highly justified.

Flexibility is an indispensable attribute of a sound plan, defining its capacity to adjust, alter, or re-strategize smoothly when unexpected shifts or environmental variables disrupt original projections. Because the future is inherently volatile and unpredictable, a plan that lacks elasticity breaks under unexpected crises, leading to project failure.

In light of the stem, the government acknowledged a fundamental truth of management: that planning premises and future assumptions may not always align with ground realities. To address this unpredictability, the project authorities built contingency measures into their roadmap, pre-determining alternative operational actions and revision pathways to execute if the project environment shifted.

Therefore, it can be evaluated that incorporating flexibility is exactly what saved the mega-project from structural delays. By having a flexible framework ready to accommodate environmental shifts, the management successfully handled real-time changes and completed the construction of the Karnaphuli Tunnel precisely within the stipulated deadline.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Goutam is the chief executive of a cement manufacturing and marketing enterprise. Plans are made for each department for a specific period to achieve its purposes. Accordingly, he determines the work targets for each department and coordinates the work of the departments. Before the expiration of the scheduled time, he formulates plans again. Due to these activities, the organization is becoming successful very quickly.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is planning?
questions.answer: Planning is the advance determination of a future course of action to achieve a specific target or organizational objective.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Planning is always goal-centric—Explain.
questions.answer:
Planning is always goal-centric because it is formulated solely to achieve a predetermined target or specific objective, and without a clear goal, a plan becomes entirely meaningless.

The entire structural bridge of planning—including strategies, resource distribution, and operational steps—is built entirely around achieving that designated center point. If there is no specific target to hit, organizational activities become directionless, coordination fails, and resources are wasted, meaning every viable plan must originate from and terminate at a definitive objective.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of plan is Mr. Goutam's plan? Explain.
questions.answer:
The plan formulated by Mr. Goutam in the stem is a Short-term operational plan (স্বল্পমেয়াদি বা পরিচালনামূলক পরিকল্পনা) on the basis of time and organizational functionality.

In management theory, plans are classified by time duration into short, mid, and long-term, where plans covering a period of one year or less are short-term. Concurrently, from a functional perspective, these break down into specific departmental plans designed to run the routine, tactical functions of an enterprise.

According to the stem, Mr. Goutam formulates specific plans for "each department" for a "specific period" to hit their targets. Since he continuously designs these operational frameworks and prepares new ones immediately "before the expiration of the scheduled time" to maintain continuous manufacturing and marketing runs, it perfectly fits the characteristics of short-term departmental operational planning.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Which characteristic of an ideal plan is primarily responsible for the success of Mr. Goutam's planning? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
The specific characteristic of an ideal plan that is primarily responsible for the rapid success of Mr. Goutam's enterprise is "Continuity" (পরিকল্পনার ধারাবাহিকতা).

Continuity in planning means that the planning process is a continuous and unending cycle where, as soon as one scheduled plan or period terminates, a new plan is immediately formulated and implemented to keep operations running smoothly without interruption. A gap between plans creates administrative confusion, whereas maintaining a continuous chain ensures optimal operational momentum and resource utilization.

Based on the stem, the systematic reasons for his success can be analyzed through the following points:

১. Elimination of Operational Gaps: Mr. Goutam ensures that there is no structural pause in his factories; he actively designs the next phase of plans before the running timeline expires.

২. Inter-departmental Coordination: By mapping out clear, successive targets for each department concurrently, he aligns manufacturing and marketing without frictional delays.

৩. Cyclical Progression: The seamless transition from one operational phase to the next allows his cement company to adapt quickly and maintain high productivity.

Therefore, it can be concluded that the core driver behind the organization's rapid success is the strict maintenance of planning continuity. Because Mr. Goutam never lets the strategic momentum drop and keeps the operational wheel turning through proactive, back-to-back planning loops, his management model stands out as highly efficient, justified, and successful.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Habib is the production manager of an enterprise. He formulates a plan to achieve a specific objective. This increases his workload. However, it helps solve a specific problem. On the other hand, Mr. Hasan is the sales manager of that enterprise. He formulates a plan for everyone's use to solve the recurring problems of his department. This reduces his workload.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a budget?
questions.answer: A budget is the numerical or quantitative expression of a plan expected for a defined future period.

questions.label: b
questions.text: "Planning is a forward-looking activity"—Explain.
questions.answer:
Planning is a forward-looking activity because it is always formulated for the future and focuses entirely on anticipating upcoming events, opportunities, and risks.

Since the past cannot be changed and the present is already being executed, all planning efforts are naturally directed toward guiding tomorrow's operations. It bridges the gap between where an organization stands today and where it wants to be tomorrow by forecasting environmental changes and pre-determining proactive tasks.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of plan is Mr. Habib's plan? Explain.
questions.answer:
The plan formulated by Mr. Habib in the stem is a Single-use plan (একাত্মক পরিকল্পনা).

In management theory, plans are classified based on their nature of consumption into standing plans and single-use plans. A single-use plan is a non-recurring course of action designed specifically to accomplish a unique, specific goal or to resolve a one-time problem, which becomes obsolete once the event concludes.

According to the stem, Mr. Habib (the production manager) formulates a plan targeting a "specific objective" to solve a "specific problem." Because this framework is custom-tailored for a unique operational constraint rather than routine activities, and increases his direct oversight workload to handle that specific crisis, it represents a textbook single-use plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Whose plan is better under the policy of cost reduction? Analyze.
questions.answer:
Under the policy of cost reduction and operational efficiency, Mr. Hasan's plan is better than Mr. Habib's plan.

A standing plan is designed for repeated use across recurring organizational situations, significantly lowering routine administrative costs, saving management time, and establishing operational consistency. Conversely, single-use plans demand continuous research, higher design expenses, and constant executive supervision, which increases the organizational workload.

In light of the stem, the comparative analysis between the two managers highlights the following evaluation metrics:

১. Workload and Time Conservation: Mr. Hasan's plan addresses "recurring problems" for "everyone's use," which automatically reduces his routine supervisory workload. Mr. Habib's plan, being a single-use setup, increases his active operational burden.

২. Reusability and Formulation Savings: Because Mr. Hasan's standing guidelines are designed once and applied continuously, the company avoids the financial drain of re-planning daily tasks.

৩. Administrative Cost Efficiency: Standing plans establish clear rules or procedures that empower lower-level workers to act independently, which perfectly aligns with the core principles of an institutional cost-reduction policy.

Therefore, it can be concluded that Mr. Hasan's plan is far superior for long-term operational economy. By standardizing answers to recurring issues, his standing plan minimizes procedural friction and administrative expenditure, making it the ideal choice under any systematic cost-minimization strategy.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Kajal set up a cement company according to a plan. The enterprise was running quite well at the beginning. But after a few days, it was observed that the supply of raw materials and labor was becoming limited. In terms of reality, both workers' wages and the price of raw materials were inconsistent. As a result, within a few days, the enterprise shut down.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is planning?
questions.answer: Planning is the predetermined outline or roadmap of future courses of action to be executed to achieve specific organizational goals.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is flexibility necessary in planning?
questions.answer:
Flexibility is necessary in planning to ensure that an organization can dynamically adjust its operational strategies when faced with unexpected environmental or market fluctuations.

The business environment is highly volatile due to changing economic factors, resource shortages, or cost hikes. If a plan is formulated with rigid guidelines, it breaks down completely when unforeseen events occur; incorporating flexibility keeps alternative options open, minimizing structural losses and protecting the core survival of the business.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of plan, on the basis of nature, is the plan in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
The plan formulated by Mr. Kajal in the stem is a Standing plan (স্থায়ী পরিকল্পনা) in terms of its nature.

In management, a standing plan is a continuous, permanent guide designed to manage recurring and routine operations of an organization over an extended period. Once established, it remains operational for day-to-day production runs until long-term economic shifts make it obsolete.

In the stem, Mr. Kajal established an industrial cement manufacturing company meant for continuous production and operational stability. He mapped out a standard operational plan to run his factory's daily activities routinely from the beginning. Since this plan was designed to govern continuous, ongoing business operations rather than a single event, it is classified as a standing plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate how the enterprise faced disaster because a specific characteristic of planning was not taken into account during formulation.
questions.answer:
The enterprise faced total disaster because the vital characteristic of "Realism" (বাস্তবমুখিতা) or "Accurate Planning Premises" (পরিকল্পনার উপাদান বা পূর্বাবস্থা মূল্যায়ন) was not taken into account during formulation.

Realism means a plan must be formulated based on practical facts, market availability, and stable cost assumptions rather than imaginary assumptions or guesswork. Evaluating planning premises requires a thorough pre-assessment of internal resources and external economic realities before launching long-term execution.

According to the stem, Mr. Kajal designed his plan on paper but completely failed to scan his industrial environment realistically. He did not evaluate the market constraints regarding the steady availability of raw materials or standard labor supply thresholds. When wages and raw material prices fluctuated rapidly, his plan collapsed under financial strain, leading directly to the shutdown of his factory.

Therefore, it can be concluded that the core reason for the business failure was a complete lack of realistic foresight during the planning phase. By ignoring real-world market supply limits and cost structures, his strategy became impractical, proving that a plan detached from reality will inevitably lead an enterprise to severe operational disaster.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Farhad is the owner of an export-oriented ready-made garment industry. The garments produced in his factory are exported to various countries in Europe. Recently, he received an order to export 100,000 t-shirts from Canada. But due to an electrical malfunction, he failed to complete the production on time. Mr. Raiyan is the owner of "ABC Garments Industries." He exports surplus garments after meeting domestic demand. He supplies garments according to customer orders. He arranges a generator to keep production activities uninterrupted.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a process?
questions.answer: A process is a systematic series of sequential steps or interdependent actions taken to achieve a specific end result.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is the objective crucial in formulating a plan? Explain.
questions.answer:
The objective is crucial in formulating a plan because it acts as the primary focal point, destination, and directional guide for all organizational activities.

Without a clear objective, planning becomes completely directionless and speculative, making it impossible to coordinate departmental efforts or measure progress. It serves as the foundation upon which strategies, policies, and operational schedules are constructed, ensuring that all physical and human resources are efficiently utilized toward a specific milestone.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which step of planning was disrupted in Mr. Farhad's activities? Explain.
questions.answer:
The step of planning formulation that was disrupted in Mr. Farhad's activities is "Formulating derivative or supportive plans" (সহায়ক বা অনুগামী পরিকল্পনা প্রণয়ন).

In management theory, after establishing the primary strategic plan, a manager must develop one or complementary supportive plans to ensure the smooth execution of the main goal. Failing to identify auxiliary dependencies—such as backup utilities, logistics, or emergency resource alternatives—leaves the main plan highly vulnerable to sudden environmental disruptions.

In light of the stem, Mr. Farhad's main plan was to manufacture and export 100,000 t-shirts to Canada. However, he relied entirely on the primary line of production without designing a supportive contingency plan for alternative power sources. When an unexpected electrical malfunction hit his factory, his main operational line completely stalled, proving that a lack of supportive planning derailed his export delivery.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "The necessity of supportive planning alongside the main plan to achieve goals is undeniable"—Evaluate in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
The statement "The necessity of supportive planning alongside the main plan to achieve goals is undeniable" is entirely justified, as clearly illustrated by the contrasting outcomes of the two entrepreneurs in the stem.

A main plan establishes the primary targets, while supportive planning acts as an operational safety net, providing alternative routes, contingent resources, and backup systems to counter unexpected internal or external crises. Without auxiliary plans, an organization is left defenseless against routine operational disruptions, whereas proactive backup arrangements guarantee business continuity.

According to the stem, the practical differences between the two business approaches can be evaluated through these points:

১. Vulnerability of a Standalone Plan: Mr. Farhad's approach represents a rigid reliance on a main plan. When power grid instability hit his facility, his operations collapsed, destroying his client delivery commitment and market reputation due to the complete absence of a supportive backup framework.

২. Resilience through Derivative Planning: In sharp contrast, Mr. Raiyan demonstrated exemplary administrative foresight. He anticipated that utility failures could paralyze manufacturing, so he integrated a clear supportive plan by arranging an industrial generator.

৩. Uninterrupted Goal Attainment: By executing this auxiliary strategy, Mr. Raiyan ensured his machinery kept running continuously during grid failures, allowing him to systematically satisfy his client orders on schedule.

Therefore, it can be concluded that a main plan alone cannot survive real-world business hazards. The strategic foresight of Mr. Raiyan confirms that supportive plans are indispensable assets for risk mitigation. To ensure consistent operational survival and shield primary corporate goals from unexpected external shocks, establishing secondary backup frameworks is an absolute necessity.

fullCQData.stem: Swapno Furniture is a renowned furniture manufacturing company. Every year, the management of the organization decides to manufacture 50,000 units of wardrobes. Around the middle of the year, it is observed that products are not being manufactured according to the target. As a result, products cannot be supplied according to customer demand. Due to this, the market is slipping into the hands of competing organizations.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a goal?
questions.answer: A goal is the expected or desired end result toward which planning and organizational activities are directed.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is vision important? Explain.
questions.answer:
Concept of Vision:

Vision refers to a clear, comprehensive, and forward-looking blueprint or ultimate dream of what an organization aspires to become or achieve in the long-term future.

Importance of Vision:

Vision is highly important because it serves as the ultimate source of direction and inspiration for an organization. It guides strategic planning, aligns the efforts of all employees toward a shared destiny, and helps the organization maintain its core identity while adapting to changing competitive landscapes.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which step of planning formulation is missing in setting the target? Explain.
questions.answer:
Identification of the Missing Step:

The step of planning formulation that is missing in setting the target for Swapno Furniture is "Evaluating Alternatives and Feasibility Analysis" (specifically, evaluating internal capacity and resource availability before finalizing the target).

Contextual Explanation:

In the stem, the management of Swapno Furniture fixed an annual target of producing 50,000 wardrobes without thoroughly assessing their operational capacity, raw material availability, or labor efficiency. By mid-year, they realized they could not achieve this target, leading to a supply shortage and loss of market share to competitors.

Analysis of the Flaw:

A realistic plan requires analyzing the organization's strengths, weaknesses, and available resources before locking in a goal. Because the management skipped the critical step of checking whether their production infrastructure could realistically support the 50,000-unit target, the planning failed, demonstrating a clear gap in the pre-execution feasibility phase.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze what needs to be done to reclaim the market of the organization described in the stem.
questions.answer:
Core Recommendation for Market Reclamation:

To reclaim its lost market share from competitors, Swapno Furniture must immediately implement effective "Controlling Measures" and "Revised/Corrective Planning."

Step 1: Deviation Analysis:

First, the management must identify the specific reasons behind the mid-year production deficit. They need to analyze whether the shortfall was caused by mechanical failures, shortage of skilled labor, supply chain disruptions of raw materials, or unrealistic target setting in the first place.

Step 2: Corrective Actions (Points of Action):

Based on the deviation analysis, the company must execute the following corrective steps:

* Capacity Optimization: Upgrade machinery or hire temporary skilled workers to boost daily production output.

* Supply Chain Adjustments: Partner with reliable suppliers to ensure an uninterrupted flow of materials.

* Revised Short-Term Targets: Formulate realistic, short-term broken-down targets for the remaining months to bridge the supply gap quickly.

Conclusion:

In conclusion, planning without control is meaningless. By adopting strict controlling mechanisms, comparing actual performance with standard benchmarks, and taking timely corrective measures, Swapno Furniture can successfully fulfill consumer demand and win back its competitive edge in the market.

fullCQData.stem: To ease communication between the tourist city Cox's Bazar, the Chittagong seaport, and the rest of Bangladesh, the government undertook a plan to construct a tunnel under the Karnaphuli River. After commencing the work, keeping in mind the river's navigability and coordination with connecting roads, some qualitative changes were made to the plan. The construction work of the tunnel has been successfully completed.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a project?
questions.answer: A project is a scheme or a specific, time-bound, non-recurring single-use plan designed to achieve particular objectives within predefined resource and budget constraints.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is strategic planning important for an industrial enterprise? Explain.
questions.answer:
Concept of Strategic Planning:

Strategic planning refers to the long-term, high-level planning formulated by top management to achieve the ultimate goals of an enterprise by considering competitive threats, market opportunities, and organizational strengths.

Importance for an Industrial Enterprise:

Strategic planning is vital for an industrial enterprise because it enables the organization to survive and thrive amidst intense market competition, technological shifts, and economic volatility. It assists top management in making crucial decisions regarding major capital investments, resource allocation, market expansion, and long-term sustainability, ensuring all departmental goals align with the core mission.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on its nature, what type of plan is the construction of the tunnel under the Karnaphuli River? Explain.
questions.answer:
Identification of Plan Nature:

Based on its nature, the construction of the tunnel under the Karnaphuli River is a "Single-use Plan" (specifically, a Project).

Contextual Explanation:

A single-use plan is formulated to achieve a specific goal or handle a non-recurring situation and ceases to exist once the target is accomplished. The construction of the Karnaphuli River tunnel is a unique, massive infrastructural task with a specific start and end date, meaning it will not be repeatedly constructed once finished.

Detailed Analysis:

In management, programs and projects fall under single-use plans. Because this mega-infrastructure involves independent resource allocations, specialized engineering designs, and a specific timeline dedicated solely to establishing this distinct connectivity route, it perfectly exemplifies a project-level single-use plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Based on which characteristic of a good plan was it possible to make qualitative changes to the tunnel construction plan? Analyze in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Identification of the Characteristic:

The qualitative changes made to the tunnel construction plan were possible due to the essential characteristic of a good plan known as "Flexibility" (or adaptability).

Concept of Flexibility:

Flexibility in planning means incorporating enough elasticity so that the plan can be altered, adjusted, or modified smoothly without major disruptions if unexpected field realities, environmental changes, or new requirements surface during execution.

Analysis in Light of the Stem:

In the given stem, after the construction began, authorities encountered unexpected situations regarding the river's navigability and alignment with the connecting road network. Since the original plan possessed the trait of flexibility, engineers and administrators were able to make qualitative structural changes mid-way through execution without abandoning the entire mission.

Conclusion:

Ultimately, rigid plans fail when faced with real-world physical and environmental challenges. It was the presence of flexibility in the government's blueprint that allowed the team to safely adapt to the river's conditions, leading to the successful completion of the Karnaphuli tunnel project.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Sagar is the owner of a garment industry. This year, he received an order to supply four lakh (400,000) meters of fabric. His factory operates 250 working days a year and remains in production for eight hours daily. He instructed the production manager accordingly. But at the end of the year, it was found that only three lakh (300,000) meters of fabric had been produced. Mr. Sagar unearthed the actual reason for the production shortfall compared to the target and took measures to introduce new technology as well as arrange necessary training for the workers.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a mission?
questions.answer: A mission is the fundamental, unique purpose that sets an organization apart from other firms and identifies the scope of its operations in product and market terms.

questions.label: b
questions.text: "Planning is the mirror of the future"—Explain.
questions.answer:
Concept of Planning and Future Reflection:

Planning is considered the mirror of the future because it is a forward-looking process that predetermines what is to be done, how it is to be done, when it is to be done, and who is to do it to achieve a specific organizational goal.

Why it acts as a Mirror:

By looking into a plan, an organization can clearly visualize its upcoming activities, potential challenges, resource requirements, and final destinations before execution even begins. Just as a mirror reflects a clear image of reality, a well-formulated plan reflects a clear blueprint of future organizational operations, minimizing uncertainty and risk.

questions.label: c
questions.text: To achieve the target of the order described in the stem, how many meters of fabric must be produced per hour?
questions.answer:
Mathematical Target Statement:

To achieve the targeted order described in the stem, the garment factory must produce 200 meters of fabric per hour.

Step-by-Step Calculation:

The mathematical calculation based on the data given in the stem is as follows:

* Total target production = 400,000 meters

* Total annual working days = 250 days

* Daily production hours = 8 hours

Therefore, the total annual production hours can be calculated as:

$$\text{Total Hours} = 250 \times 8 = 2,000\text{ hours}$$

Final Result Derivation:

Now, dividing the total production target by the total working hours yields the required hourly production rate:

$$\text{Hourly Production} = \frac{400,000\text{ meters}}{2,000\text{ hours}} = 200\text{ meters/hour}$$

Hence, the required hourly output rate is exactly 200 meters.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the justification of the activities undertaken by Mr. Sagar to fulfill the production target in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Core Evaluation of Mr. Sagar's Action:

The activities undertaken by Mr. Sagar—specifically, detecting the causes of the shortfall, introducing modern technology, and arranging training—are highly justified, logical, and fully aligned with the principles of "Controlling" and "Strategic Human Resource Management."

Point 1: Scientific Deviation Identification:

Control is the final function of management that measures performance against standards. Instead of blindly blaming the workforce for the 100,000-meter production deficit, Mr. Sagar chose to scientifically investigate the root cause of the deviation, which is the hallmark of an effective leader.

Point 2: Technological and Skill Modernization:

By introducing advanced technology and providing proper training, Mr. Sagar directly tackled structural limitations. Workers cannot achieve high-efficiency standards with backdated tools; upgrading technology combined with training bridges the skill gap, reduces wastage, and systematically raises the hourly output closer to the 200 meters/hour standard.

Conclusion:

In conclusion, Mr. Sagar's actions are entirely justified because they transition the factory from a state of passive failure to active operational excellence. These corrective measures ensure that the factory will minimize future deviations, maximize productivity, and easily meet its supply commitments in the upcoming production cycles.

fullCQData.stem: Rabta Sisters Ltd. of Chittagong has decided to open a new boutique in Pallabi, Dhaka. For this purpose, they have completed determining the sources of capital and evaluating the opportunities and facilities. However, during the selection process, they did not consider any area other than Pallabi. As a result, it is becoming difficult for them to survive in the highly competitive new market.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a major program (or primary project/schedule)?
questions.answer: A major program (or primary schedule) is a comprehensive, large-scale single-use plan that outlines the major steps, resource allocations, and timelines required to achieve a significant organizational objective.

questions.label: b
questions.text: "Planning is the foundation of other management functions"—Explain.
questions.answer:
Concept of Planning as the Foundation:

Planning is the primary and most fundamental function of management, which predetermines what needs to be done, when, how, and by whom to achieve desired organizational objectives.

Why it acts as the Foundation:

All subsequent management functions—such as organizing, staffing, directing, motivating, coordinating, and controlling—are designed and executed based on the blueprint provided by the plan. Without a clear plan, there is no structural base to organize resources, no direction to guide employees, and no standard against which to measure and control performance, making it the bedrock of management.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which function of management is indicated by Rabta Sisters Ltd.'s decision to establish a new boutique? Explain.
questions.answer:
Identification of the Managerial Function:

The decision of Rabta Sisters Ltd. to establish a new boutique indicates the managerial function of "Planning" (specifically, Decision Making, which is an integral part of the planning process).

Contextual Explanation:

Planning involves choosing a future course of action from various options, and decision-making is the core mechanism through which goals and operational paths are selected. Rabta Sisters Ltd. followed a systematic path of determining capital sources and assessing facilities to execute a new business expansion, which is the definition of formulation.

Detailed Analysis:

Every strategic move an enterprise makes—such as branching out into a new city or opening a retail unit—requires a formal set of objectives and pre-calculated arrangements. Since the management of Rabta Sisters Ltd. engaged in determining capital sources and analyzing situational benefits before committing assets to the Dhaka branch, their activities strictly fall within the purview of the planning function.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "The existence of Rabta Sisters is threatened because they did not consider a specific step of that particular managerial function described in the stem"—Analyze.
questions.answer:
Identification of the Neglected Step:

The specific step of the planning function that Rabta Sisters Ltd. failed to consider is "Developing and Evaluating Alternative Courses of Action" before making the final selection.

Point 1: The Principle of Alternatives in Planning:

A crucial step in effective planning and decision-making is identifying all possible alternative solutions or locations and evaluating their pros and cons. By looking at multiple options, an organization can weigh factors like competition density, cost structures, and target demographics to pick the most viable path.

Point 2: Analysis of the Failure in the Stem:

According to the stem, Rabta Sisters Ltd. fixed their sights solely on Pallabi without assessing or comparing other potential commercial areas in Dhaka (such as Banani, Dhanmondi, or Mirpur). Because they skipped the step of generating alternative locations, they failed to realize that Pallabi was highly saturated with competitors, which severely crippled their market entry.

Conclusion:

In conclusion, the statement is completely accurate. Blindly choosing a single course of action without evaluating competitive alternatives turns a strategic plan into a gamble. Because Rabta Sisters Ltd. ignored this foundational step of the planning process, they entered a hyper-competitive market unprepared, pushing the entire existence of their new boutique toward a state of crisis.

fullCQData.stem: Nipa Apparels has set a target to increase sales by 10% over the next five years. Keeping this target in mind, the production and sales departments achieved good results in the first year. However, due to the COVID-19 pandemic, the desired progress was not made in the second year. In this context, the apparel company restructured its plan. Currently, the sales rate of Nipa Apparels is increasing according to the desired target.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a planning premise?
questions.answer: A planning premise refers to the anticipated future environment, conditions, assumptions, or expected factors within which a plan is expected to operate.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is the relationship between planning and objectives?
questions.answer:
Concept of Relationship:

Objectives are the target end results that an organization seeks to achieve, while planning is the detailed operational blueprint or course of action designed to reach those specific targets.

Core Connection:

The relationship between them is foundational; planning always begins with an objective, making objectives the guiding star of the planning process. Without a clear objective, planning becomes directionless and meaningless, whereas, without structured planning, an objective remains an unachievable dream.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on duration, what type of plan is the one mentioned in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Identification of Plan Type:

Based on duration, the initial plan mentioned in the stem is a "Long-term Plan."

Contextual Definition:

In management, plans that are formulated for a duration exceeding five years (or spanning exactly five years or more in the context of strategic corporate frameworks) are classified as long-term plans.

Detailed Analysis:

According to the stem, Nipa Apparels formulated a growth roadmap with a clear 5-year timeline to increase its sales by 10%. Since the operational and strategic boundary of this corporate target is structured around a multi-year horizon rather than a standard operational fiscal year, it directly fulfills the criteria of a long-term plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "A specific characteristic of a good plan has played a role in the current desired sales growth of Nipa Apparels"—Analyze.
questions.answer:
Identification of the Characteristic:

The specific characteristic of a good plan that enabled Nipa Apparels to bounce back and achieve its desired sales growth is "Flexibility" (or adaptability).

Point 1: Understanding Flexibility in Planning:

Flexibility is the capability of a plan to be altered, adjusted, or restructured in response to unforeseen environmental variations, sudden economic shifts, or macro-level crises without destroying the central objective.

Point 2: Application to the Stem:

In this case, Nipa Apparels encountered a massive external disruption in its second year due to the global COVID-19 pandemic, halting their initial momentum. Instead of rigidly following a failing track, the management utilized the elasticity of their strategic framework to completely overhaul and restructure their planning execution to suit the new market reality.

Conclusion:

In conclusion, the statement is completely accurate. A rigid plan collapses when faced with Black Swan events like a global pandemic. Because Nipa Apparels incorporated flexibility into their management culture, they were able to pivot smoothly, realign their departments, and ultimately restore their sales growth trajectory toward the target.

fullCQData.stem: Vita Company, in order to capture markets across the entire country, divides the total market of the country into several regions and designs different types of plans for each region accordingly. Keeping an uncertain future in mind, the company keeps scope to make necessary changes to the adopted plans under altered circumstances. As a result, they can bring quick changes to the plans in unexpected situations and continue their work accordingly.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a standing plan?
questions.answer: A standing plan is a recurring, long-term plan designed to guide repetitive or ongoing organizational activities, remaining in use until it is officially modified or replaced.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is planning called the primary function of management? Explain.
questions.answer:
Concept of Primary Function:

Planning is called the primary function of management because it is the initial, foundational step that predetermines what is to be done, how, when, and by whom to achieve organizational goals.

Why it is Called Primary:

Every other management function—such as organizing, staffing, directing, motivating, coordinating, and controlling—flows directly from and is dependent upon the established plan. Without formulating a plan first, a manager has no objective basis to assemble resources, assign roles, lead personnel, or evaluate final outcomes, making it the chronological and functional starting point of the entire management cycle.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on organizational structure, what type of plan is the one adopted by the company in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Identification of Plan Type:

Based on organizational structure (or administrative levels), the plan adopted by Vita Company is a "Regional Plan" (which falls under Divisional/Departmental Planning).

Contextual Explanation:

When plans are structured and classified according to specific organizational levels, functional units, or geographic territories rather than just time horizons, they are known as structural or regional plans. This allows large-scale enterprises to address distinct geographic demands effectively.

Detailed Analysis:

According to the stem, Vita Company divided the country's macro market into separate regional territories and formulated a distinct, customized plan for each distinct region. Because this planning layout segments corporate strategy based on geographical operational divisions rather than a single centralized office blueprint, it represents an ideal example of structural regional planning.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the justification of following the specific principle in planning that leaves scope for changes in the plan.
questions.answer:
Identification of the Principle:

The principle followed by Vita Company that leaves scope for necessary modifications under altered circumstances is the "Principle of Flexibility" in planning.

Point 1: Understanding Flexibility as a Core Tenet:

Flexibility in planning refers to the built-in adaptability or elasticity that allows a plan to be restructured, adjusted, or altered smoothly whenever unexpected external market shifts, economic crises, or sudden field realities arise during execution.

Point 2: Analysis of Practical Justification in the Stem:

The application of this principle is highly justified for Vita Company because the future is inherently dynamic and uncertain. The stem highlights that when unexpected situations hit the market, the management was able to bring rapid changes to their regional blueprints without halting operations, allowing them to maintain momentum and successfully protect their market share.

Conclusion:

In conclusion, a rigid plan is prone to complete failure when faced with volatile real-world conditions. By building flexibility into their regional planning system, Vita Company successfully minimized corporate risks, ensured uninterrupted workflow during crises, and proved that adaptable planning is a vital requirement for long-term business survival.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Karim is the chairman of 'Mahi Shoe Ltd.'. In 2015, the company planned to manufacture two lakh (200,000) pairs of shoes. However, around the middle of the year, several experienced skilled workers retired. Additionally, some more workers left their jobs due to higher salary offers from a new factory. As a result, the organization was able to manufacture only 1 lakh 60 thousand (160,000) pairs of shoes at the end of the year. Due to the inability to supply shoes on time, the company's reputation was damaged.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a budget?
questions.answer: A budget is the numerical expression or quantitative statement of an operational plan expressed in financial or physical terms for a specified future period.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is flexibility in planning required? Explain.
questions.answer:
Concept of Flexibility:

Flexibility in planning refers to the built-in adaptability or elasticity that allows a plan to be modified, adjusted, or altered smoothly in response to unexpected variations or environmental changes during execution.

Why it is Required:

Flexibility is absolutely essential because the business environment is highly dynamic, uncertain, and unpredictable. If an organization implements a completely rigid plan, it will collapse when faced with sudden crises like raw material shortages, workforce turnover, or macroeconomic shocks; whereas a flexible plan allows management to pivot, realign resources, and keep working toward the core goal.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which step of planning formulation was not properly followed, resulting in the creation of the current situation in the organization? Explain.
questions.answer:
Identification of the Neglected Step:

The specific step of the planning formulation process that was not properly followed by Mahi Shoe Ltd. is "Evaluating Alternatives and Analyzing Planning Premises" (specifically, forecasting labor market risks and human resource availability).

Contextual Explanation:

Planning premises are the anticipated future environmental factors, assumptions, and constraints within which a plan operates. When Mahi Shoe Ltd. set an annual target of 200,000 pairs of shoes, they formulated their blueprint based on static conditions without forecasting expected employee retirements or analyzing competitive labor market movements.

Detailed Analysis:

According to the stem, the sudden retirement of seasoned artisans and the structural migration of workers to a competitor offering higher wages left the factory short-staffed, causing a 40,000-pair production deficit. Because management failed to evaluate internal workforce demographics and external labor environment dynamics during the initial planning phase, they could not prevent the supply bottleneck that ruined their corporate reputation.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Recommend what Mr. Karim should do to restore the reputation of the organization described in the stem.
questions.answer:
Core Recommendation for Reputation Restoration:

To restore the damaged reputation of Mahi Shoe Ltd., Mr. Karim must immediately design and implement a comprehensive "Corrective Action Plan" focused on "Human Resource Control" and "Supply Chain Management."

Step 1: Workforce Stabilization Measures (Points of Action):

Mr. Karim must immediately address the root cause of the production failure by optimizing the company's human resource framework:

* Competitive Compensation Restructuring: Revise the company's wage structure and financial incentives to match or exceed the competitor's offers, putting an immediate stop to skilled labor drain.

* Aggressive Recruitment and Training: Launch an urgent recruitment drive to hire skilled shoemakers, paired with fast-tracked training programs to bring productivity levels back to standard capacity.

Step 2: Operational and Customer Relations Recovery:

Simultaneously, tactical corporate measures must be taken to rebuild trust with client networks:

* Revised Short-Term Production Targets: Utilize automated manufacturing tools or introduce overtime shifts to clear the backlogged demand as rapidly as possible.

* Transparent Client Communication: Engage directly with dissatisfied buyers, offering transparent explanations, delivery guarantees, or short-term discounts to secure future orders.

Conclusion:

In conclusion, planning without robust management control functions cannot succeed. By addressing competitive market realities, introducing attractive employee retention policies, and optimizing production speeds, Mr. Karim can systematically eliminate the production deficit, fulfill pending market orders, and successfully win back the company's market goodwill.

fullCQData.stem: Considering national development and public interest, the government has taken a decision to establish a metro rail system in Dhaka, which will take several years to construct. The premises and conditions based on which a plan is prepared may not always remain matching over time. Therefore, if future conditions change, the authorities predetermine corrective courses of action, including plan revisions, so that the objective of constructing the metro rail can be achieved.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is decision making?
questions.answer: Decision making is the process of selecting the best and most feasible alternative course of action from among two or more available options to achieve a specific goal.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is vision important?
questions.answer:
Concept of Vision:

Vision refers to a highly inspirational, forward-looking, and ultimate long-term dream or blueprint of what an organization or nation aspires to achieve or become in the distant future.

Importance of Vision:

Vision is extremely important because it provides a clear sense of ultimate direction, serving as the foundational guide for all strategic planning. It acts as a unifying force that inspires employees or citizens, aligns long-term organizational energies toward a shared destiny, and ensures the entity remains focused on its core purposes despite short-term environmental challenges.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on its nature, what type of plan is the establishment of the metro rail mentioned in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Identification of Plan Nature:

Based on its nature, the plan to establish a metro rail system in Dhaka is a "Single-use Plan" (specifically, a Program).

Contextual Explanation:

A single-use plan is formulated to achieve a specific, unique goal or handle a non-recurring major scenario, and it naturally ceases to exist as an active blueprint once that specific mission is fully accomplished. Establishing a country's mega mass-transit infrastructure like a metro rail is a massive, multi-year, non-repetitive initiative.

Detailed Analysis:

In management frameworks, large-scale single-use plans that combine goals, policies, procedures, and budgets for a massive national or corporate mission are called programs. Because this metro rail construction involves billions in capital, independent engineering layouts, and a multi-year execution timeline targeting a singular objective, it stands as an ideal example of a strategic program.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the characteristic of planning that makes it easy to handle changing future conditions, in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Identification of the Characteristic:

The essential characteristic of a good plan highlighted in the stem that simplifies handling volatile future conditions is "Flexibility" (or adaptability).

Point 1: Understanding Flexibility in Management:

Flexibility in planning refers to the structural elasticity or built-in capability of a plan to be updated, revised, or modified mid-course without major losses whenever unexpected environmental variations, field shifts, or macro-level events alter the initial planning premises.

Point 2: Analysis of Application in the Stem:

In this case, the government acknowledged that long-term mega-projects face unpredictable changes over the years since real-world conditions rarely match static historical data perfectly. To safeguard the project, the authorities integrated flexibility by mapping out contingent revisions and alternative operational paths right from the beginning, ensuring that dynamic future risks would not paralyze construction.

Conclusion:

In conclusion, the analysis confirms that rigid plans inevitably fail when faced with changing socio-economic or environmental realities. By intentionally incorporating the trait of flexibility into the Dhaka metro rail blueprint, the management successfully shielded the national project from execution failure, ensuring its ultimate objectives are safely met regardless of external changes.

fullCQData.stem: Bitac Ltd. has started shipbuilding work near Payra Seaport. In a meeting held in January 2020, various important decisions were taken by the organization. Among them, a decision was made to export manufactured ships to several countries, including Europe, by 2025. On the other hand, a domestic pharmaceutical manufacturing company, M/S M.N. Co., has been operating its business with a good reputation for a long time. Recently, due to the impact of globalization, the establishment of similar firms by foreign entrepreneurs, policy changes by the government, and technological advancements, they are unable to retain their previous market position. As a result, the sales volume of M.N. Co. is decreasing day by day.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is decision making?
questions.answer: Decision making is the process of selecting the best and most feasible alternative course of action from among two or more available options to achieve a specific goal.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Explain the relationship between objective and planning.
questions.answer:
Concept of Relationship:

An objective represents the desired target end result that an organization intends to achieve, while planning provides the comprehensive roadmap or operational blueprint designed to reach that target.

Core Connection:

The relationship between them is foundational; planning always begins with an objective, making objectives the guiding star of the planning process. Without a clear objective, planning becomes directionless and meaningless, whereas, without structured planning, an objective remains an unachievable dream.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on duration, what type of plan is the one adopted by Bitac Ltd.? Describe.
questions.answer:
Identification of Plan Type:

Based on duration, the plan adopted by Bitac Ltd. is a "Long-term Plan."

Contextual Definition:

In management frameworks, plans that are formulated to guide an organization's major actions and resource allocations over a timeline spanning five years or more are classified as long-term plans.

Detailed Analysis:

According to the stem, during their January 2020 meeting, the management of Bitac Ltd. set a strategic target to export manufactured ships to European markets by the year 2025. Since the execution and realization horizon of this operational blueprint spans a full five-year period, it directly fulfills the criteria of a strategic long-term corporate plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the necessary steps to restore the reputation of the organization by identifying the reasons behind the decline in sales of M.N. Co. in the stem through planning actions.
questions.answer:
Identification of the Core Problem:

The decline in sales of M.N. Co. is caused by structural threats in the macro-environment—specifically globalization, foreign competition, shifting government policies, and technological advancements—which can be resolved through "Strategic Planning Re-formulation" and "Contingency Planning."

Step 1: Environmental Scanning and Problem Identification:

First, M.N. Co.'s management must utilize a SWOT analysis plan to identify exactly where their domestic operations are falling short. They need to analyze how foreign entrepreneurs are outperforming them, whether it is due to lower pricing, superior chemical formulations, or more efficient marketing.

Step 2: Corrective Planning Measures (Points of Action):

Based on the analysis, the company must execute the following tactical planning adjustments:

* Technological Upgradation Plan: Formulate an immediate investment layout to import modern pharmaceutical manufacturing equipment to match the technological excellence of foreign rivals.

* Product Diversification Strategy: Develop new medicine lines or cost-effective formulations to adapt to the government's updated regulatory policies.

* Market Retention Planning: Launch aggressive local branding campaigns to leverage their long-standing historical reputation and regain domestic consumer trust.

Conclusion:

In conclusion, when a traditional business encounters external macro-shocks, rigid traditional methods guarantee failure. By evaluating the shifting environmental parameters and actively introducing modern, flexible strategic plans, M.N. Co. can successfully neutralize foreign competition, boost its daily sales volume, and fully restore its market goodwill.

fullCQData.stem: According to the decision of 'Zara Garments' company, the task of recruiting and training 2,000 employees at various stages between 2015 and 2018 was successfully completed. Due to the establishment of a new branch in Chittagong, a meeting was called in 2023, and 100 people from among the employees and officers were urgently promoted and sent there. Those deprived of promotion raised demands for promotion by following rules, policies, and procedures. Finally, with the assurance of the authorities, the situation returned to normal.
fullCQData.meta: Viqarunnisa Noon School and College, Dhaka · 2026

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a program?
questions.answer: A program is a major, comprehensive single-use plan that outlines the necessary steps, resources, and timelines required to achieve a specific, significant organizational goal.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is strategic planning? Explain.
questions.answer:
Concept of Strategic Planning:

Strategic planning refers to the long-term, high-level planning formulated by top-level management to achieve the ultimate goals of an enterprise by considering competitive threats, market opportunities, and organizational strengths.

Core Characteristics:

It typically covers a long horizon and guides the broad direction of the entire firm. This type of planning determines how an enterprise will position itself against competitors and navigate volatile macroeconomic changes to ensure long-term market survival.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Under which time-based plan is the recruitment and training process of 'Zara Garments' included? Explain.
questions.answer:
Identification of Plan Type:

The recruitment and training process of 'Zara Garments' between 2015 and 2018 is included under a "Mid-term Plan" based on duration.

Contextual Definition:

In management frameworks, plans that are formulated for a duration extending beyond one year up to a maximum of five years are classified as mid-term plans.

Detailed Analysis:

According to the stem, the company systematically executed its human resource recruitment and training initiative over a span starting from 2015 to 2018, which constitutes a three-year implementation window. Since this corporate roadmap is longer than a standard single-year operational plan but does not exceed the five-year threshold, it perfectly represents a mid-term plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: For which type of plan are the promotion-deprived employees protesting? Analyze.
questions.answer:
Identification of the Target Plan:

The promotion-deprived employees are protesting for the proper implementation and preservation of a "Standing Plan" (specifically, a Policy or Procedure regarding promotions).

Point 1: Understanding Standing Plans (Policy and Procedure):

A standing plan is a recurring guideline designed to handle repetitive organizational activities consistently over time. Within standing plans, a "policy" acts as a general guide for decision-making, while a "procedure" outlines the exact chronological steps employees must follow to execute a specific task.

Point 2: Analysis of the Situation in the Stem:

The stem states that the management suddenly promoted 100 people on an "urgent basis" in 2023 to fill vacancies in Chittagong, bypassing established norms. This abrupt action caused grievance among other qualified employees because it violated the permanent, structural promotion rules. By demanding that the company follow specific guidelines, rules, and sequences, the staff was fighting to uphold the standing plan framework of the organization.

Conclusion:

In conclusion, when a management team ignores its own standing plans to make arbitrary, ad-hoc decisions, it triggers internal conflict and labor dissatisfaction. The protest of the employees was entirely focused on forcing the authorities to respect and implement the transparent, long-term standing promotion policies of Zara Garments, which ultimately restored workplace peace once the management assured them of compliance.

fullCQData.stem: Considering national development and public interest, the government has taken a decision to establish a metro rail system across the entirety of Dhaka, which will take several years to fully construct. The conditions based on which a plan is prepared may not always be feasible to implement over time. Therefore, if future conditions change, the authorities predetermine corrective courses of action, including plan revisions, so that the objective of constructing the metro rail can be achieved.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is training?
questions.answer: Training is a systematic process of increasing the professional knowledge, operational skills, and practical capabilities of employees to execute specific tasks efficiently.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is planning called an intellectual or mental process? Explain.
questions.answer:
Concept of Intellectual Process:

Planning is called an intellectual or mental process because it involves rigorous cognitive exercises, creative thinking, foresight, and sound judgment rather than manual labor or physical execution.

Why it requires Mental Processing:

Before a manager can finalize an operational path, they must mentally visualize upcoming circumstances, evaluate past performance, analyze market variables, and logically assess multiple alternative courses of action. Since it demands a high degree of imagination, intelligence, and analytical decision-making to predetermine what needs to be done in an uncertain future, it is entirely a reflective thinking exercise.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on its nature, what type of plan is the establishment of the metro rail mentioned in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Identification of Plan Nature:

Based on its nature, the plan to establish a metro rail system in Dhaka is a "Single-use Plan" (specifically, a Program).

Contextual Explanation:

A single-use plan is formulated to achieve a unique, specific objective or handle a non-recurring corporate or national mission, and it naturally expires once that specific goal is achieved. Building a massive city-wide mass rapid transit system is a unique infrastructural task that will not be repeatedly constructed once completed.

Detailed Analysis:

In management science, a large-scale single-use plan that incorporates a mix of goals, concrete policies, chronological procedures, and major budget allocations for a complex project is classified as a program. Because this multi-year national expansion project involves independent engineering networks, unique financial layouts, and a dedicated structural path, it functions as a highly strategic administrative program.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the characteristic of planning that makes it easy to handle changing future conditions, in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Identification of the Characteristic:

The essential characteristic of a good plan highlighted in the stem that simplifies handling volatile future conditions is "Flexibility" (or adaptability).

Point 1: Understanding Flexibility as a Design Principle:

Flexibility in planning refers to the built-in elasticity or capability of an operational blueprint to be modified, updated, or restructured mid-way without major structural failure whenever environmental factors or real-world parameters shift away from initial expectations.

Point 2: Application to the Stem Environment:

According to the stem, the college authorities highlight that mega-infrastructure layouts face unpredictable variations across their long implementation periods because real-world environments rarely remain static. To protect the project, the government embedded flexibility into the planning core by mapping out preset revision methods and alternative administrative contingencies, ensuring that future physical or macro-shocks will not stall development.

Conclusion:

In conclusion, rigid corporate or national plans collapse when confronted with fluctuating real-world environments. By prioritizing the characteristic of flexibility within the Dhaka metro rail project, the planners ensured that the administrative framework remains resilient, allowing execution teams to safely alter paths when necessary and ensure the core transit goals are met.

fullCQData.stem: A popular and established toothpaste manufacturing company wishes to add a new product to its product line. In this regard, they have verified the market and taken a decision to market toothbrushes. As a market entry strategy, the company is verifying and evaluating the option of selling a toothbrush directly under the company name or offering a 'Buy one get one free' promotion, meaning providing a free toothbrush with a toothpaste for one month.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a goal?
questions.answer: A goal is the expected or desired end result toward which planning and organizational activities are directed.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is planning called an intellectual or mental process? Explain.
questions.answer:
Concept of Intellectual Process:

Planning is called an intellectual or mental process because it involves rigorous cognitive exercises, creative thinking, foresight, and sound judgment rather than manual labor or physical execution.

Why it requires Mental Processing:

Before a manager can finalize an operational path, they must mentally visualize upcoming circumstances, evaluate past performance, analyze market variables, and logically assess multiple alternative courses of action. Since it demands a high degree of imagination, intelligence, and analytical decision-making to predetermine what needs to be done in an uncertain future, it is entirely a reflective thinking exercise.

questions.label: c
questions.text: At which step of planning formulation is the company currently positioned in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Identification of the Planning Step:

The company described in the stem is currently positioned at the "Evaluating Alternative Courses of Action" step of planning formulation.

Contextual Explanation:

After determining objectives and establishing planning premises, a management team identifies various alternative ways to achieve their targets. Once the alternatives are listed, each option must be thoroughly analyzed and verified regarding its costs, benefits, risks, and feasibility before choosing the best one.

Detailed Analysis:

According to the stem, the toothpaste company has already settled on the objective of launching toothbrushes and has analyzed the market environment. Currently, they are evaluating two distinct strategic alternatives: launching the toothbrush directly as a standalone product under their established brand name, or utilizing a high-impact promotional campaign ("Buy one get one free") for a month to drive trial. Since they are actively analyzing and verifying the feasibility of these specific paths, they are at the evaluation stage.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Will the company described in the stem be successful in tackling competition? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
Core Evaluation of Competitive Success:

In my opinion, the company will be highly successful in tackling market competition because they are following systematic planning principles and choosing high-impact entry strategies like "Brand Leverage" and "Sales Promotion."

Point 1: Utilizing Established Brand Equity:

The stem highlights that the firm is already a popular and established manufacturer. By entering the toothbrush market, they can smoothly transfer their existing consumer goodwill and brand loyalty to the new product category, significantly lower customer acquisition costs compared to a brand-new competitor.

Point 2: Effectiveness of the Promotional Strategy:

Evaluating a 'Buy one get one free' campaign for a month is an excellent tactical move to break into a competitive market. This promotional mechanism eliminates the initial purchase risk for loyal consumers, incentivizes immediate shifting from rival brands, and ensures rapid market penetration and high product visibility within a short period.

Conclusion:

In conclusion, because the company is an established market player and is scientifically analyzing its entry options before execution, they possess a strong competitive advantage. By executing either of their well-calculated strategies—especially the promotional bundle—they can easily counter rival firms, secure substantial market share, and successfully expand their corporate footprint.

fullCQData.stem: On June 25, 2022, Bangladesh's longest Padma Bridge was inaugurated. As a result of this bridge, the communication system of the southern districts of Bangladesh with Dhaka district improved further. Initially, although the bridge construction project was adopted as a single continuous plan, later due to the strong currents of the Padma River and various natural reasons, qualitative changes had to be made to the plan.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a procedure?
questions.answer: A procedure is a chronological, step-by-step sequence of specific operational actions required to execute a routine or repetitive activity within an organization.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why does planning have to be data-dependent? Explain.
questions.answer:
Concept of Data Dependence:

Planning must be data-dependent because it is a forward-looking process that predetermines future courses of action based on historical facts, current market statistics, and realistic projections.

Why it is Crucial:

If a plan is formulated without accurate, empirical, and verified data, it turns into mere guesswork or unrealistic speculation. Relying on reliable information minimizes risks, enables managers to establish precise planning premises, and ensures that the final organizational targets are achievable under volatile real-world economic and environmental conditions.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on its nature, what type of plan is the plan to build the Padma Bridge? Explain.
questions.answer:
Identification of Plan Nature:

Based on its nature, the plan to construct the Padma Bridge is a "Single-use Plan" (specifically, a Project).

Contextual Explanation:

A single-use plan is engineered to manage a unique, non-recurring activity or mission that ceases to function as an active blueprint once its specific organizational or national target is fully achieved. The construction of a mega-infrastructure like the Padma Bridge is a distinct historical enterprise with clear start and completion boundaries.

Detailed Analysis:

In management frameworks, individual sub-units of a massive program that possess independent engineering layouts, separate resource allocations, and specific time-bound milestones are classified as projects. Since the construction of this particular bridge represents a specialized, non-repetitive engineering task aimed at achieving a distinct regional connectivity milestone, it constitutes a standard project-level single-use plan.

questions.label: d
questions.text: In light of which characteristic of a good plan was it possible to bring qualitative changes to the plan of the Padma Bridge? Analyze.
questions.answer:
Identification of the Characteristic:

The qualitative modifications made to the Padma Bridge construction plan were possible due to the essential characteristic of a good plan known as "Flexibility" (or adaptability).

Point 1: Understanding Flexibility in Project Blueprints:

Flexibility in planning refers to the structural elasticity or built-in capability of an operational plan to be revised, adapted, or altered smoothly mid-way through execution without compromising the ultimate goal whenever unexpected field realties or environmental shocks occur.

Point 2: Analysis of the Stem Scenario:

According to the stem, during the execution phase, engineers encountered severe real-world physical disruptions caused by the unpredictable river morphology and strong underwater currents of the Padma River. Because the initial engineering and management blueprint possessed the administrative trait of flexibility, authorities were able to recalibrate structural parameters and adapt the architectural design smoothly to survive these natural challenges without abandoning the construction.

Conclusion:

In conclusion, the analysis confirms that rigid plans inevitably fracture when faced with unpredictable environmental forces. Incorporating flexibility within the Padma Bridge planning framework proved highly justified, as it allowed the implementation teams to overcome geological crises dynamically, leading to the successful completion and historic inauguration of the mega-project.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Imon, Chairman of Anha Group of Industries, was involved in the import-export business for a long time. Due to the ongoing Ukraine-Russia war situation, a recession has appeared in his business. Therefore, Mr. Imon has decided that he will now operate his business within the country. He called an emergency meeting with the board of directors and formulated a three-year plan.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a decision?
questions.answer: A decision is the selection of a specific course of action from among two or more alternatives to solve a problem or achieve a goal.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Explain the factors to be considered in decision-making.
questions.answer:
Paragraph 1: Definition

Decision-making is the process of selecting the best course of action from various available alternatives. To ensure that the selected decision is realistic and effective, several internal and external factors must be systematically analyzed before making the final choice.

Paragraph 2: Key Factors

The key factors to be considered include:

* Nature of the Problem: Understanding the depth, urgency, and scope of the problem.

* Availability of Information: Gathering accurate, reliable, and relevant data.

* Feasibility and Cost: Assessing financial capability and analyzing the cost-benefit ratio.

* Time Constraints: Ensuring the decision can be implemented within the required timeframe.

* Acceptability and Risk: Evaluating the potential risks and whether the stakeholders/employees will accept the decision.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which characteristic of planning is reflected in Mr. Imon's decision to do business within the country? Explain.
questions.answer:
Paragraph 1: Identification of Characteristic

The characteristic of planning reflected in Mr. Imon's decision to operate his business within the country is "Flexibility" (or Adaptability). Planning must be dynamic so that it can be altered or adjusted in response to changes in the external environment.

Paragraph 2: Theoretical Explanation

Flexibility is one of the essential qualities of a good plan. Since the future is uncertain and business environments constantly change due to political, economic, or international events, a rigid plan can lead to failure. A flexible plan allows management to adjust its strategies or shift directions smoothly when unexpected obstacles arise, ensuring the survival and continuity of the organization.

Paragraph 3: Contextual Application

In the given stem, Mr. Imon was successfully running an import-export business. However, the unexpected Ukraine-Russia war created an international recession that adversely affected his business. Instead of sticking rigidly to his old international trade plan, Mr. Imon adapted to the changing global environment and decided to shift his business operations domestically. This timely shift clearly highlights the flexibility characteristic of planning, allowing his business to cope with external environmental threats.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the logical justification of the type of plan formulated by Mr. Imon.
questions.answer:
Paragraph 1: Identification of Plan Type

Mr. Imon has formulated a "Medium-Term Plan" (specifically, a three-year plan). Based on duration, a plan that is formulated for a period of more than one year but up to five years is classified as a medium-term plan.

Paragraph 2: Theoretical Significance

A medium-term plan serves as a vital bridge between short-term operational tactics and long-term strategic visions. It allows an enterprise enough time to stabilize a new strategy, mobilize necessary local infrastructure, and recover from immediate losses without locking the company into an unalterable long-term commitment during highly volatile global economic conditions.

Paragraph 3: Analysis of the Stem

The stem shows that Mr. Imon's international business collapsed due to the war, forcing him to completely restructure his business model toward the domestic market. Shifting from global trade to domestic trade cannot be achieved overnight through a short-term plan (less than a year), nor is it wise to make a long-term plan (above 5 years) when global political situations are unstable. By calling an emergency meeting and adopting a three-year plan, he allowed his business a reasonable timeframe to establish local supply chains, understand domestic demand, and rebuild revenue streams.

Paragraph 4: Conclusion/Justification

Therefore, the formulation of a medium-term plan by Mr. Imon is highly logical and justified. It provides the perfect balance of stability and time required to execute his new domestic business strategy while keeping the organization safe from the immediate uncertainties of international conflicts.

fullCQData.stem: XYZ Private Ltd. is one of the leading particle board and processed wood manufacturing companies. After meeting the domestic demand, they regularly export products to India, Singapore, and Malaysia. In 2021, the company's CEO, Mr. Farhad, decided to increase production by 20% with the intention of exporting products to various countries in Europe. Due to the high demand for 'Cosmic Door' and 'Cosmic Window' abroad, he integrated new technology into the factory and made extensive changes to the production plan.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is SWOT Analysis?
questions.answer: SWOT analysis is a strategic planning tool used to evaluate the Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats of an organization or project.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is Vision 2041 considered a long-term plan?
questions.answer:
Paragraph 1: Definition

A long-term plan is a strategic framework designed for a long duration, typically extending beyond five years. It outlines the core mission, overarching goals, and vision of an organization or a nation.

Paragraph 2: Justification

Vision 2041 is a comprehensive national development plan designed to transform Bangladesh into a high-income, developed nation by the year 2041. Achieving such grand economic, structural, and social milestones requires monumental infrastructural overhauls, massive resource mobilization, and policy continuity over decades. Because its scope spans over multiple decades and its execution takes far longer than the standard 5-year macro-planning limit, Vision 2041 is inherently classified as a long-term plan.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which type of plan based on nature did the CEO make in the first phase of the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Paragraph 1: Identification of Plan Type

In the first phase of the stem, the plan made by the CEO according to its nature is a "Single-Use Plan" (specifically, a Project or specific target-driven plan aimed at a 20% production increase for a specific market extension by 2021).

Paragraph 2: Theoretical Explanation

Based on nature or use, plans are primarily classified into standing plans and single-use plans.
