fullCQData.stem: Sushama Cosmetics' is a cosmetics manufacturing enterprise. Every year its growth is positive. This year, the management set a target to increase profits by 30%. The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) set a target to increase sales by 40% compared to the previous year. Similarly, he asked the production, procurement, accounts, and advertising departments to set their respective targets. Along with this, he requested them to contribute to achieving the target by completing the work through internal communication among themselves.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is coordination?
questions.answer: Coordination is the administrative process of integrating, balancing, and synchronizing the efforts, timing, and resources of different individuals and departments within an organization to achieve its common goals.

questions.label: b
questions.text: How does the principle of 'team spirit' (or group understanding) assist in coordination? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: The principle of team spirit or group understanding assists in coordination by fostering mutual trust, cooperation, and unity among employees, transforming individual efforts into a collective force.

Realization: An organization consists of diverse individuals with different personalities and roles. When team spirit is established, employees look past their personal differences and understand how their work affects others. This mutual understanding eliminates internal conflict, encourages voluntary communication, and builds a supportive work culture. Consequently, instead of working in isolated silos, employees collaborate smoothly, which simplifies horizontal alignment and makes overall coordination naturally successful.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which principle of coordination has been applied in setting the targets of 'Sushama Cosmetics' in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge (Para 1): The principle of coordination applied in setting the targets of 'Sushama Cosmetics' is the "Principle of Unity of Goal" (or Principle of Common Goal alignment).

Contextual Connection (Para 2): In the stem, the central management of 'Sushama Cosmetics' sets a primary overarching goal: increasing profits by 30%. To achieve this, the Chief Executive Officer sets a secondary goal of increasing sales by 40%. Following this master plan, the individual functional departments—production, procurement, accounts, and advertising—are asked to establish their own departmental objectives in such a manner that they directly support the broader corporate goals.

Explanation & Conclusion (Para 3): The principle of unity of goal dictates that all individual and departmental efforts within an organization must move in the same direction toward a singular main objective. If the production or procurement departments set their goals independently without aligning with the 40% sales increase target, the organization would suffer from a mismatch between supply and demand. By instructing all departments to design their goals around the central theme and maintain internal communication, 'Sushama Cosmetics' ensures that every sub-goal serves as a building block for the main corporate target, successfully demonstrating the principle of goal unity.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "The practice of a specific principle of coordination has made the coordination of 'Sushama Cosmetics' effective" — Analyze.
questions.answer:
Knowledge (Para 1): The specific principle of coordination that has made the coordination of 'Sushama Cosmetics' effective is the "Principle of Mutual Communication" (coupled with the Principle of Horizontal Integration).

Contextual Analysis (Para 2): In the stem, after the primary and departmental goals are defined, the Chief Executive Officer explicitly requests all departments (production, procurement, accounts, and advertising) to complete their operations through "internal communication among themselves" (আন্তঃযোগাযোগ). This deliberate emphasis on inter-departmental dialogue ensures that information flows smoothly across all parallel functional lines.

Justification & Logical Arguments (Para 3): The practice of this specific communication principle enhances effectiveness through the following dimensions:

• Synchronization of Supply and Demand: Through mutual communication, the procurement department knows exactly how much raw material to buy based on the production department's targets, which are directly informed by the sales department's 40% increase goal.

• Elimination of Informational Silos: When departments communicate horizontally, delays and misunderstandings are minimized, preventing the common bottleneck where accounts or advertising operate without knowing production limits.

• Faster Problem Solving: Real-time internal communication allows the advertising and sales departments to quickly adjust their campaigns if the production department faces temporary shortages, keeping the 30% profit goal intact.

• Fostering Team Spirit: Continuous lateral communication converts independent departmental tasks into a collective organizational effort, bridging the functional gaps between distinct departments.

Conclusion (Para 4): In conclusion, while setting an aligned goal provides the destination, it is the active practice of the principle of mutual communication that provides the vehicle to get there. Without this cross-departmental interaction, individual targets would remain isolated numbers on paper. Therefore, the CEO's directive to maintain robust internal communication is precisely what transforms separate departmental goals into a cohesive, highly effective organizational engine for Sushama Cosmetics.

fullCQData.stem: Y Ceramics sets its production and sales targets at the very beginning of the year. In 2020, their target was 1,000,000 units. In June, it was observed that although the production department had manufactured 500,000 units of products, the sales department managed to sell only 350,000 units. Upon investigation, it was revealed that the employees of the sales department were not aware of the target. Furthermore, there was a shortage of experienced personnel in the sales department. The top management of Y Ceramics is deeply concerned about this matter.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is coordination?
questions.answer: Coordination is the administrative process of integrating, balancing, and synchronizing the efforts, timing, and resources of different individuals and departments within an organization to achieve its common goals.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by decision-making? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Decision-making is the managerial process of identifying, evaluating, and selecting the best alternative course of action from among two or more available choices to solve a specific problem or achieve a goal.

Realization: In management, decision-making is a continuous and core administrative responsibility. Every planning, organizing, or controlling activity requires choices. A sound decision requires analytical thinking, assessing the future risks of each option, and gathering accurate market data. If a manager fails to make a timely and logical decision, corporate resources will be wasted, and the firm will lose its competitive edge in the market.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which prerequisite of effective coordination was violated in the organization described in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge (Para 1): In Y Ceramics, the core prerequisites of effective coordination that have been severely violated are "Clear Communication of Objectives" (লক্ষ্যের স্পষ্টতা ও জ্ঞাপন) and "Sound Staffing Policy (Adequate and Skilled Personnel)" (উপযুক্ত জনবল কাঠামো).

Contextual Connection (Para 2): The stem shows a major mid-year mismatch: the production floor manufactured 500,000 units, but the sales team moved only 350,000 units. The internal investigation revealed that the frontline sales employees were operating blindly, completely unaware of the 1,000,000-unit master target. Additionally, the sales wing was crippled by a severe shortage of experienced professionals capable of driving distribution.

Explanation & Conclusion (Para 3): An absolute prerequisite for coordination is that goals must not just be written down by top management; they must be actively communicated to the people executing them. If employees do not know the target, they cannot align their daily efforts with other departments. Furthermore, coordination requires capable human capital. Expecting an inexperienced and uninformed sales team to smoothly clear the inventory generated by an efficient production floor is structurally impossible. Therefore, keeping the staff in the dark and ignoring workforce capability directly violates the essential prerequisites of coordination.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the problems of the organization described in the stem in light of the principles of coordination.
questions.answer:
Knowledge (Para 1): From the perspective of coordination principles, the operational crisis at Y Ceramics is a direct result of violating the "Principle of Unity of Goal" (উদ্দেশ্যের ঐক্য নীতি) and the "Principle of Balanced Coordination" (ভারসাম্যের নীতি).

Contextual Analysis (Para 2): The breakdown at Y Ceramics highlights a massive disconnect between parallel departments. The production department kept manufacturing goods based on the corporate target, while the sales department fell far behind because it lacked both the information and the experienced workforce required to match the production output. This created an immediate inventory backup of 150,000 unsold units, putting a strain on the firm's profitability.

Justification & Logical Arguments (Para 3): The operational failures can be analyzed through the following core principles of coordination:

• Violation of Unity of Goal: This principle dictates that all individuals and departments must pull in the same direction toward a shared target. Because the sales team was unaware of the master goal, their field actions were completely disconnected from the corporate strategy, fracturing institutional unity.

• Violation of Balanced Coordination: A sound coordination framework requires horizontal balance, meaning the capacity and speed of the sales department must match the capacity and speed of the production department. Overproduction paired with underselling shatters this equilibrium, leading to dead stock and capital blockage.

• Lack of Timing and Synchronization: Without experienced personnel in sales to predict market demand and synchronize distribution channels with factory schedules, the timing of the supply chain broke down entirely.

Conclusion (Para 4): In conclusion, the anxiety of Y Ceramics' top management is entirely justified, as their current operational structure violates the most basic principles of organizational harmony. Setting high-level goals is meaningless if the departments operate as isolated islands. To fix this, the authorities must immediately establish regular horizontal communication between production and sales, recruit experienced sales professionals to eliminate the skill deficit, and ensure that every future target is clearly communicated down to the grassroots level.

fullCQData.stem: The way Mr. Shakil has developed his organizational structure, there is no balance between the workload and the number of employees across different departments. For this reason, some departments are heavily overburdened with work, while other departments have no work at all. As a result, there is no end to the problems in the workplace. Therefore, Mr. Shakil consulted with a friend and restructured the workforce. He encouraged all departmental heads to voluntarily align themselves with other departments and strengthen collective group efforts. He intervenes in this matter only when it is absolutely necessary.
fullCQData.meta: Dr. Mahbubur Rahman Molla College, Dhaka · 2026

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is coordination?
questions.answer: Coordination is the administrative process of integrating, balancing, and synchronizing the efforts, timing, and resources of different individuals and departments within an organization to achieve its common goals.

questions.label: b
questions.text: How does the principle of 'team spirit' (or group understanding) assist in coordination? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: The principle of team spirit or group understanding assists in coordination by fostering mutual trust, cooperation, and unity among employees, transforming individual efforts into a collective force.

Realization: An organization consists of diverse individuals with different personalities and roles. When team spirit is established, employees look past their personal differences and understand how their work affects others. This mutual understanding eliminates internal conflict, encourages voluntary communication, and builds a supportive work culture. Consequently, instead of working in isolated silos, employees collaborate smoothly, which simplifies horizontal alignment and makes overall coordination naturally successful.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which principle of coordination did Mr. Shakil violate that caused him to face problems? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge (Para 1): Mr. Shakil faced severe problems because he initially violated the fundamental "Principle of Balanced Coordination" (ভারসাম্যের নীতি) or the principle of proper resource distribution.

Contextual Connection (Para 2): The stem states that in Mr. Shakil's original organizational design, there was a complete mismatch between the workload and human resources across parallel units. Some departments were completely overwhelmed and struggling under intense work pressure, while neighboring departments had virtually no tasks and left their employees idle, creating endless operational issues and friction.

Explanation & Conclusion (Para 3): The principle of balanced coordination dictates that an administrator must distribute tasks, operational schedules, and human capital equitably across all departments so that every wing moves forward at an equal, harmonious pace. If one division is severely overloaded while another is underloaded, horizontal synchronization collapses, breeding resentment and structural inefficiency. By failing to allocate staff sizes according to actual department workloads, Mr. Shakil destroyed this essential equilibrium, causing the workplace chaos.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the effectiveness of the prerequisite for effective coordination that Mr. Shakil established subsequently.
questions.answer:
Knowledge (Para 1): The prerequisites for effective coordination that Mr. Shakil established subsequently are "Fostering Voluntary Coordination" (স্বতঃস্ফূর্ত সমন্বয়) and practicing the "Principle of Management by Exception" (ব্যতিক্রম নীতি).

Contextual Analysis (Para 2): After restructuring his workforce to fix the staffing imbalance, Mr. Shakil changed his leadership style. Instead of forcing top-down administrative orders, he motivated his departmental heads to "voluntarily align themselves through collective group efforts" and explicitly decided to intervene "only when it is absolutely necessary."

Justification & Logical Arguments (Para 3): The high effectiveness of establishing these self-driven, decentralized prerequisites is proven by the following angles:

• Elimination of Bureaucratic Delays: When department heads collaborate voluntarily and laterally in real-time, they do not have to wait for long, formal approvals from top management to solve daily operational conflicts.

• Reduction of Administrative Burden: By intervening only in exceptional, critical situations (Management by Exception), Mr. Shakil frees up his own valuable time to focus on long-term corporate strategies rather than getting bogged down in micro-managing routine tasks.

• Enhanced Accountability and Ownership: Allowing managers to coordinate on their own initiative builds a deep sense of psychological ownership over their roles, making them highly motivated to ensure their departments succeed.

• Organic Team Synergy: True corporate harmony cannot be forced through penalties. Encouraging spontaneous horizontal links transforms isolated units into a single cohesive engine pulling toward the master goal.

Conclusion (Para 4): In conclusion, while structural reallocation fixes the mechanical design of an enterprise, it is the cultivation of a voluntary and autonomous environment that keeps it running dynamically. Mr. Shakil's new approach shifts the organization from forced compliance to an efficient, self-correcting system. Therefore, this subsequent strategy is highly effective and completely essential for establishing long-term harmony, maximizing resource utility, and ensuring seamless institutional success.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Dulal Miah is the Chief Executive Officer of Madhumati Textile Mills. A change in one department is interrelated with other departments. Without considering this, the Human Resource department made arbitrary transfers. This caused an imbalance in the number of employees across various departments. As a result, anger and resentment arose among both skilled and unskilled workers. Within a few months, production decreased.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a goal?
questions.answer: A goal is the specific, measurable, and time-bound end result or target toward which all organizational activities and planning are directed.

questions.label: b
questions.text: How does the principle of 'team spirit' (or group understanding) assist in coordination? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: The principle of team spirit or group understanding assists in coordination by fostering mutual trust, cooperation, and unity among employees, transforming individual efforts into a collective force.

Realization: An organization consists of diverse individuals with different personalities and roles. When team spirit is established, employees look past their personal differences and understand how their work affects others. This mutual understanding eliminates internal conflict, encourages voluntary communication, and builds a supportive work culture. Consequently, instead of working in isolated silos, employees collaborate smoothly, which simplifies horizontal alignment and makes overall coordination naturally successful.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which principle of coordination was not followed regarding the transfers at Madhumati Textile Mills? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge (Para 1): Regarding the transfers at Madhumati Textile Mills, the fundamental "Principle of Balanced Coordination" (ভারসাম্যের নীতি) or the "Principle of Recognizing Mutual Interdependence" was completely ignored.

Contextual Connection (Para 2): The stem indicates that the Human Resource department executed employee transfers arbitrarily without evaluating how shifting staff would ripple through other wings. This blind decision created a structural mismatch—leaving some departments short-handed and others overstaffed—which directly triggered widespread resentment among both skilled and unskilled factory hands and crippled the mill's collective workflow.

Explanation & Conclusion (Para 3): The principle of balanced coordination dictates that all departments in an enterprise form an interconnected ecosystem where an alteration in one sector creates a domino effect across parallel units. Management must maintain an equal balance between worker counts and task volumes across all departments. By moving employees without consulting parallel operations or checking department-to-work ratios, the HR division shattered this delicate internal equilibrium. Therefore, ignoring these vital interdependencies represents a direct violation of balanced coordination.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "The lack of a proper coordination system is the main barrier that caused the production decrease at Madhumati Textiles." Analyze in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge (Para 1): The operational downfall at Madhumati Textile Mills serves as clear evidence that the lack of a proper integrated coordination framework acts as the ultimate barrier to production by causing operational imbalances, worker demoralization, and functional stagnation.

Contextual Analysis (Para 2): In Madhumati Textiles, the isolated action of the HR wing severely damaged the factory's production output. By executing an arbitrary transfer in an informational vacuum, they created a mismatch between skilled and unskilled workers across the factory floor, causing widespread resentment and a noticeable drop in manufacturing output within just a few months.

Justification & Logical Arguments (Para 3): The lack of a proper integrated system paralyzed the production process due to the following reasons:

• Disruption of the Production Chain: Manufacturing relies on a precise sequence. When the HR department created an imbalance in worker numbers, bottlenecks formed where raw materials could not be processed at a uniform speed.

• Demoralization of the Workforce: Effective coordination requires keeping employee morale high. The arbitrary transfers created intense anger among both skilled and unskilled workers, destroying their motivation to hit production targets.

• Breakdown of Horizontal Intercommunication: A proper integrated system ensures that before the HR department alters staffing, it must coordinate with the production manager. The absence of this cross-departmental dialogue led to a highly impractical decision.

• Loss of Operational Continuity: Production requires steady, predictable human resource allocation. Swapping staff haphazardly breaks the continuity of routine work, resulting in high error rates and reduced output.

Conclusion (Para 4): In conclusion, the crisis at Madhumati Textile Mills clearly shows that no department can operate as an isolated island. The sharp decline in production was a direct consequence of a broken coordination framework that failed to integrate HR actions with factory-floor realities. To restore efficiency, the CEO must implement a unified, inter-departmental communication system that ensures all future personnel changes are balanced with the operational needs of the production floor.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Halim is the Managing Director of ABC Garments Company. His company has received an order to manufacture 100,000 pieces of pants. The task must be completed within 1 month. He divided the workers into different teams and assigned them responsibilities. It was observed that the workers of one team often gossiped during their working hours. On the other hand, the workers of another team remained busy with work even during lunchtime but still could not finish their entire assigned work within the specified time. At the end of the month, it was found that the target of manufacturing 100,000 pieces of pants was not achieved.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is coordination?
questions.answer: Coordination is the administrative process of integrating, balancing, and synchronizing the efforts, timing, and resources of different individuals and departments within an organization to achieve its common goals.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Explain the importance of coordination in management.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Coordination is highly important in management because it acts as the ultimate balancing force that unifies isolated efforts, prevents operational overlaps, and ensures a seamless workflow across all departments.

Realization: An organization consists of multiple departments like procurement, production, and sales. Without coordination, these divisions operate as isolated silos, creating internal friction and waste. Coordination synchronizes their operational timing and resource allocation. It eliminates conflict, boosts overall efficiency, maximizes resource utilization, and binds separate tasks into a single corporate machine pulling toward the macro organizational goal.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which principle of coordination was misused or poorly applied in Mr. Halim's organization in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge (Para 1): In Mr. Halim's organization, the "Principle of Balanced Coordination" (ভারসাম্যের নীতি) was severely misused and poorly applied during the division of work.

Contextual Connection (Para 2): The stem shows a massive imbalance in workload distribution among teams. One team is severely underworked, giving its members enough idle time to sit and gossip during operational hours. Meanwhile, an adjacent team is heavily overburdened—sacrificing their lunch breaks to work continuously—yet failing to hit deadlines due to an unrealistic volume of assigned tasks.

Explanation & Conclusion (Para 3): The principle of balanced coordination dictates that a manager must distribute workloads, human capital, and operational capacities equitably across all executing units. If one team is underloaded while another is crushed by overwork, the internal equilibrium collapses. By failing to evaluate team capacities and dividing the 100,000-unit order disproportionately, Mr. Halim created bottleneck dynamics and workplace inefficiency, showcasing a direct failure to apply balanced coordination.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Do you think that the company failed to reach its goal due to not properly following a prerequisite of effective coordination? Analyze your opinion in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge (Para 1): Yes, I completely agree. The company failed to reach its target because management did not follow the vital coordination prerequisite of a "Sound Staffing Policy and Work-Capacity Integration" (উপযুক্ত জনবল ও কার্যক্ষমতার সমন্বয়) along with "Effective Supervision."

Contextual Analysis (Para 2): In ABC Garments, the failure to manufacture 100,000 pieces of pants was a direct result of ignoring work capacities. The over-allocated team fell behind because a human workforce can only handle a specific workload per hour, regardless of whether they skip lunch. Concurrently, the under-allocated team wasted valuable production hours gossiping due to a lack of standard task tracking and active monitoring.

Justification & Logical Arguments (Para 3): Failing to establish these essential prerequisites of effective coordination derailed the company's objective due to the following reasons:

• Lack of Time-Motion Integration: A prerequisite for effective coordination is matching a task's size with human time limits. Management did not calculate how many pieces a single team could realistically produce in a month, resulting in one team drowning in impossible targets.

• Failure of Supervised Control: Effective coordination requires active, direct supervision. Allowing workers to spend paid hours gossiping proves that there was no real-time supervisory loop to correct idleness and reallocate work.

• Absence of Mid-Course Correction: In a strict 1-month contract, a sound coordination system requires checking progress weekly. If management had monitored the teams, they could have transferred part of the overloaded team's work to the idle team before the month ended.

Conclusion (Para 4): In conclusion, setting a high-volume target means nothing without proper structural balancing and supervision on the factory floor. ABC Garments lost its order because its teams operated out of step—one wasting time and the other burning out. To prevent such failures in the future, Mr. Halim must implement standardized workload assessments and set up active floor supervisors to ensure that human labor is balanced dynamically with production deadlines.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Tareq is the owner of a production-oriented enterprise named 'Blue Ocean'. Regarding various organizational matters, he takes joint decisions in consultation with the departmental heads of production, finance, procurement, and sales. If any problem arises during the execution of work, he takes corrective measures and brings necessary changes to the work methods.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is PERT?
questions.answer: PERT (Program Evaluation and Review Technique) is a statistical, network-based project management tool designed to analyze and represent the sequential tasks involved in completing a given project, especially for estimating the time required for each activity.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is coordination essential to achieve organizational goals? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge: Coordination is essential to achieve organizational goals because it unifies and synchronizes the scattered efforts of various independent individuals and departments into a single collective force.

Realization: An organization is made up of diverse functional units such as finance, production, and marketing. If these units operate in isolation without synchronization, it leads to duplication of work, waste of valuable resources, and internal conflicts. Coordination bridges these departmental gaps, ensures a smooth workflow, and guides all separate sub-plans and actions toward the main corporate destination, making goal achievement possible.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which prerequisite of effective coordination has been fulfilled by the enterprise 'Blue Ocean' in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Knowledge (Para 1): The enterprise 'Blue Ocean' has successfully fulfilled the critical prerequisite of effective coordination known as "Participative Management and Consultative Planning" (অংশগ্রহণমূলক ব্যবস্থাপনা বা যৌথ পরামর্শ পদ্ধতি).

Contextual Connection (Para 2): In the stem, Mr. Tareq does not rule his enterprise with a rigid, autocratic style. Instead, before finalizing any major organizational strategies or operational plans, he sits down with the heads of the production, finance, procurement, and sales departments to take dynamic joint decisions based on mutual consultation.

Explanation & Conclusion (Para 3): A foundational prerequisite for smooth coordination is that the people responsible for executing a plan must have a say in its creation. Involving parallel functional heads in the decision-making process ensures that everyone feels valued, which eliminates internal resistance, clears up communication bottlenecks, and ensures that each department is fully aligned with the central objective right from the start. By setting up this collaborative routine, 'Blue Ocean' has established an ideal framework for participative coordination.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the logical justification of the principle of coordination followed by Mr. Tareq in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Knowledge (Para 1): Mr. Tareq has brilliantly followed the "Principle of Flexibility" (নমনীয়তার নীতি) and the "Principle of Continuous Feedback and Control" (অবিরামতা ও সংশোধনমূলক নীতি), which are highly justified for survival in a dynamic market environment.

Contextual Analysis (Para 2): The stem states that Mr. Tareq's leadership extends beyond just making joint plans. When his teams face real-world operational problems during execution, he does not rigidly force them to stick to outdated mandates. Instead, he steps in with "corrective measures" and actively introduces "necessary changes to the work methods" to adapt to the situation.

Justification & Logical Arguments (Para 3): The high logical justification of following this flexible principle can be analyzed through these critical dimensions:

• Resilience Against Market Uncertainty: Production-oriented businesses face sudden external shifts like material shortages or changing demand. A flexible work method allows the production and sales lines to adjust dynamically without grinding operations to a halt.

• Prompt Problem Mitigation: By enforcing corrective controls immediately when an issue pops up, Mr. Tareq prevents minor departmental errors from snowballing into a major company-wide crisis.

• Minimizing Operational Friction: Rigidity in management often leads to employee frustration. Knowing that work methods can be adapted to solve practical difficulties keeps worker morale high and maintains system balance.

• Continuous Optimization: Changing work methods based on real-world challenges allows the organization to learn from its mistakes, leading to more practical workflows over time.

Conclusion (Para 4): In conclusion, Mr. Tareq’s approach is highly justified and serves as a model for modern management. While his consultative style builds structural unity at the top, his commitment to flexibility ensures that his organization remains agile and resilient on the ground. By combining joint decision-making with rapid, real-time corrections, he creates a perfectly coordinated corporate engine capable of steering 'Blue Ocean' toward long-term financial and operational success.

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: Jony, having recently completed his Master's degree, joined Karnafuli Garments as a supervisor in the production department. He compares each manufactured garment with a pre-established standard and separates the defective items. Q. Which management function is Jony involved in?
mcqData.explanation: Jony evaluates actual performance against a pre-set standard to spot defects. This process of identifying deviations and taking corrective measures defines Controlling (নিয়ন্ত্রণ).

mcqData.text: How does a manager evaluate and measure the actual progress of an organization's work operations?
options: By comparing actual performance with standards
mcqData.explanation: Progress is measured by comparing actual performance with established standards (আদর্শমানের সাথে তুলনা করে). This calculation helps identify any gaps or variations.

mcqData.text:
In any business enterprise, the function of controlling is considered a/an—

(i) Continuous process
(ii) Managerial process
(iii) Social process

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Controlling is a continuous (i) and core managerial process (ii) used to measure targets. It is not an administrative social process (iii) like community development. Thus, statements i and ii are correct.

mcqData.text: Which of the following management functions serves as the fundamental basis or foundation for controlling?
mcqData.explanation: Planning (পরিকল্পনা) provides the targets and standards. Without a plan, there are no benchmarks available to measure performance against, making controlling impossible.

mcqData.text: Through which core management function are the actual operating results and performance of an organization verified?
mcqData.explanation: Controlling (নিয়ন্ত্রণ) verifies operational results by checking performance against set expectations, highlighting deviations, and correcting issues.

mcqData.text: Which of the following steps represents the final task or concluding phase within the management control process?
mcqData.explanation: The control process ends by taking corrective measures (সংশোধনমূলক ব্যবস্থা গ্রহণ). This step fixes variations and aligns operations back with the initial plan.

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: An enterprise sets a target weekly production volume of 50,000 units. However, at the end of the week, the actual production volume is 45,000 units. The management takes necessary measures to ensure this variance does not happen again. Q. What is the pre-set production volume of 50,000 units called in management terms?
mcqData.explanation: A pre-set performance target used as a benchmark to measure actual outcomes is defined as a standard yardstick or standard (আদর্শমান).

mcqData.text:
Read the stem and answer the question: An enterprise sets a target weekly production volume of 50,000 units. However, at the end of the week, the actual production volume is 45,000 units. The management takes necessary measures to ensure this variance does not happen again. Q. To hit the company's targets, what corrective actions should management take?

(i) Setting more accurate targets
(ii) Organizing training programs for employees
(iii) Ensuring proper and close supervision

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: To fix a production drop from 50,000 to 45,000 units, management should train staff (ii) and tighten supervision (iii). Resetting targets lower (i) alters the goal rather than correcting the operational issue. Thus, statements ii and iii are correct.

mcqData.text: Which of the following choices represents the terminal, period-ending function within the standard management cycle?
mcqData.explanation: Controlling is a period-ending function (কালান্তিক কাজ). It occurs at the end of an operational cycle to review results before a new planning phase starts.

mcqData.text: For which specific management function is establishing a performance standard an essential first step?
mcqData.explanation: Controlling (নিয়ন্ত্রণ) requires standard performance yardsticks to be set first, as it cannot evaluate operations without predefined baselines.

mcqData.text:
In corporate administration, the function of controlling serves as—

(i) The foundation for the next management plan
(ii) The base foundation for the current plan
(iii) A period-ending or terminal management task

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Controlling is a period-ending task (iii) that generates performance data. This feedback acts as the foundation for the next plan (i), not the current plan's base. Thus, statements i and iii are correct.

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: The target weekly production volume for MGI Enterprise is 10,000 units. At the end of the week, actual production reaches 8,500 units. As a result, the firm fails to deliver its customer orders on schedule. Q. What is the exact volume of variance or deviation in this enterprise?
mcqData.explanation: Variance is calculated by subtracting actual performance from the standard target: $10,000\text{ units} - 8,500\text{ units} = 1,500\text{ units}$ (১500 একক).

mcqData.text:
Read the stem and answer the question: The target weekly production volume for MGI Enterprise is 10,000 units. At the end of the week, actual production reaches 8,500 units. As a result, the firm fails to deliver its customer orders on schedule. Q. To help this enterprise reach its target production capacity, what steps should management implement?

(i) Setting more accurate targets
(ii) Organizing training systems for employees
(iii) Strengthening and reinforcing supervision systems

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: To close the 1,500-unit production gap, management should focus on employee training (ii) and stronger supervision (iii). Adjusting the target numbers (i) avoids the problem instead of correcting it. Thus, statements ii and iii are correct.

mcqData.text: According to standard business management textbooks and academic curriculum, how many sequential steps are there in the control process?
mcqData.explanation: The traditional management control process consists of exactly 5 sequential steps (৫ টি): setting standards, measuring performance, comparing performance, analyzing variances, and taking corrective steps.

mcqData.text: What serves as the definitive structural foundation or basis for the execution of the control function?
mcqData.explanation: Planning (পরিকল্পনা) serves as the foundation for controlling. It establishes the goals that determine what the performance benchmarks will be.

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: Mr. Fazlar Rahman inspects the ongoing construction work of his new house, spots several structural defects, and immediately arranges to have them fixed. Q. Mr. Fazlar Rahman's actions are directly related to which core management function?
mcqData.explanation: Monitoring ongoing work, identifying structural defects, and implementing fixes is an application of Controlling (নিয়ন্ত্রণ).

mcqData.text: Controlling is defined as a period-ending or terminal management task. Within management theory, this statement describes what aspect of control?
mcqData.explanation: Being a period-ending or terminal task (কালান্তিক কাজ) is a core characteristic (বৈশিষ্ট্য) of control, as it takes place at the end of an operating cycle.

mcqData.text: With which of the following core managerial functions is establishing a performance standard or benchmark directly connected?
mcqData.explanation: Setting standard targets is the first step of Controlling (নিয়ন্ত্রণ), creating the benchmarks used to review performance later.

mcqData.text: Which of the following options serves as the core functional basis or foundation for drafting a new management plan?
mcqData.explanation: Controlling (নিয়ন্ত্রণ) evaluates previous outcomes and highlights defects. This feedback provides the foundational data needed to build an improved plan for the next cycle.

mcqData.text: Which of the following tasks falls completely outside the steps of the standard management control process?
mcqData.explanation: Measuring performance (a), calculating variance (b), and correcting issues (c) are steps in the control process. Choosing alternative plans (d) belongs under the function of planning.

mcqData.text: Which of the following management functions serves as the primary basis or structural foundation for controlling?
mcqData.explanation: Planning (পরিকল্পনা) acts as the foundation for controlling by setting the objectives that determine performance targets.

mcqData.text: Which specialized principle should be applied to make a corporate control system highly effective without wasting managerial time?
mcqData.explanation: The Principle of Exception (ব্যতিক্রমের নীতি) states that managers should focus only on significant variances from the plan, while leaving minor deviations to lower-level staff.

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: Mr. Hasan, a production manager at a manufacturing firm, set a target annual production capacity of 2,400,000 units. After 8 months, a performance review shows that only 1,400,000 units have been produced, leaving him deeply concerned. Q. How far along the steps of the control process has Mr. Hasan progressed?
mcqData.explanation: Mr. Hasan has set a target, measured actual production, and calculated the shortfall. This brings him to the step of comparing performance and determining deviations (বিচ্যুতি নিরূপণ).

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: Mr. Hasan, a production manager at a manufacturing firm, set a target annual production capacity of 2,400,000 units. After 8 months, a performance review shows that only 1,400,000 units have been produced, leaving him deeply concerned. Q. Based on the annual target, what should the standard production milestone have been at the 8-month mark?
mcqData.explanation: Monthly standard is $\frac{2,400,000}{12} = 200,000\text{ units}$. For 8 months, the target is $200,000 \times 8 = 1,600,000\text{ units}$ (১৬ লক্ষ).

mcqData.text: Which of the following options represents an inherent, defining characteristic of the control function?
mcqData.explanation: Controlling is an ongoing, continuous process (অবিরাম প্রক্রিয়া). It requires managers to track operations constantly rather than running checks as a single event.

mcqData.text: Which of the following steps falls completely outside the standard management control process?
mcqData.explanation: Setting standards (a), measuring performance (b), and calculating deviations (c) are parts of control. Selecting alternative options (d) belongs under planning.

mcqData.text: Following the standard sequence of management cycle functions, which task comes immediately after completing the control phase?
mcqData.explanation: Controlling completes the management cycle. The data and feedback gathered during this final phase lead directly back into the next round of Planning (পরিকল্পনা).

mcqData.text:
In any business enterprise, the function of controlling is considered a/an—

(i) Continuous process
(ii) Managerial process
(iii) Social process

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Controlling is a continuous (i) and core managerial process (ii) used to measure targets. It is not an administrative social process (iii) like community development. Thus, statements i and ii are correct.

mcqData.text: Through which core management function are the actual operating results and performance of an organization verified?
mcqData.explanation: Controlling (নিয়ন্ত্রণ) verifies operational results by checking performance against set expectations, highlighting deviations, and correcting issues.

mcqData.text: Which of the following management functions serves as the primary basis or structural foundation for controlling?
mcqData.explanation: Planning (পরিকল্পনা) acts as the foundation for controlling by setting the objectives that determine performance targets.

mcqData.text: What is universally recognized as the absolute first step or initial phase in the management control process?
mcqData.explanation: The control process must always begin by establishing standard benchmarks (আদর্শমান নির্ধারণ), which serve as the baseline targets for evaluation.

mcqData.text:
Why is implementing an efficient and robust control system highly critical for a business enterprise?

(i) It corrects mistakes and performance variations
(ii) It reduces operational and commercial risks
(iii) It breaks down group unity and cohesion

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: A strong control system fixes performance errors (i) and reduces business risks (ii). It supports team alignment rather than breaking down unity (iii). Thus, statements i and ii are correct.

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: Mr. Mohaimen, a production manager at a manufacturing firm, set an annual production target of 36,000 metric tons. After 9 months, a performance review shows that only 22,000 metric tons have been produced, leaving him anxious. Q. How far along the steps of the control process has the production manager progressed?
mcqData.explanation: Mr. Mohaimen has set his target, measured actual output, and calculated the shortfall. This brings him to the step of comparing performance and determining deviations (বিচ্যুতি নিরূপণ).

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: Mr. Mohaimen, a production manager at a manufacturing firm, set an annual production target of 36,000 metric tons. After 9 months, a performance review shows that only 22,000 metric tons have been produced, leaving him anxious. Q. Based on the annual target, what should the standard production milestone have been at the 9-month mark?
mcqData.explanation: Monthly target is $\frac{36,000}{12} = 3,000\text{ tons}$. For 9 months, the required standard milestone is $3,000 \times 9 = 27,000\text{ metric tons}$ (২৭,০০০).

mcqData.text: Which of the following choices represents the final function or concluding phase within the standard management cycle?
mcqData.explanation: Controlling (নিয়ন্ত্রণ) is the final function of the management cycle, evaluating performance data before restarting the loop with a new plan.

mcqData.text: Which of the following options represents the third sequential step within the management control process?
mcqData.explanation: The sequence runs: 1. Set standards, 2. Measure performance, 3. Compare actual performance with standards (আদর্শমান ও কার্যফলের তুলনা).

mcqData.text: Through the execution of control systems, the performance and accuracy of which other management function is verified?
mcqData.explanation: Controlling checks the accuracy of Planning (পরিকল্পনা). It measures actual performance against the plan's initial targets to see if the goals were realistic.

mcqData.text: According to standard business management textbooks and academic curriculum, how many sequential steps are there in the control process?
mcqData.explanation: The traditional management control process consists of exactly 5 sequential steps (৫ টি) to evaluate performance and correct issues.

mcqData.text: Which of the following steps represents the final task or concluding phase within the management control process?
mcqData.explanation: The final step in the control process is taking corrective measures (সংশোধনমূলক ব্যবস্থা গ্রহণ করা), which adjusts workflows to get performance back on track.

mcqData.text: Following the standard sequence of management cycle functions, which task comes immediately after completing the control phase?
mcqData.explanation: Once the control phase wraps up, its performance insights and data flow directly into the next round of Planning (পরিকল্পনা) to start the management cycle fresh.

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: Mr. Sadat is a manager. He decides to produce 10,000 units of products per month in his organization. However, at the end of the month, the actual production turns out to be 8,000 units. Q. What is this pre-determination of production volume by Mr. Sadat called?
mcqData.explanation: A pre-determined quantitative target set to measure and compare future actual performance is called a standard yardstick or standard (আদর্শমান) in control theory.

mcqData.text:
Read the stem and answer the question: Mr. Sadat is a manager. He decides to produce 10,000 units of products per month in his organization. However, at the end of the month, the actual production turns out to be 8,000 units. Q. To ensure that future production aligns with the set target, what steps should be taken?

(i) Verifying and re-evaluating the target numbers
(ii) Increasing factory productivity levels
(iii) Creating an appropriate and favorable work environment

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: To bridge the 2,000-unit gap, management can review if the target was realistic (i), boost productivity via training (ii), and optimize the shop floor environment (iii). Thus, all three points are correct.

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: Moyna Company Ltd. sets a target for its workers to produce 500,000 toys per week. However, in 5 days, the workers are only able to produce 300,000 toys. This matter becomes a cause of concern for the authorities. Q. Under which step of the control function does Moyna Company Ltd.'s target setting fall?
mcqData.explanation: Setting a performance target of 500,000 toys to evaluate weekly output acts as establishing a standard yardstick (আদর্শমান) within the control process.

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: Moyna Company Ltd. sets a target for its workers to produce 500,000 toys per week. However, in 5 days, the workers are only able to produce 300,000 toys. This matter becomes a cause of concern for the authorities. Q. Which step of the controlling process in the stem can lead to the modification or correction of future planning?
mcqData.explanation: Correcting deviations (বিচ্যুতি সংশোধন) is the actionable step where managers fix errors. If the gap cannot be bridged, it leads to amending or modifying future plans.

mcqData.text: Which of the following choices is classified as a subsidiary or secondary function of corporate management?
mcqData.explanation: Core management functions include planning, organizing, staffing, directing, motivating, coordinating, and controlling. Decision making (সিদ্ধান্ত গ্রহণ) is a subsidiary or secondary function that supports these core tasks.

mcqData.text:
An efficient and robust control system helps an enterprise by—

(i) Fulfilling organizational objectives and goals
(ii) Enhancing employee morale and confidence
(iii) Setting accurate standard baselines using fair judgment

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Effective control keeps workflows aligned with goals (i), gives clear feedback that improves morale (ii), and refines standards using historic data (iii). Therefore, all options apply.

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: Mr. Joy is the sales manager of an enterprise. He designs a new plan for annual sales. After six months, he observes that only 30% of his sales target has been achieved. Under these circumstances, he takes several administrative actions to reach his desired goal and succeeds. Q. Which management function should Mr. Joy perform to find out why he failed in the first phase?
mcqData.explanation: Checking progress mid-way, discovering a 70% shortfall, and finding out why goals were missed is the exact purpose of the Controlling (নিয়ন্ত্রণ) function.

mcqData.text:
Read the stem and answer the question: Mr. Joy is the sales manager of an enterprise. He designs a new plan for annual sales. After six months, he observes that only 30% of his sales target has been achieved. Under these circumstances, he takes several administrative actions to reach his desired goal and succeeds. Q. To reach his desired sales targets within the remaining timeframe, Mr. Joy can—

(i) Re-establish or adjust the target numbers
(ii) Arrange training programs for the sales staff
(iii) Enforce close and intensive supervision

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: To rescue a lagging project, a manager can adjust goals if they were unrealistic (i), upskill staff through training (ii), or increase floor supervision (iii). Therefore, all three apply.

mcqData.text: Which of the following options represents an inherent, defining characteristic of the control function?
mcqData.explanation: Controlling is an ongoing, continuous process (অবিরাম প্রক্রিয়া). It requires managers to track operations constantly rather than running checks as a single event.

mcqData.text: Which of the following choices is directly and fundamentally related to the steps of the control process?
mcqData.explanation: The control process begins with establishing standard benchmarks (আদর্শ মান প্রতিষ্ঠা). Without these benchmarks, actual results cannot be evaluated.

mcqData.text: Which of the following steps represents an authentic phase within the standard management control process?
mcqData.explanation: The control process starts by establishing standard yardsticks (আদর্শমান নির্ধারণ). Defining objectives and identifying problems are part of planning and diagnostic steps.

mcqData.text: According to standard academic business management textbooks, how many steps are there in the control process?
mcqData.explanation: The traditional management control process consists of exactly 5 sequential steps (৫ টি) to evaluate performance and correct issues.

mcqData.text:
What are the core features and defining characteristics of an effective control system?

(i) It is a period-ending or terminal task
(ii) It is backward-looking or backward-oriented
(iii) It is an ongoing, continuous process
mcqData.explanation: Control is period-ending (i) because it checks final results, backward-looking (ii) because it reviews past work against data, and continuous (iii) as it runs across cycles. Thus, all three points are correct.

mcqData.text:
It is impossible to implement a control system effectively without—

(i) Establishing standard performance benchmarks
(ii) Identifying and determining operational variances
(iii) Taking necessary corrective measures
mcqData.explanation: A control system cannot function without setting targets (i), calculating performance gaps (ii), and taking corrective steps (iii) to fix variances. Thus, all are required.

mcqData.text: Which of the following activities represents the fourth sequential step in the management control process?
mcqData.explanation: The process follows: 1. Set standards, 2. Measure actual performance, 3. Compare performance with standards, 4. Determine and analyze deviations (বিচ্যুতি নির্ণয়).

mcqData.text:
Which of the following options represent core, undeniable characteristics of an institutional control system?

(i) It represents the final step of the management cycle
(ii) It operates as an ongoing, continuous process
(iii) It is executed as a period-ending/terminal task
mcqData.explanation: Control is the final phase of the management cycle (i), is continuous across business cycles (ii), and serves as a period-ending task to review outcomes (iii). All match its core characteristics.

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: A college Principal, while inspecting classrooms, noticed that a teacher was not using any visual aids or teaching materials during the lecture. He instructed the teacher to come to class with proper teaching materials, and the next day, the teacher complied. Q. Which management function did the college Principal execute in this scenario?
mcqData.explanation: Monitoring an ongoing activity, identifying a process variance (no teaching materials), and issuing an order to fix it is an application of Controlling (নিয়ন্ত্রণ).

mcqData.text: Which of the following options represents a core, defining characteristic of management control?
mcqData.explanation: Control depends heavily on foundational plans (ভিত্তি নির্ভরতা). It cannot function without the benchmarks and targets set during the planning phase.

mcqData.text: A worker has a daily target to produce 50 product units. In management control terms, this 50-unit target is called the—
mcqData.explanation: A pre-set target (50 units) used as a baseline to evaluate actual performance is defined as the standard yardstick or standard (আদর্শমান).

mcqData.text: Read the stem and answer the question: Mr. Ziko set a production target of 500 units. However, at the end of the specified timeframe, the actual production reached 425 units. Q. What is the exact quantity of deviation or variance in this scenario?
mcqData.explanation: Deviation is calculated by subtracting actual performance from the standard milestone: $500\text{ units} - 425\text{ units} = 75\text{ units}$ (৭৫ একক).

mcqData.text:
Read the stem and answer the question: Mr. Ziko set a production target of 500 units. However, at the end of the specified timeframe, the actual production reached 425 units. Q. If Mr. Ziko wants to reach his targets in the next production cycle, his required actions should include—

(i) Measuring actual performance results carefully
(ii) Analyzing and evaluating the root causes of the variance
(iii) Implementing necessary corrective measures
mcqData.explanation: Mr. Ziko has already measured his output (425 units). To hit his target next time, he needs to analyze why the 75-unit gap happened (ii) and apply corrective fixes (iii). Thus, statements ii and iii are correct.

mcqData.text: Which of the following choices represents the final step or concluding phase in the management control process?
